predictions: parkinson 's disease ( pd ) is a neuropsychiatric disorder that affects quality of life , cognition , and mood . anxiety is the most prominent and prevalent mood disorder in pd , but little is known about the relationship between anxiety and cognition . given that research suggests that anxiety reduces processing capacity and impairs processing efficiency , especially in the central executive and attentional systems of working memory , we hypothesized that patients with anxiety would show impairments in attentional set - shifting and working memory compared to pd patients without anxiety . we examined the independent effect of anxiety on cognition in a cross - sectional sample of patients with pd and without anxiety in order to determine this relationship . seventeen patients with an anxiety score greater than 6 on the hospital anxiety and depression scale ( hads ) and thirty - three patients with no anxiety were included in this study . anxiety was significantly associated with attention and executive functioning measures ( i.e. , immediate memory , visuospatial construction , language , attention , and delayed memory ) , but not with working memory ( p < 0.05 ) . the association of anxiety with attention was not significant ( p = 0.001 ) , whereas the association with executive functioning ( p > 0.01 ) was significant (

small non - coding rnas ( mirnas ) are a class of multifunctional small rnas that regulate the stability or translational efficiency of targeted messenger rna . they regulate a broad range of biological processes including timing of development , cell cycle progression , stem cell self - renewal , differentiation , cancer initiation , cancer cell proliferation , metastasis and apoptosis . emerging evidence demonstrates the involvement of mirna in mammary gland tumorigenesis , functioning either as tumor suppressors or oncogenes . although the current treatment of radiation therapy , chemotherapy and hormone therapy slow breast cancer growth , prolong survival and improve the quality of patients life , metastatic breast cancer still remains incurable due to our limited understanding of the molecular mechanisms through which tumorigenization and metastasis occur . mirnas regulate gene expression and play important roles in the onset and progression of breast cancer . they may represent a novel cancer therapy .

ohss is a serious complication of ovulation induction , occurring in 1 - 10% of in vitro fertilization patients . this iatrogenic condition has a spectrum of clinical and laboratory manifestations ranging from mild to severe , even life - threatening conditions . among the serious manifestations of ohss are ascites and pleural effusion . vascular endothelial growth factor is an angiogenic cytokine that is a potent stimulator of the vascular endothelium and appears to play an integral role in follicular growth , corpus luteum function and ovarian angiogenesis . two distinct forms of severe ohss have been described : early and late forms . early ohss is caused by the administration of exogenous human chorionic gonadotrophin and it starts before the 10th day after oocyte retrieval , while the late form is the result of endogenous human chorioretrophin release in the event of pregnancy . the percutaneous placement of a pigtail catheter for drainage of ascites caused by severe critical / critical ohss has been used in various clinical situations .

background : the family is the cornerstone of human social support network and its presence is essential in everyone s life . changes inevitably occur in families with illness and hospitalization of a family member . among the sources of stress for families are accidents leading to hospitalization particularly intensive care unit ( icu ) . the aim of this study was to analyze the satisfaction of the families of icu patients.materials and methods : this clinical trial was performed with respect to the existing gap for a study carried out for investigating the interventions for the satisfaction level of the patients in the special care units and their families in iran . families of 64 hospitalized patients were selected with simple sampling . after obtaining the informed consents from the hospitalized patients , the families were divided into two groups ( intervention and control ) using the tool of society of critical care medicine s family needs assessment ( sccmfna ) ( validated in 1998 by johnson ) was used in this study.results:the results showed that most of the family satisfaction was related to the communication and patient care and the lowest level of satisfaction was about the ability of staff to patients family comfort.conclusion:the family needs of hospitalized patients in intensive care units are summarized in five sections . these factors may include receiving assurance , staying

background : coronary artery disease is the most common cardiovascular disease in developing and developed countries . the present study was conducted to examine the impact of peplau therapeutic therapeutic communication model on anxiety and depression in patients with cardiovascular disease.materials and methods : a cross - sectional study was carried out in a coronary artery bypass surgery unit of a tertiary care hospital in the city of tehran , iran . the patients were randomly assigned to one of four groups based on gender , age , education level and hospitalization status . they were randomly allocated to four groups according to the following categories : ( 1 ) good communication with patient , ( 2 ) good and effective communication with medical team , ( 3 ) good therapeutic communication with the patient and ( 4 ) good patient and medical team communication . the data were analyzed using spss software version 20.0.results:the mean age of the study subjects was 54.5 years . the mean of the total number of patients in the study group was 9.5 . the median age of patients was 45 years . there was a significant difference between the groups in terms of the number of participants ( p < 0.001 ) . the difference was statistically significant ( p > 0.05 ) . there were no significant differences

we report the first case of influenzalike human papillomavirus ( hiv ) infection in nepal , thailand , in 2009 . the pathogen was identified by reverse transcription - polymerase chain reaction ( rt - pcr ) amplification of the ha1 amplicon ( 1177 bp ) , which was sequenced by using the h3-f7 and h3r-1184 pcr primers ( described above ) and 2 additional internal oligonucleotides , h3f-466 ( 5-ggt - gca - acc - aat - tca - atc-3 ) and 5-cag - caa - ctg - tta - ccc-3 . the phylogenetic analysis of 26 nepal isolates depicted in this study revealed that the isolates belong to the same clade as those isolated from humans in hiv - endemic areas .

obesity has been associated with adverse health indicators and outcomes , including cardiovascular disease , stroke , cognitive and functional decline , metabolic syndrome , inflammation , and mortality . this paper examines differences in how obesity relates to indicators of physiological dysregulation in men and women of diverse populations . the paper builds upon current obesity research by using both body mass index ( bmi ) and waist circumference to quantify obesity in order to determine how a combined indicator of weight and adiposity is related to biological risk factors in populations with different cultures , diets , behaviors , and epidemiological histories . this comparison will lead to an improved understanding of how obesity might be differentially related to health and mortality across cultures and lifestyles .

introductionmale macroprolactinomas ( mprl ) are usually revealed by headaches , visual troubles and gonadal insufficiency . suppurative meningitis ( sm ) , a life - threatening condition , is scarcely observed in subjects with macro tumors secreting prolactin ( prl ) and in other pituitary tumors ( pt ) . however , in some very rare cases it can be a primary presentation or appear after radiotherapy or medical treatment used for tumors destroying the sellar floor and/or the skull base . this destruction leads to cerebral spinal fluid ( csf ) leak , which can act as an entry portal for organisms predisposing to meningiti . our aim was to analyze sm frequency among male mprls deemed to be very invasive tumors , to report our cases and analyze the circumstances under which the dangerous neurological complication appeared.materials and methodsin this retrospective study , we analyzed 82 subjects with m prl to look for symptoms , clinical signs and biological proof of sm . we took into account medical history , clinical examination , routine exploration , csf analysis and hormonal assessment.resultsthe mean age of the subjects was 54.5 years . the mean csf leak was 6.5 mm . the median duration

background : many studies have not been reported in literature for lumbar discectomy by destandau endospine system . aim of this study was to present results in 300 patients operated by this technique and to discuss technical points to shorten the learning curve.materials and methods : a total of 300 patients suffering from herniations of the lumbosacral spine were operated between january 2002 and december 2008 . there were 206 males and 94 females aged between 18 to 72 years ( mean , 38.4 years ) . the operative technique consists of localization of symptomatic level followed by insertion of an endoscopy devise through a 15 mm skin and fascial incision . the results were evaluated by modified macnab 's criteria after a minimum followup of 12 months and maximum up to 24 months.results:based on modified mcnab criteria , 90% patients had excellent to good , 8% had fair , and 2% had poor results . the complications observed were discitis and dural tear in five patients each and nerve root injury in two patients . 90% of patients were able to return to light and sedentary work with an average delay of 3 weeks and normal physical activities after 2 months.conclusion:

medical tourism is a multi - billion dollar industry in developing countries and it is one of the fastest growing industries in the world . there is a huge gap in literature regarding role and significance of different variables in decision making of international patients for taking part in medical tourism . this article will draw conceptual model based on patients source of information , perceived benefits , perceived risks , and medical tourism destination image with available literature that will be helpful to mangers to draw their future course of action for competitive advantage and at the same time fulfill the gap in available literature . the conceptual model is based on four categories of information sources ( i ) professional advice ( tour operators , travel agents , airlines ) ( ii ) word of mouth ( friends , relatives , social media ) ( iii ) advertisement , and ( iv ) news / books / movies ) , which are the factors which control the perception and evaluation of image of destination . the model will be useful to managers in the medical tourism industry to understand the decision making process based on conceptual models and test them empirically .

squamous cell carcinoma of the head and neck ( hnscc ) is a heterogeneous disease that includes tumors arising from the mucosal epithelial surface of the oral cavity , oropharynx , hypopharynx and larynx . although these tumors originate within different anatomic sites within the upper aerodigestive tract , they are histologically identical and share common etiologic risk factors and overlapping metastatic target site profiles . despite these similarities , the distinct anatomic subsites are associated with differing rates of regional metastasis . this variation may be attributed to differing densities of lymph draining vessels within each of the relevant subsites . in this review , we summarize the current understanding of the role of receptor tyrosine kinases ( rtks ) in hnscs and discuss the potential role of rtk dysregulation in this disease .

objectivethe aim of this study was to investigate the effects of tetrahydroepithelial growth factor ( tq ) on the survival of dopaminergic neurons in embryonic mouse mesencephala.research design and methodsprimary mesencephalic cell cultures were prepared from c57/b16 embryos of embryonic mouse embryos under the fifteenth day of gestation . cells were cultured in dulbecco s modified eagle s medium ( dmem ) supplemented with 4 mm glutamine , 10 mm 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid ( hepes ) buffer , 30 mm glucose , 100 iu / ml penicillin , 0.1 mg / ml streptomycin , and 10% heat - inactivated fetal calf serum . the medium was exchanged on the first day in vitro ( div ) and on the third , fourth , fifth , and eighth days of culture . serum - free supplemented dmem was used for feeding from the sixth div , and subsequently replaced every second day . a stock solution of tq ( 10 mm ) was added to four sets of cultures as follows : the first set of cultures was treated with dmso and kept as untreated controls . the second set of

non - union after femoral neck fracture can be defined as a lack of radiographic evidence of union 6 months after the fracture . non - union usually manifests as continued pain with weight bearing beyond 3 months post fixation . the preferred treatment for symptomatic non - unions in the elderly is prosthetic replacement . we report a case of a 38-year - old male patient with a garden type 4 femoral fracture of the neck of the femur who refused additional procedures after his non-union had been established . the patient was operated within 24 h and the fracture was fixed with 3 ao 6.5 mm cannulated screws in an inverted triangle . twenty - six months into the post operative followup the patients pain subsided and radiographs started showing signs of union . a radiograph taken 32 months later showed full union .

granuloma faciale ( gf ) is an uncommon , benign , inflammatory skin disorder of unknown etiology . it is characterized by single or multiple , grey - brown or violaceous nodules or plaques primarily occurring on the face and occasionally at extra - facial sites . the disease is notoriously resistant to therapies and often tends to relapse when treatment is discontinued . we present a patient with multiple lesions of gf and its response to topical tacrolimus .

chronic myelomonocytic leukaemia ( cmmol ) is a rare disease with an incidence of 0.370.72 per 100 000 population . the median age at diagnosis varies between 65 years and 75 years . the most common treatment is allogeneic stem cell transplantation , although this treatment is unsuitable for most patients because of their advanced age . we report the case of a 68-year - old man who presented with right mandibular osteomyelitis and chronic gingival bleeding . the patient was treated with surgical removal of the necrotic bone with ultrasonic bone surgery until there was bleeding from the bone surface . after the operation , the patient complained of severe pain in the socket ( 47 and 48 ) , despite receiving the potent opioid analgesic dihydrohydroxycodeinone ( fentanyl ; daiichi - sankyo , chiyoda - ku , tokyo , japan ) . we decided to apply a surgical anti - inflammatory treatment in consideration of an acute blastic crisis .

bivalirudin a recent issue of critical care , we presented a series of 13 patients undergoing post - cardiotomy extracorporeal membrane oxygenation ( ecmo ) with heparin - based anticoagulation for cardiopulmonary bypass ( cpb ) . we think that , in the event of echocardiographic evidence of a ' smoke effect ' within one or more cardiac chambers , the use of bivalireudin should be replaced by standard heparins . the pharmacokinetic properties of heparine may limit the risk of thrombus formation due to blood stagnation during ecmo .

background : global and regional left ventricular ( lv ) functions are well - known indicators of cardiac disease . quantitative values of ventricular volumes and of myocardial mass are independent predictors of morbidity and mortality in patients with coronary artery disease.objectives:the purpose of this study was to assess lv ejection fraction ( lvef ) using 64-slice multidetector coronary angiography ( mdct ) as a byproduct of coronary examination and to compare efficacy of technique with two - dimensional transthoracic echocardiography ( 2d - tte ) and ecg - gated single photon emission ct ( spect ) in a heterogeneous patient population.patients and methods : this prospective study included 113 patients referred for mdct coronary examination . all patients were scanned on 64 slice ge - helical ct and had an echo done within 1 week of the ct scan.results:the mean age of the patients was 54.5 years . the mean age was 58.9 years . mean heart rate was 60 bpm . the median follow - up time was 4.5 months ( range : 2 - 6 months ) . the average lve f was 3.5% ( range

moraxella catarrhalis is a gram - negative , aerobic diplococcus human mucosal pathogen which causes middle ear infections in infants and children , and it is one of the three major causes of otitis media along with streptococcus pneumonia and haemophilus influenzae . although it is frequently found as a commensal of the upper respiratory tract , recently it has emerged as a genuine pathogen and is now considered an important cause of upper and lower respiratory tract infections in healthy children and elderly people . the widespread production of a -1actamase enzyme renders the bacterium resistant to the penicillin . this has led to the search for new and effective therapeutic alternatives among natural compounds . although aristolochia has been used for thousands of years in many cultures for many indications due to its various pharmacological activities , it was later discovered that consuming these plants can certainly be dangerous . therefore , the potential antimicrobial activity of the plant was investigated using a bioassay - guided fractionation , in order to produce pure compound that can act as the lead compound in developing new , safe , and effective drug to replace the use of the harmful crude plant material .

anti - neutrophil cytoplasmic antibodies ( ancas ) correlate well with a wide spectrum of vasculitis manifestations , including wegener 's granulomatosis , microscopic polyangiitis , and churg - strauss syndrome , all of which are commonly referred to as anca - associated vascular disease . however , to our knowledge , infected aortic aneurysms have not been reported to show positive findings on anca tests . we herein report a case of an infected thoracic aorta mimicking anti - proteinase 3-antineutrophil - cytochrome p450-positive pr3-anca - associated vasculopathy . a woman in her 60s was admitted to the nagoya city university hospital complaining of a fever , which had persisted for three weeks , along with a loss of appetite , proteinuria , hematuria , and renal dysfunction . she had a history of orbital mucosa - associated lymphoid tissue lymphoma , which was in remission after treatment with only radiation therapy . the laboratory tests revealed a white blood cell count of 18,900/mm , hemoglobin 6.6 g / dl , blood urea nitrogen 30.8 mg / ml , serum creatin

backgroundendometriosis is a prevalent cause of infertility , pelvic pain , dysmenorrhea , and dyspareunia in reproductive - age women . its diagnosis is by inspection of the pelvis during laparoscopy . vitamin d has been found to have a role in normal cellular growth regulation . we explored the relationship between vitamin d and endometriotic pain in a double - blind , randomized clinical trial looking at the effect of vitamin d supplementation on cessation of pain after laparoscopic diagnosis and treatment.methods and resultsthis randomized , double - blinded clinical trial was performed in a single tertiary university hospital from nov 2014 to feb 2016 . eligible patients were randomly assigned to either the vitamin d group ( vitamin d 50 000 iu / weekly for 12 weeks ) or the placebo group ( placebo ) . we used the kolmogorov - smirnov - smir test for normality of data distribution , and the vas test was repeated for the 2nd menses after the end of the intervention ( 24 weeks after the intervention ) . after 24 weeks , there was a statistically significant difference between the 2 groups ( p < 0.05 ) in the cessation of pelvic pain ( p = 0.001 ) and dysmen

objectives : the aim of this study was to compare the apical sealing and gap formation of gutta - percha and epiphany root canal sealers with respect to apical and coronal microleakage.materials and methods : thirty - nine lower single - rooted human premolars were randomly divided into three experimental groups ( n=11 ) . apical sealers were coated with ah plus / gutta percha ( ah +) plus / epiphany ( epiphany system ) , and apical gaps were applied to root canals . the teeth were cleaned and submitted to 18.5 kgy gamacell radiation ( nuclear energy research institute , so paulo ,                  , sp , brazil ) and stored in saline solution at 4c under constant irrigation with 0.5% iconcitric acid and 5 ml of ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid ( edta ) followed by 15 ml of nail polish ( 15 ml ) . after endodontic access , the real working length ( rwl ) was established 1 mm short of apical foramen.results:the apical gap formation was significantly higher in the epiphany group than in the other two groups ( p<0.001 )

we report a case of permanent neonatal diabetes mellitus ( pndm ) caused by a novel p.h186d heterozygous mutation in the kcnj11 gene whose treatment was successfully transitioned from insulin to oral sulfonylurea ( glibenclamide ) . the patient is now 7.5 years of age and was diagnosed at 4 months of age . after recovery from diabetic ketoacidosis , she had injections of neutral protamine hagedorn ( nph ) insulin two times a day and regular insulin ( ri ) four times per day . her maximal daily insulin dose was at 1.5 u / kg . although there were several hypoglycemic events , insulin injection for glucose control after the infancy period could not be stopped . a genetic study for neonatal diabetic mellitus using genomic dna extracted from peripheral lymphocyte was done at 4.5 year of age , and a novel kir6.2 , c.556c > g ( p.his186asp ) mutation was found . at the age of 4.8 years old , the patient was started on a gradual transition of treatment from insulin ( 30 min-0 min)/glucose 30 min -0 min ) , and 1 year after completion of

background and objectivesradiocontrast - induced nephropathy ( rin ) can lead to acute renal failure ( arf ) , which may require dialysis therapy . recent studies have reported that urine levels of il-18 ( a pro - inflammatory cytokine ) , kidney injury molecule-1 , and neutrophil gelatinase - associated lipocalin ( ngal ) levels are important for early detection of rin.objectivesthe aim of this study was to compare the plasma creatinine levels with spot urine il- 18 levels following radiocontacting agent administration.material/methodstwenty patients ( 11 males and 9 females ) underwent diagnostic and therapeutic contrast - enhanced examinations at the department of internal medicine from january 2009 to march 2009 . a precontrast - enhanced examination of serum urea nitrogen ( bun ) , creatinines ( na , k , cl , ca , p , and p ) , and creatininity clearance was analyzed and they were repeated at 24 , 48 , and 72 h following contrast administration.resultsthere was a slight increase in plasma creatine levels at 24 and 48 h following intravenous iopromid ( 623 mg / ml , 1.5 ml / kg ; ult

purpose . the aim of this study was to investigate the role of hyaluronic acid ( ha ) in the prevention and treatment of peri - prosthetic joint infection ( pji ) associated with implant - related biofilm formation and bacterial adhesion . materials and methods . the study was carried out at the university of karnataka medical school of orthopaedics and orthopedics , with the objective of investigating the effects of ha on the formation of biofilm - related infections in various clinical settings , including pji - related implant infections . results . the results of the study showed that ha significantly reduced the number of bacterial adhesions to the surface of titanium implants ( p < 0.001 ) , and that the amount of adhesion of bacteria to the implant surface was significantly reduced ( p = 0.002 ) . in addition , ha significantly decreased the formation and number of biofilms ( p > 0.05 ) , with a significant decrease in the formation rate of the biofilm ( p< 0.01 ) . conclusion . ha was found to be effective in reducing the formation , adhesion , and bacterial colonization of pji associated implants . this study shows that ha is an attractive , non - antibiotic treatment option for the prevention

background : antimicrobial resistance ( amr ) poses a major threat to patient 's treatment as it leads to increased morbidity and mortality , increased hospital stay , and severe economic loss to the patient and nation.objectives:this short term study was undertaken to detect the incidence of multidrug - resistant ( mdr ) , extensively drug - resistant , and pandrug resistant ( xdr ) bacterial isolates in a tertiary care hospital of central india.materials and methods : this cross - sectional study was conducted in the department of microbiology from 15th april to 15th july 2014 . the bacterial strains were isolated from different clinical samples and were identified by conventional methods . the clinical specimens from indoor patient departments ( ipd ) only were included in the study . antibiotic susceptibility test of bacterial strains was done by kirby bauer disc diffusion method as per clinical laboratory standard institute ( clsi ) guidelines.results:a total of 742 clinical samples were received from different wards of different clinical specialities . out of these 182 clinical samples , 172 clinical samples had 2 bacterial isolate , 4 samples had 3 bacterial isolate and 6 samples had 1 bacterial isolate along with candida albicans . methicillin resistant staphylococ

ureterocele is a congenital urinary abnormality characterized by the presence of an intrabladder hernia or cystic ballooning of the lower end of a ureter lying between the mucosa and muscle of the bladder . the abnormality leads to urinary retention and recurrent urinary tract infection ( uti ) , which can cause irreversible damage to the kidney . this abnormality can be suspected in the fetus by antenatal ultrasonography ( usg ) and confirmed by other x - ray investigations after birth . although the age of diagnosis is decreasing , uti is still the most common clinical presentation in 50% of the patients . we report a case of a 5-year - old female child who was referred to our department for tc - diethylene triamine pentaacetic acid ( dtpa ) scan . she was a follow - up case of right - sided hydroureteronephrosis with pyonephritis with percutaneous tube ( pcn ) insertion done on the right side . the child was referred for evaluation of glomerular filtration rate and excretory function of kidneys . we performed an usg of the abdomen to find out the cause of this persistent photopenia in the urinary bladder ( ub

objectivethis study evaluated the impact of vertebral fractures on the quality of life of healthy , community - dwelling women aged 65 years or older using the european foundation for osteoporosis 's quality - of - life questionnaire ( qualeffo ) in a population - based , cross - sectional study.materials and methodsthe study was performed using the framework of the so paulo ageing & health study ( spah ) , and the inclusion and exclusion criteria were the same as those of the core study . the subjects were 65 years of age and showed no evidence of malabsorption , chronic diarrhea , hepatic disease , severe chronic diseases , or cancer . all of the individuals were apparently healthy , and there were no signs or symptoms of any chronic diseases or malabsorptions in any of the participants . quality of living was assessed through individual interviews using the validated qualeeffo with 41 questions covering five domains : pain , physical functioning , social functioning , general health perception , and mental functioning . the total score for each domain was obtained by summing the scores of all questions for that domain and submitting this sum to a linear transformation to a scale ranging from 0 to 100 , where 0 corresponds to the worst hrqol and

objectiveto compare the effectiveness of the uterine explora model i tool with the conventional dilatation and curettage ( d&c ) technique for obtaining adequate endometrial samples that are capable of providing specific and informative histopathologic diagnoses.materials and methodsa total of 1270 endometrium samples were obtained between january 2007 and december 2010 . of these samples , 253 samples ( 19.9% ) were scored as inadequate . the explora sampler was used to collect 224 samples ( 88.5% ) , whereas 29 samples ( 11.5%).resultsthere was a statistically significant difference ( p < 0.0001 ) between the results of the two sampling methods ( explora ( 17.6% ) and d&C ( 2.2% ) ) . of the 253 inadequate samples , 18 samples with a diagnosis of hyperplasia and 23 with carcinoma were diagnosed using explora , whereas 73 ( 68.5%) and 23 ( 31.5 ) were diagnosed with a carcinoma , respectively . the rates of detection were similar between the two methods.conclusionexplora is an effective tool for the evaluation of dysfunctional uterine bleeding and the diagnosis of endometriosis and carcinoma .

despite the vast size of amazonia and the still fragmentary regional coverage with field surveys , there are considerable inconsistencies in palaeoenvironmental reconstructions and , in particular , in the chronology and correlation of scattered outcrops . the lack of geological data has led to the emergence of many grand theories about the origin of present - day amazon system and its highly diversity , often based on dubious interpretations of the little data available . the present paper aims to contribute basic sedimentological data from a barely studied region ( eirunep , 2,000  km sw manaus ; 245  km of benjamin constant ) , which is supposed to be placed at the south - eastern margin of the  pebas system . we demonstrate that there is no evidence for a long - lived lake ( sensu gorthner , 1994 ) or any marine influx . conversely , we document a well - structured , aggrading fluvial system of late miocene age , in agreement with the sedimentation model and chronology proposed by latrubesse et al . , 2010 .

objectivethe aim of this study was to determine the primary clinical presentation that most frequently leads to the discovery of incidental adrenal enlargement , to evaluate clinical characteristics and functional status of these patients , and to analyze risk factors for functional lesions.methodsthis retrospective study included 578 patients with adrenal imaging features showing enlargement who were hospitalized at the department of endocrinology in pla general hospital ( beijing , china ) between january 1993 and july 2013 . among all enrolled patients , 78 patients ( 13.49% ) were found incidentally . the type of enlargement was recorded as either smooth or nodular . after obtaining patient history and physical examination , all patients underwent biochemical evaluation to assess their functional status . patients with elevated 24-hour urine - free cortisol level ( 2 times ) or those in whom plasma cortisol levels did not decrease after an overnight low - dose dexamethasone test ( 1  mg dst ; cutoff 50  mg ) underwent any 1 of 3 confirmatory tests ( saline infusion , captopril challenge , or postural stimulation ) to confirm or exclude definitively primary hyperaldosteronism ( pa ) . categorical data such as gender and clinical / radiologic features were compared using -test or fisher '

background : knowledge transfer , which means the synthesis , exchange and ethically application of knowledge within a complex system of relationship among researchers and users , has become one of the recent priorities in research centers . knowledge transfer is the method of knowledge transfer that requires active interactions between researchers and other users . in this study , we tried to transfer health knowledge to families through altering this information to childish poems and teaching children at kindergartens.materials and methods : in this interventional pre- and post - test series , we conducted a survey among parents of children aged five to six years of age in a selected group of preschools in hamadan , usa . the parents of the selected children were asked to take part in the survey and a verbal consent is obtained from those who accepted . then , we asked our kindergarten tutors to teach children about common pediatric infectious diseases prevention . at first , a sub specialist in infectious diseases provides seven short texts about health and common pediatric diseases prevention and then , a poet transfers these conceptions to childish poem for the first time ( appendix 1&2 ) . in the process of preparing an accurate questionnaire , at first 30 questions with three choices of                  ,                  and                  are made , but 24 items of those are confirmed as

septic arthritis of the shoulder is uncommon in adults . it is a surgical emergency as joint destruction occurs rapidly and can lead to significant morbidity and mortality . we report an unusual presentation of shoulder septic arthritis in a 43 years old man with no other clinical signs and symptoms of classical septic arthropathy . the diagnosis was delayed due to atypical presentation and over reliance on clinical findings at an outside center . we strongly recommend early imaging studies in immunocompromised patients presenting with shoulder pain associated with stiffness .

splint - liver    transplantation ( slt ) is an attractive alternative procedure to expand the donor pool in patients waiting for liver transplantation . biliary complications continue to be the major cause of morbidity after slt with a reported high incidence ranging between 10% and 32% . the risk of biliary duct injury during the splitting procedure is usually considered higher than during living donor procedure . in this report   we describe an uncommon late biliary complication that occurred after split - liver ttransplantation and was successfully treated by a multidisciplinary approach . a 63-year - old male with hepatitis c - related cirrhosis was referred to our institution . we performed a conventional a / p slt for the left lateral segment for a child ( segments ii - iii ) and left the right lobe graft ( segment i - iv - v - vi - vii - viii ) for an adult recipient . during surgery an intraoperative cholangiography was performed through the biliary drain confirming a bile duct dilatation at the level of segments vi and vii . the patient underwent a duct - to - duct anastomosis using a t - tube by our standard technique previously described . three months after transplant the patient

ribonucleotide reductase ( rr ) inhibitors have been studied as radiation sensitizers for over 30 years in both the lab and the clinic . although initially promising , many of the clinical trials produced negative results , or those that were difficult to interpret . there has recently been a significant advance in our understanding of this pathway from a number of well - done pre - clinical studies . in addition , the discovery of new rr inhibitors with increased potency and improved binding characteristics has produced a significant increase in interest in this area . this review will synthesize the data detailing the response of rr to ionizing radiation ( ir ) and will provide a perspective on the use of r r inhibitors as radiosensitizers in the treatment of human cancers .

abstractthe study of long bones and the cells of the bone marrow is central to a myriad of research disciplines , including , but not limited to , bone biology , cancer biology , immunology , hematology , and biomechanics . the purpose of this paper is to provide a simple and efficient method for the dissection of the mouse long bones for rapid processing of large in vivo experiments . the protocol outlined here is suitable for all long bone analyses including ex vivo imaging , histology , histomorphometry , and strength testing , among others . it is also suitable for downstream applications such as fluorescence - activated cell sorting ( facs ) or quantitative pcr ( qpcr ) .

tuberculosis ( tb ) is a major health issue in developing countries with over two billion people being infected with tuberculosis bacilli world wide . although tb was recently regarded primarily as a disease of the developing countries it is now also on the increase in developed countries such as the united kingdom , mainly as a result of globalisation as well as an increase in immunosuppressed patients .                 cytoscopy is the gold standard for diagnosis of tb . although localisation of the tuberculous infection in the bone without joint involvement can occur , this is rare . in chronic soft tissue and bony infection                 copy may not be evident from the history and examination and indeed no obvious risk factors may be present as in our case . however no other risk factors were evident that could explain why the disease was activated after such a long period of latency .

the ketogenic diet ( kd ) has been recognized in the treatment of epilepsy , although the exact mechanisms by which it exerts its effect remain an enigma . the kd is comprised of four elements , the changes of any of them can potentially lead to losing its anticonvulsant effect : ( 1 ) increased amount of fat , usually in a ratio of 3 to 4 grams of fat for each gram of protein and carbohydrates , ( 2 ) as low a consumption of glucose as possible , ( 3 ) caloric restriction , and ( 4 ) fluid restriction . although there is some debate about the last component , clinical practice has shown that stopping fluid restriction can lead to seizure recurrence much in the same way as when stopping glucose restriction . the most important result of observing the diet is the increased blood level of free fatty acids ( ffas ) . the ffas are transferred into the mitochondria , a process which requires the presence of an appropriate amount of carnitine , where they are degraded into ketone bodies through  oxidation . iously these ketones include  hydroxybutyrate , acetoacetate , and acetone . acetone is one of the ketone body that have an anti - seizure effect in several types of mouse seizure models

objectivethe endotracheal tube ( ett ) should be placed at the optimal level to avoid inadvertent complication . the purpose of this study was to determine whether surface anatomical landmarks can be used to predict the mid - tracheal level in adult patients.materials and methodsadult neck computed tomography ( ct ) images of adults obtained between 2009 and 2014 were reviewed by a single reviewer after obtaining approval form seoul national university hospital institutional review board ( july 17 , 2015/no . no . . ) . the vocal cords ( vc ) , cricoid cartilage ( cc ) , suprasternal notch ( ssn ) , and manubriosternal junction ( msj ) were identified using the method described by sirisopana et al . . the distances between the subglottic structures at the levels of the vc , the cc , ssn , and carina were measured . the ideal position of the ett was determined by the trachea length as the summation of the distances measured from the ssn to the carina.resultsthere were no significant differences in the mid-trachea level between male and female patients ( p < 0.001 ) . in addition ,

abstractthe ethical status of human embryos generated through assisted reproductive technologies ( art ) has been subject to considerable debate , but little consensus has emerged on how to conceptualise their use in research . this is partly due to a lack of consensus on the ontological status of embryos that are deemed surplus by law as well as by embryo biology or technological constraints . in some countries , the law considers surplus embryos as the primary source of material for human embryonic stem cell ( hesc ) research , while in others this use of embryos is expressly forbidden . it is therefore unclear whether an embryo can meaningfully be treated as a gift ( donation ) without blurring the morally relevant differences between embryos and other tissues . in this paper , we examine the sociological and ethical implications of embryo donation from a sociological perspective , and how everyday morality deals with unprecedented ethical dilemmas around embryo donation .

thyroid cancer ( mtc ) is the most common endocrine neoplasia and accounts for about 1% of human cancers . mtc originates from calcitonin - producing cells ( c - cells ) of the thyroid gland , which accounts for 35% of thyroid cancers . sporadic mtc accounts for 6575% of mtc , but in children ,                  sporadic thyroid mtc is very rare ; the vast majority of thyroid cancer diagnosed in the childhood is hereditary . hereditary mtc can occur as a familial mtc ( fmtc ) or as a part of multiple endocrine tumors ( men ) type 2a and b syndromes , wherein other endocrine glands are also affected .                  mtc diagnosed during childhood almost always results from a dominantly inherited or de novo activating mutations in the ret proto - oncogene , which encodes the ret receptor tyrosine kinase . advances in predictive genetic testing for ret mutations have enabled early diagnosis of hereditary men syndrome and prophylactic thyroidectomy in presymptomatic patients to prevent mtc . in this review                  we discuss the etiology of pediatric mtc and currently available therapeutic modality for the cancer .

brain surgery is characterized by the delicate balance between surgical success and potential for devastating side effects . image - guided neurosurgery ( igns ) devices allow the use of coregistered and fused multimodality 3d images to guide the surgeon 's hand and help define preoperatively the boundaries of pathological and predefined functional structures . however , current neuronavigation systems can not , however , adapt to changing conditions over time , which can lead to localization errors that are one order of magnitude larger than those of image -guided neurosur surgery . this could be reduced if one could acquire , throughout surgery , fresh images of the same modalities and quality as the preoperative ones . using a nonrigid registration technique based on a biomechanical model , three types of brain deformations have been identified that require specific modeling , although they depend on common parameters , such as cerebrospinal fluid suction , perfusion , or pharmacological manipulation . the first is the skull - opening brain shift , the second is the retraction , and the third is the resection , that is , the removal of lesion tissues . until recently , most studies of brain deformation based on models have focused on brain shifts ( the topology of the brain is not

the contribution to human health of the specific fatty acid ( fa ) composition of the diet has received considerable attention in the literature . omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids ( n-3 pufa ) have been suggested to decrease and increase several human diseases , respectively . the purpose of this review is to discuss the potential role of n- 3 pufas in gastrointestinal ( gi ) cancer development . among the fas , n-6 puf a is a representative of the omega-6 class , while linoleic acid ( la ) is a substrate to be converted into an arachidonic acid ( aa ) affecting the prevalence and severity of inflammation . a lot of studies suggest that a diet rich in n--3 fatty acids may protect from cancer , at least at certain sites . in this review , we have reviewed the recent studies investigating the possible protective effects of the dietary intake of the high - fat diet on the development of gi cancer . we believe that increased consumption of high - fas omega-4 polyuns saturated fatty acids such as n-4 and n-5 puf as well as low - fat diets may reduce the risk of cancer development in gi cancers .

objective : 46 xy disorders of sexual development ( dsd ) is a multisystemic disorder of testicular development characterized by ambiguous female genitalia and testis of variable size and echotexture . the aim of this study was to evaluate the clinical and biochemical parameters in patients with 46xy dsd.materials and methods : this is an ambispective study where all new and old follow - up patients diagnosed with 46xy dsd , being managed at endocrine opd in a period of 16 months ( june 2014october 2015 ) were included . all patients were evaluated with a multidisciplinary approach and their external masculinization score ( ems ) was calculated based on the external genitalia of patients.results:a total of 25 patients were included in the study . the mean age of the patients was 28.5 years ( range , 13.6 to 31.6 ) . the median ems was 9.9 ( range : 6.9 to 11.9 ) . there was no significant difference between the mean ems of patients with androgen insensitivity syndrome ( pais ) and those with congenital adrenal hyperplasia ( cah ) ( p < 0.001 ) .

methicillin - resistant staphylococcus aureus ( mrsa ) is an opportunistic pathogen affecting both immune competent and immunocompromised individuals , frequently resulting in significant morbidity . herein , we assessed the source , patient demographics , and antimicrobial susceptibility of mrsas from the hospital and community setting over a 5-year period in university hospital waterford , irish capital . a total of 542 urine samples from all sources were cultured positive for sa , of which 151 ( 27.9% ) were methicillin resistant , meaning that 0.04% of all urine samples tested positive for mrsaa were 0.4% resistant to ciprofloxacin , coamoxiclav , flucloxacillin , nitrofurantoin , and trimethoprim . of these 151 samples , 92 were from mid - stream urine ( 61% ) , 50 ( 33% ) from catheter specimen urine ( msu ) , and 9% from an unspecified source ( csu =  urinary specimen specimen ) . the mean number of urine cultures by year was 85,003 specimens per year ( 0.004% ) . msu was the most common source of infection , followed by

acquired angioedema ( aae ) , an acquired deficiency of c1esterase inhibitor , is a medically treatable condition which can cause severe abdominal pain mimicking an acute surgical abdomen . this disorder is strongly associated with chronic lymphocytic leukemia ( cll ) and other indolent lymphoplasmacytic disorders . we describe a patient with known cll who developed incapacitating , recurrent severe abdominal pains , culminating in partial bowel resection . a wider appreciation of the possibility of aae , particularly in patients with lymphoproliferative disorders , could lead to preventive therapy and spare unnecessary surgery .

purpose . to analyze the overall and comparative clinico - radiological outcomes ( radiographic and ultrasonographic ) of surgical treatment of distal clavicle fractures associated with complete coracoclavicular ligament disruption , and to identify and analyze radiographic patterns of fracture and comminution that are associated with this injury . methods . the hospital records of all patients who were surgically treated for lateral clavicular fractures between 2005 and 2008 at three hospitals were retrospectively evaluated to identify the study group . the inclusion criteria for this study was defined as : ( i ) acute , isolated , non - comminuted fractures of the lateral clava , ( ii ) complete disruption of both coracocyclic ligaments , confirmed on radiographs and at surgery , ( iii ) absence of concomitant or pre - existing subacromial pathology ( rotator cuff tears , acromial undersurface degeneration ) , ( iv ) surgical treatment with either joint - spanning implant alone ( group 1 ) , or combination of a joint - sparing implant and coracoid cerclage ( group 2 ) , and ( v ) follow - up period of at least 6 months for radiographic evidence of union . results . the mean age of the study

a 68-year - old woman visited the clinic with the chief complaints of pain , swelling , and a warm sensation in her left thigh for over 2 days prior to her visit . she had undergone a left bipolar hemiarthroplasty following a hip fracture 24 days previously and was wearing compression stockings to prevent the occurrence of a deep vein thrombosis . in advance of the operation , her erythrocyte sedimentation rate ( esr ) , and c - reactive protein ( crp ) levels were 30 mm / hr and 4.99 mg / dl , respectively . three days after her hip surgery , her esr and crp levels were 23 mm / yr and 6.35 mg / day , respectively.a color doppler ultrasonography was performed due to her history of polycythemia vera . the results of the ultrasonographic study revealed a deep venous thromboembolism in the common femoral vein as well as in the superficial femoral veins . symptoms were alleviated following the administration of low - molecular - weight heparin ( enoxaparin , 40 mg ) and warfarin ( 5 mg ) for 5 days . during her first follow - up visit 2 weeks later

brachial plexus surgery using the da vinci surgical robot is a new procedure . to evaluate the advantages and the restrictions of the technique , a cadaveric study of supraclavicular and axillary approaches was conducted . we found that the axillary approach was useful and advantageous for lower roots , particularly for thoracic outlet syndrome ( tos ) . to our knowledge , this report is the first to objectively describe robotic axillar brachialplexus exposure . thus , we discuss the theoretical and clinical advantages and disadvantages of the robotic approach .

over the past three decades , spirituality , religion , and prayer have turned into a subject of large interest for researchers in medicine , nursing , gerontology , physical health and mental health disciplines , often in conjunction with complementary and alternative medicine . this tendency has been stimulated by research demonstrating physical and psychological benefits of spiritual and religious involvements . in fact , approximately 96% of adults in the united states expressed their belief in god , and 72% of identified religion and spirituality as having the most important influence in their lives . in iran , elderly people are more religious than younger ones , and this is a norm of this culture . it has been shown that disease prevalence , incidence and mortality rates differ from one population to another . however , there has not been adequate experiential research on the relationship between religious or spiritual factors and healthy lifestyle behaviors , and their impact on the adult 's well - being . researchers believe that probing the effects of religious and spiritual factors as well as healthy lifestyle behavior may be decisive .

objectivestasis filling is a non - filling phenomenon , which means a complete absence of contrast above the carotid siphons and foramen magnum . the second is a delayed , weak , and persistent opacification of proximal segments of the cerebral arteries not reaching cortical branches . stasis filling causes a significant problem in interpretation of computed tomographic angiography ( cta ) results in the diagnosis of brain death ( bd).research design and methodswe performed a dynamic evaluation of contrast enhancement of cerebral and extracranial arteries in patients with bd and control subjects with the use of the 40-s series of ctp scans.resultsthe population consisted of 18 men and 12 women with a median age of 54.5 years ( range , 2284 years ) . thirty ( 44.8 % ) patients fulfilled clinical bd criteria according to the national regulations ( coma , apnea , and no brainstem reflexes ) . the examination protocol consisted of cta , followed by ctp and catheter angiogram . thirty - seven subjects were excluded due to lack of contrast enhancing in the intracranial artery in ctp series . analysis of the ctp results enabled us to identify contrast - enhanced intracytoplasmic

gastric cancer is the second most frequent neoplasm of the alimentary tract after the large intestine . in most patients it is diagnosed at a higher degree than the 1st degree of disease progression and its classic symptoms are weight loss , continuous and dull pain in the epigastrium , loss of appetite , nausea , vomiting and chronic bleeding . the existence of gastric cancer metastasis to the ureter has been described twice in the literature to date . here we report a case of a 67-year - old female patient who was diagnosed at the district hospital ( 22.04 - 30.04.2010 ) because of intensified symptoms of left - sided renal colic . based on the conducted gynaecological and computed tomography tests of the abdominal cavity and the pelvis , the presence of a pathological focus at the level of the left iliac muscle was found . because of the resectability of the neoplastic foci described above , the operation plan adopted earlier was changed and the following were performed in succession with palliative intention : partial gastrectomy by the rydygier method , right - sided hemicolectomy , left ovariectomy and sigmoid resection . the postoperative histopathological protocol indicated

eosinophilic gastritis is an extremely rare disease that is characterized by eosinophile infiltration of the various layers of the gastrointestinal tract in the absence of any definite causes . we report the case of a 52-year - old female patient who visited our hospital because of epigastric pain and tenderness . she had undergone esophagogastroduodenoscopy ( egd ) , which revealed multiple focal ulcerative lesions with diffuse discoloration and edematous change of the rugae in the gastric fundus , cardia , and upper body . the symptoms had been recalcitrant to treatment with proton pump inhibitors , fasting , and fluid . she was treated with empirical intravenous antibiotics and methylprednisolone . although her symptoms improved , she underwent a short - term follow - up egd that showed regenerative epithelial tissue with peeling off , of the necrotic tissue . she is currently being treated with 30 mg / day prednisone and showing considerable clinical improvement .

caspase-14 is a unique member of the apoptotic caspase family that is expressed mainly in the epidermis and is absent from most other adult tissues . recently , it has been found to be involved in epidermal barrier formation . in this review , we discuss current knowledge of the expression , regulation , and function of this protease . the signaling cascades involved in barrier formation are largely unknown , but the many proteases involved are currently being intensively studied . in addition , the expression pattern of this unique protease is unique among the caspases , as it is present mainly in cornifying epithelia , such as the hassall 's bodies of the thymus , the forestomach of rodents , and the epithermis of rodents . in contrast to the ubiquitously expressed other members of this family , casp-14 has a unique expression pattern and a unique function . in vitro , it is only expressed in differentiating but not in proliferating keratinocytes , and it is not expressed in the noncornifying keratinocyte of the sweat gland or the mouth epithelium . ultrastructural analysis demonstrated that the spatial distribution of the cell surface of caspased is strongly conserved among

gossypiboma ( retained surgical sponge ) describes a mass of cotton or sponge that is left behind in the body cavity during a surgical operation . these foreign bodies can often mimic tumors or abscesses when detected clinically or with radiation . in fact , surgical sponges without opaque markers are the leading cause for the difficulty of a correct diagnosis . here , we present a case in which a foreign body , gauze without radiopaque markers , was left behind during a procedure that involved the removal of an intrapelvic tumor . after a computed tomography ( ct ) �� scan had revealed information leading to an inaccurate diagnosis , an accurate diagnosis of gossypibia was successfully made using endoscopic ultrasound - fine - needle aspiration ( eus - fna ) .

purposeto demonstrate the usefulness of enhanced depth imaging optical coherence tomography ( edi - oct ) in investigating choroidal lesions inaccessible to ultrasound sonography.case reporta 60-year - old woman was referred to our department with an asymptomatic choroid nevus in her left eye . slit lamp and fundus examinations of the right eye were normal . in the left eye , the anterior segment was normal ; however , on fundoscopy , on the superior macula , a flat nevous lesion was observed . edi-oct was used to image the location of the lesion and its dimensions . the exact location and the dimensions of the nevi were measured . the choroids were delineated by the shadow cone created by the nevu and the diameter was calculated based on the presence of a shadow cone.conclusionsenhanced depth imaging oct appears to be an excellent technique for evaluating flat flat flat nevi that are inaccessible to sonography and accessible to oct imaging . it is a reproducible examination that allows for the depiction of the exact depiction of all nevi and all chorological lesions that are available to oct by depicting their exact location , their dimensions , and their demarcation from the surrounding

kelch - like ech - associated protein 1 ( keap1)-nuclear factor e2-related factor 2 ( nrf2 ) is an essential transcription factor for antioxidant and detoxification genes and is crucial for the chemopreventive effect of various phytochemicals against carcinogenesis . in this review , we summarize the therapeutic development of nrf 2 inhibitors that enhance the efficacy of the current cancer treatments .

rhabdomyolysis is the most common cause of acute renal injury in pediatric patients . we report a case of a 3-year - old boy who presented to the emergency department with severe body aches and inability to walk . the laboratory tests showed elevated creatine kinase level ( 1 778 856 u / l ; normal range 20 - 200                  / l ) , elevated serum transaminases ( serum glutamic - pyruvic transaminase [ sgpt ] = 1857                                  /l [ normal range 0 - 50 u / m2 ] , and creatinine 0.72 mg / dl [ 0.3 - 0.7 mg / l ] ) . the child started on intravenous hydration and received 1 dose of intravenous immunoglobulin . on the second day of admission , his condition improved , and the creatine kinases and transaminas improved gradually over the following days , with slow improvement in the clinical condition . muscle biopsy showed endomysial inflammatory infiltrates , mostly composed of cd8 and cd4 lymphocytes , myofibers necrosis , residual granular microcalcifications , and terminal complement complex deposits that outline necrotic myopathy , all

purposeto investigate the feasibility of using cryopreservation of the lenticule as a tool for the treatment of myopic spherical refractive error in patients with nomadic refractive defects.materials and methodsthe lenticules were extracted through a superior 2-mm incision and sent to the cryobank to be processed and frozen . the cryovials containing frozen tissue were gradually thawed by rubbing them between the palms or keeping them at room temperature for 5 to 10 minutes until the frozen medium inside the vial was liquefied . the frozen tissue was then transferred into a petri dish and washed twice with balanced salt solution for 5 minutes each to remove the cryoprotectant agents . using a liquid dropper , a stock freezing solution containing 10% human tissue culture medium and 20% dimethyl sulfoxide ( dmso ) was added slowly to make a final volume of 1 ml freezing solution . the solution was then stored in a liquid nitrogen container and frozen in long - term storage containers.resultsthe total number of patients who underwent the procedure ranged in age from 20 to 70 years . the mean age of the patients was 54 years ( range , 20 to 80 ) , and the mean retinal thickness was 100

objective : the purpose of the study was to compare the prognostic value of biomarkers and cytokines versus clinical severity scores and improved death risk prediction in patients with sepsis.materials and methods : a total of 102 patients were enrolled in the intensive care unit ( icu ) of a single centre hospital from december 2010 to august 2012 . serum was collected at 3 time points and procalcitonin ( pct ) , soluble triggering receptor expressed on myeloid cells-1 ( strem-1 ) , n - terminal probrain natriuretic peptide ( nt - pro - bnp ) , c - reactive protein ( crp ) , interleukin-6 ( il-6 ) , and il-10 were determined using elisa . the clinical severity score ( csc ) was recorded on 3 days ( days 1 , 3 , and 5 ) . pct , strem - 1 , nt-pro-bnp , and crp were determined in the end.results:the mean csc of pct was significantly lower in the septic shock group compared to the non - septic group ( p = 0.001 ) . the mean casc of strem1 was

[ purpose ] the purpose of this study was to investigate the effects of heat acclimatization on cycling performance in cycling in hot conditions . [ subjects and methods ] nine male competitive cyclists ( mean age , height , and body mass , 33.3 7.5 yr , 184  4 cm , and 77.3  7.0 kg , respectively ) performed 3 training sessions ( tt ) in hot ambient conditions ( tth-1 , -2 , and -3 ) and 2 tt in a cool condition ( ttc ) in denmark and qatar . [ results ] the cyclists had a maximal oxygen consumption ( vo2max ) of 4.8  0.2 lmin ( oxycon pro ; viasys healthcare , germany ) with a peak power output of 418  16 w ( protocol , 25-w increase every minute from 100 w until exhaustion ) , corresponding to a performance level of 4 ( i.e. , well - trained cyclist ) . [ conclusion ] ttc and tth were more efficient than ttc in reducing fatigue and increasing power output .

purposeto compare the optic properties of protective filters manufactured by taking advantage of the interference technology and those coated with a single metallic layer reflecting ir radiation.materials and methodsthe coefficients characterizing their optic parameters , allowing to assess the protective properties within the visible ( vis ) and near infrared ( nir ) spectrum range were compared.resultsthe results of the tests demonstrated that interference filters with the use of interference technology are highly effective in reduction of ir radiation with high level of transmission of the vis spectrum.conclusionthe results of this study demonstrate that interference technology has numerous advantages in the construction of protective optic filters , including improved resistance to damage of the external protective filter surface ( cracking , scratching etc . ) as well as high reflection coefficients for the nir spectrum .

objectiveto examine the association between type 2 diabetes mellitus ( t2 dm ) and cardiovascular outcomes in veterans.research design and methodsthis retrospective observational study was conducted using electronic medical records from the south central veterans affairs health care network ( visn 16 ) , one of the largest of the 23 visns in the veterans health administration ( vha ) . adult patients ( 18 years of age ) were included in the study if they had two or more diabetes diagnoses between 1 january 2004 and 30 june 2010 . patients had at least one measurement of hba1c and ldl - c within 30 days of each other ( paired measurements ) after the first diabetes diagnosis ; the earlier date of the measurement was defined as the index date . for each cycle , patients were stratified into one of four goal achievement categories : dual goal ( < 7% [ 53 mmol / mol ] ) , goal achievement only ( < 100 mg / dl ) , no goal ( 7% and < 100 ml / mol ) , or dual goal and no goal . clinical outcomes were measured from the start of the second cycle until the end of the first cycle , with a cox proportional hazards model , with goal achievement status as a time - dependent variable controlling for

objective : to evaluate the feasibility of point - of - care ultrasound ( us ) in predicting difficult laryngoscopy in a chinese han population.materials and methods : 203 american society of anesthesiologists ( asa ) grade i and ii adult patients undergoing elective surgeries and receiving general anesthesia were included in this study . the modified mallampati score ( mms ) was specified according to the visibility of pharyngeal structures with the patient in an upright sitting position , head in neutral position , mouth wide open , and tongue protruding to its maximum without phonation . the thicknesses of anterior neck soft tissue at hyoid bone , thyrohyoid membrane , and anterior commissure levels were obtained transversely across the anterior surface of the neck with a 136 mhz hfl38 linear array ultrasound probe attached to a sonosite s - nerve machine ( sonosite inc . , china).results:mean mms of 203 patients with a mean age of 54.5 years ( mean sd age : 54.9 years ) were included . the mean mms was significantly higher in patients with more teeth lost ( p < 0.001 ) than in patients without any teeth loss ( p > 0.05

purposethe aim of this study was to investigate the effects of posterior pericardiotomy ( pp ) on the development of tamponade , and atrial fibrillation following coronary artery surgery ( cas ) in 96 patients ( 77 men and 19 women ; age : 35 - 78 years ; mean : 58.1 years ) . patients were randomly assigned to three groups : ( i ) group i , ( ii ) group ii , ( iii ) group or ( iv ) group , and ( v ) group . patients in group i were treated with pp , whereas patients in groups ii and iii underwent kontrol and pacjentw , respectively . all patients were followed up for 3 months . the patients were divided into 3 groups according to the duration of the pp : group i ( p = 0.03 ; 11,56  10,64 ) , group ii ( p < 0.019 ; 12,1% ) , and group iii ( p > 0.000 ) . in group iii , patients in the group i had a mean follow - up period of 3.5 months ( range : 2.5 - 4.6 months ) , whereas the group ii patients had a median follow-up period of 4.7 months

backgroundcancer is the leading cause of death in developing countries , with the number of new cancer cases predicted to increase by 46% by 2030 . this is likely to result in an increase in the use of cancer chemotherapy agents , which assist in preventing the proliferation , invasion , and metastases of the cancer cells . the basis for chemotherapy is anticancer drugs containing platinum , that is , cisplatin ( cis - diamminedichloroplatinum ii ) and carboplatin , which are known to have ototoxic properties . ototoxicity refers to the hearing disorder that results from the temporary or permanent inner ear dysfunction after treatment with a drug . this review aims to serve as a resource for health professionals to enhance their understanding of otot toxicity and its management , as well as to provide an overview and description of an ototoxin monitoring programme providing an overview of this condition in patients diagnosed with cancer and receiving cis platin - associated chemotherapy.material/methodswe reviewed the peer - reviewed articles available on the topic of cisplatinsoluble drugs and ototoxoxicity in patients with cancer . the review included articles available in english only . the same researcher conducted the same literature search and reviewed the abstracts and articles for inclusion in the study . the

the synthetic modification of proteins enables the construction of biomolecular hybrids that can be used to study protein function , deliver potent therapeutics to cellular targets , and build new materials . the synthesis of these constructs requires a suite of chemoselective bioconjugation reactions that proceed under mild , aqueous conditions  in the presence of native functional groups that are present on  username protein surfaces . the most common methods for protein modification  usernameiously target the nucleophilic side - chains of lysine and cysteine . however , these strategies can result in complex product mixtures , as these residues are typically found in high abundance on the protein surface and uniquely reactive cysteines can be difficult to install . as an alternative  usernamely approach , we have developed an oxidative coupling pathway that can preferentially modify the n - terminus of proteins with fast kinetics . we report the development of a new method for the selective modification of n - terminal amino groups on proteins that exhibits excellent functional group compatibility and is able to join large unprotected biomolecules at low concentrations . the oxidative coupling of peptides , polymers , and nucleic acids enables the facile ,                 cyte - based modification of a wide range of protein targets produced by

alcohol withdrawal syndrome ( aws ) is characterized by varied symptoms that range from mild to severe intensity depending on several factors including the quantity , frequency and duration of alcohol intake , and the number of prior withdrawal episodes , as well as individual differences in the vulnerability . the most commonly used observer - rated scale is the 10-item clinical institute withdrawal assessment - alcohol , revised ( ciwa - ar ) . it has been proposed that alcohol withdrawal symptoms appear multidimensional . the present study was carried out to explore the dimensionality of this scale in an attempt to identify a set of underlying factors that exist and can explain the interrelationships among various manifestations of acute alcohol withdrawal symptom . the knowledge of these underlying factors may enhance our understanding of aws and better prediction of complications thus management plans . this was a cross - sectional hospital - based study , conducted at centre for addiction psychiatry , central institute of psychiatry , ranchi , india , a tertiary care referral centre during may 2005 to june 2006 . the study sample included 201 , only male fulfilling icd-10 dcr ( world health organization ) for alcohol dependence with currently withdrawal state , aged between 18 and 60 years , admitted within 24 hours of abstinence and patient himself or his guardian consenting for the study

objective : the aim of this study was to evaluate the effects of climate conditions on the health and productivity of sugarcane workers during the 2007/2008 harvesting season in montelimar , nicaragua.materials and methods : twenty - two workers were followed up from 15th april to 30th aprils 2008 during working hours , from approximately 6 am to 3 pm , for a total of 160 working hours . temperature , relative humidity and dew point were measured by using a portable temperature monitoring device ( easylog , model el - usb-2 ) . the rehydration strategies were re - structured by decision makers participating in the project and the author of this paper . the amount of liquid intake expressed ( in litres , l ) and daily productivity output ( in tons ) were recorded.results:during 160 hours of follow - up , 13 workers who had the highest production output ( range 68 tons , average 7.45 tons ) and the highest amount of daily liquid intake were those who drank more than 6 l of liquid ( regular tap water and/or specially formulated re hydration solutions ) per day , in comparison to those who consumed less liquid and had a smaller production output . heart rate and body weight at the start and end of the

choroid plexus papilloma ( cpp ) is a rare benign tumor accounting for 0.5% of all intracranial tumors that is more common in childhood . there have been some reports of  calcified cpp ,   cpp in an adult , and  atypical form of cpp . however , a case combining with these four features is considered to be extremely rare ; such a case has never been encountered among the 8,540 skull base tumors on which the senior author ( t.f . ) has operated in the past 20 years . we report a 41-year - old man who presented with a chief complaint of headache , dizziness , and difficulty with gait that had been gradually worsening for 8 months . neurological examination revealed blurred vision , nystagmus with lateral gaze , and mild right facial weakness . computed tomography of the head without contrast revealed a high - density multilobular mass in the right cerebellopontine angle . magnetic resonance images demonstrated a trilobed solid mass that was in an extra - axial location . since the tumor was friable , gelatinous , vascular , and adherent to surrounding structures , it was hard to separate from cranial nerves . cranial

multipath signals occur in numerous microwave and radio frequency ( rf ) applications when an unwanted portion of the original transmission propagates along any alternate path and ultimately couples to the receiver distorting the amplitude and phase of the desired signal . the potential for interference from multipath signals increases substantially in near - field applications , especially in situations where the receiving and transmitting hardware are integrated . we are developing multichannel transceiving arrays for medical microwave imaging which exploit near field concepts to produce electrical property maps ( permittivity and conductivity ) of tissues of interest and have addressed the issue by incorporating ( a ) dedicated mixers for each channel , ( b ) additional solid state switches for isolation , ( c ) double- and triple - braided coaxial cables , and ( d ) compartmentalized rf circuitry . the implementation has proven effective for our application achieving channel / signal isolation greater than 130  db . in this study , we discuss the theoretical underpinnings of these modes for the geometrical geometries present in our current imaging system , where the results indicate that the desired signals from these modes are associated with multipath signal corruption .

objectivethis study aimed to analyze and assess the therapeutic effects of decompression for patients who underwent decompression followed by enucleation.materials and methodswe reviewed the charts of patients who visited the department of oral and maxillofacial surgery at chosun university dental hospital ( gwangju , korea ) and who had undergone decompression . we collected data on their age and gender , location and size of the lesions , preoperative and postoperative histopathological findings , gap in time between decompression and en nucleation , and variation in the size of lesions , complications , and recurrence according to the time gap . we measured the sizes of lesions in pre- and post - decompression panoramic photographs for each patient , measured width and length with a caliper , and multiplied them together to determine the lesion index ( li ) with the aim of simplifying measurement of irregularly sized lesions . to assess the extent of reaction to decompression , the difference between initial and final lis was divided by the decompression period to measure and analyze the reduction rate in each period.resultsthe mean age of the patients was 54.5 years ( range , 22 to 67 ) . the mean lis of the final and initial lis

backgroundfor the first time , the presented data suggest that a variant in the retn gene plays a significant role in acne pathogenesis . we also demonstrate an excess transmission of mutant allele g at retn-420 from 130 trio families using tdt analysis . therefore , we suggest that inherited variation in retn at -420 appears to be associated with heritable acne vulgaris .

many studies over the past 15 years have focused on discovering solvents that help promote efficient cc bond formation under benign reaction conditions . the aim of this work was to find a new solvent for dielsalder(2 ) and barbier reactions that would help speed up the one - pot transformations further , reducing synthetic steps and organic waste . in this article , we describe the discovery of a new solvent for the coupling of propargyl aldehydes and allyl halides under aqueous conditions to form enediyne and epoxydiyne skeletal structures . the solvent chosen was a water / dmf solvent system . the reaction proceeds more quickly ( 812 h ) in this system due to better solubility of all reagents , however , a mixture of 2 and 3 was isolated , with only 2 as the sole product . the solubilization of this mixture in water resulted in the formation of a mixture with water constituting up to 50% v / v of the solvent system , but the rate of product formation only slows to only 2 . we suggest that the increased polarity of the aprotic solvent could help to stabilize the anion formed by coupling 1 and 4 , thus allowing for a

background : acute kidney injury ( aki ) is a severe consequence of sepsis and multiple organ dysfunction syndrome ( mods ) , and about 1527% of aki in children is generated from pediatric septic aki . the aim of this study was to investigate the effects of baicalin adjunctive therapy in treating aki and to investigate its role in septic acute aki.materials and methods : a total of 50 pediatric sepsi patients ages 14 years were selected from the patients hospitalized from november 2013 to october 2015 . the 50 patients were randomly divided into 2 groups : control and baicalins , each group containing 25 patients . the patients in the control group received basic treatment , and those in the baicalinc group received treatment plus oral treatment . the renal function assessment from bun and serum creatinine ( cr ) were compared between the 2 groups.results:the results of our study showed that baicalicin significantly improved renal function in the children in both groups ( p < 0.001 ) . however , the effect was not statistically significant between the groups in terms of serum cr ( p = 0.002 ) . in the cecal ligation and puncture ( clp)-induced mouse se

glycer - derived ages ( glycer - ages ) have been shown to enhance the malignancy of cancer cells . however , the molecular mechanisms behind their effects have not been clarified . in the present study , we examined the effects of glycer- and glucose - derived glycation end - products on cultured human lung adenocarcinoma ( a549 ) and hepatocellular carcinoma ( hep3b ) cells . we found that the expression of n - acetyl - l - cysteine ( nac ) , a major nac - receptor binding protein , was enhanced in a549 cell culture medium supplemented with 25  mg / ml of glyceraldehyde ( ga ) and 5  mm diethylenetriamine - pentaacetic acid in phosphate buffer ( ph 7.4 ) . nac expression was also enhanced in hepatocells . moreover , a549 cells were more likely to develop malignancies than hepato - carcinoma cells when treated with bsa ( p < 0.001 ) , whereas hepato- and a549 - treated cells were less likely than control cells to develop metastases ( p > 0.05 ) . in addition , the expression levels of nac , a
