objective : the aim of the study was to demonstrate the accuracy and clinical precision of conventional surgical stent protocol for optimum installation of dental implants in terms of position and diameter.materials and methods : 45 patients ( 45 patients for whom 89 implants were placed at different sites ) who followed the standard inclusion criteria for implant supported prosthesis placement were included in the study . all the patients were treated in this study with the conventional stents ; all the implants were then placed to the desired depth as planned in virtual implant planning . the efficacy of stents was then evaluated at the time of surgical exposure for implant placement and after the surgery , with the help of radiographs.results:the position of the implants was determined by the use of computed tomography ( ct ) and computer added designing ( cad)/computer added machining ( cam ) technology . the stents were placed in the mouth and radiograph was taken to evaluate the position of gutta percha.conclusions:conventional surgical stents are more accurate and more precise than ct and cam stents in determining the position and the diameter of implants .

objectiveb cells play an important role in the initiation of -cell destruction by the autoimmune response . we hypothesized that an altered phenotype of b - cell subsets is associated with autoimmune diabetes.research design and methodsin this study , we investigated a change in the frequencies of peripheral b cells in patients with type 1 diabetes ( t1d ) , latent autoimmune diabetes in adults ( lada ) , and type 2 diabetes . two hundred fifty - eight patients ( n = 81 , mean diabetes duration 3.1  3.5 years ) , 82 patients with lada , and 95 patients with t2d were compared with 218 healthy control subjects . the results show altered frequencies of various b - cells in peripheral blood of patients with various subpopulations of b cells , including marginal zone b ( mzb ) , follicular b ( fob ) , cd19cd5cd1d , and cd4 + t cells , which have a regulatory function in infection , inflammation , and autoimmune disease.resultsthe frequency of mzb and fob b cells was significantly higher in t1D patients than in lada patients ( p < 0.001 ) . the frequency of cd4+ t cells was lower in t2D patients compared with controls (

human papillomavirus ( hpv ) , a virus from the papillomeirus family , is capable of infecting humans . about 200 different strains of hpv identified , based on dna homology , have been found to be etiologically linked to cervical , vaginal , vulvar , penile , anal , oral , and plantar infectious lesions and cancers , as well . among the existing 200 hpv strains , 150 have been sequenced as of now , and their data are available at the national center for biotechnology information ( ncbi ) . the hpv proteome database ( hpvpdb ) was developed as a relational reserve with a web interface , tools , and advanced search interfaces to obtain strain- and protein - specific information . hpv genome and proteome information can be precisely accessed through the web interface and the advanced search interface , respectively . the database also provides a phylogeny analysis tool to perform multiple sequence alignment and phylogenetic tree construction of selected hpv proteins using the original human papilomaviruses : a 's compilation of human and human papiruses ' human papilloma virus genome and human and papiloma virus 's human papirus proteome databases . additionally , the database platform also provides

purposethe aim of this study is to report the clinical results of acrysof   toric intraocular lens ( iol ) implantation for preexisting astigmatism correction and compare the postoperative rotation of the iols one and six months postoperatively.materials and methodsthis prospective study included eyes that had cataract surgery at the papageorgiou general hospital , thessaloniki , greece , between may 2008 and december 2008 . all patients had a complete ophthalmologic examination , including visual acuity ( va ) , slit - lamp examination , intraocular pressure ( iop ) measurement , and dilated fundus examination . a foldable iol was implanted in the capsular bag through a 2.75 mm limbal incision on 110.5 mm of ophthalmic viscosurgical device ( sodium hyaluronate ) and placed around 1015 axis axis . the iol axis was rotated to its final position by exactly aligning the toric reference corneal mark with the limbal implantation axis marks . because of this difficulty , an image was captured from the real - time recording of the surgery and was assessed six months after surgery.resultsthere were no significant

the arcopallium ( ra ) of adult zebra finches ( taeniopygia guttata ) is a robust brain region that controls the vocal and respiratory muscles . during song , ra neurons in adult birds generate a highly stereotyped sequence of bursts of bursts and also receive input from lateral magnocellular nucleus of the anterior nidopallum ( lman ) , the output nucleus of anterior forebrain pathway ( afp ) , which is necessary for song learning and plasticity . it has been observed that ra received excitatory glutamatergic inputs from hvc and lman . the former is mediated by a mixture of n - methyl - d - aspartate ( nmda ) and -amino-3-hydroxy-5-methyl-4-isoxazolepropionic - acid- ( ampa- ) type receptors , and the latter is mediated almost exclusively by nmda - type receptors . the lman - ra synaptic transmission is crucial for the variability of song in adult finch , suggesting that the activity of nmda receptor might induce the activity pattern change of ra synapses and change the bursts that control the vocal / respiratory muscle bursts . here , we test the gain change of

pythiosis is a life - threatening infection endemic to thailand , but rarely seen in the western hemisphere . here , we report a case of a 17-year - old jamaican male with a history of paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria and severe aplastic anemia refractory to horse anti - thymoglobulin treatment , which was transfusion - dependent . he presented with a classic case of vascular pythiosis with an antecedent history of exposure to surface water and vascular insufficiency , including an exquisitely tender and erythematous left inguinal mass . mri showed a t2 bright left groin mass with edema along the fascial planes , and lower extremity angiogram demonstrated occlusion of the left common femoral artery with distal profunda femoris and mid - superficial femoral arteries reconstitution . vascular repair was performed using a nonreversed left external iliac to superficial popliteal bypass graft complicated by pseudoaneurysm formation . intraoperative wound cultures were positive for pythium insidiosum and the patient was started on a combination of voriconazole , terbinafine , and amphoteric

objectivethe aim of this study was to investigate the effects of different amounts of radiation ( i.e. 0 , 2 , or 12 gy ) on the expression of genes in human oral keratinocytes and human fibroblasts.materials and methodswe used a three - dimensional tissue culture model ( 3d epioral ) in order to study the effect of different doses of radiation on the transcriptomic profiling of human keratinocyte and fibroblast cells.resultsthe total rna expression was significantly differentially expressed in the differentially treated tissues ( p - value < 0.05 ) . in addition , the expression levels of genes were significantly higher in the irradiated tissues than in the control tissue ( p-values > 0.5).conclusionin this study , we show that the differentiation of expression levels between differentially exposed and control tissues is due to the different amount of radiation used for the analysis of the rna profiles .

human immunodeficiency virus ( hiv ) infection among adolescents in sub - saharan africa is high , despite all efforts to prevent the disease . little is known about the prevalence of hiv in adolescents under 15 years of age , and adolescents are rarely tested for hiv unless they present in poor health or have recurrent infections . although a small number of randomized controlled trials have used biomarker data as outcomes in adolescent hiv prevention research , few studies have used these data to assess intervention effectiveness because of concerns about the difficulties in collecting and analyzing these data in resource - limited settings . moreover , many studies have relied on self - reported sexual behavior , which is subject to social desirability bias and likely to differ between study arms . to address the issue of feasibility , we present two case studies of prevention trials of adolescents in kenya and zimbabwe using biomarker testing and data collection . we describe the challenges faced by investigators in developing and implementing ethical procedures for informed consent , biomarker screening , and disclosure of test results . we also present the practical challenges of collecting biological samples and compare sample collection by blood spots relative to venipuncture . in addition , we examine difficulties with analyzing biological samples when the performance of assays in certain populations is unclear

background : decompressive - hemicraniectomy and duroplasty was performed in 5 toddlers and preschool children with non - traumatic , malignant refractory high intracranial pressure ( icp ) due to acute stroke . the aim of this study was to evaluate the functional and clinical outcome after decompressive craniectomy to control high icp in pediatric patient.materials and methods : between march 2004 and december 2006 , decompressive skull flap removal was performed on 5 children with high icps . all patients were evaluated with computed tomography ( ct ) and magnetic resonance image ( mri ) . in all patients , ct or mri was obtained immediately , after stabilization of ventilation and hemodynamicsat first.results:average age of the patients was 3.5 years ( range : 0 - 6 years ) . mean age of patients was 2.5 ( range 2 - 4 ) years . mean ct and mri were obtained immediately after stabilization and hemodynamic stabilization . mean arterial blood pressure ( bp ) of all patients was 0.7 ( range 0.5 - 1.5 ) and 0.8 ( range 1.0 - 2 ) , respectively . mean bp of patients with high bp

objectivealbuminuria is a common complication of type 2 diabetes mellitus ( type 2 ) , and furosemide ( aliskiren , irbesartan , and placebo ) has been shown to reduce albuminuria . the aim of this study was to compare the effects of these agents on 24-h blood pressure , glomerular filtration rate ( gfr ) , biomarkers , and raas components.research design and methodsthis was a double - blind , randomized , crossover trial in accordance with the declaration of helsinki and good clinical practice . from a list of 41 patients screened for study participation , twenty - six patients were randomly assigned to treatment with placebo ( n = 20 ) , 300 mg aliskirens ( n=20 ) , or 300 mg irbesartsins ( n < 20 ) or the combination of the two , in random order , for 2 months . serum albumin excretion rate ( uaer ) and gfr were measured at baseline and at the end of each treatment period.resultsthere were no significant differences in uaer between the three treatments ( p < 0.001 vs. placebo for all treatments ) . there was no significant difference in gfr between treatment with the combination (

nano- and microstructured biomaterials are used to enhance the biocompatibility of polymeric drug delivery systems , enhance the                 cytogenetic properties of bone - marrow - derived osteoblasts and osteoclasts , and improve the surface properties of orthopaedic implants . this review summarizes recent data of effects by nano- and                 glycerol - based nanomaterials and particles in vitro designed for orthopedic application to get a solid framework outlining the critical interactions that govern the                                                             cytograhmic interactions between cell - surface nanostructured surfaces and the osteo - surface structures of bone and bone marrow derived cells . the review focuses on the interactions between osteoblast and osteoclonus interactions with structured biomaterial surfaces .

mtt3.5 is a new gene that has the potential to play a key role in the regulation of gene regulation in many aspects of the human genome . it is the first genome - wide transcriptome that has been sequenced and annotated using a combination of bac and fosmid libraries . we describe the sequencing and analysis of this gene , which we describe here as the first example of a single - nucleotide polymorphism ( snp ) in the mammalian genome . we show that the snp is a highly conserved locus in the genome and that it can be expressed in a variety of cell types and tissues .

background : bipolar hip arthroplasty ( bha ) is indicated in the young individual in avascular necrosis ( avn ) with acetabular involvement ; however , its role is unclear.objectives:in this retrospective series , we have assessed the midterm outcome of bha in young adults ( age < 60 ) with ficat stages 3 and 4 avn.patients and methods : a retrospective study with a prospective followup was planned . 80 consecutive patients below 60 years of age ( 96 hips ) were operated by the senior author between 1995 and 2010 using bha with tight fitting cup technique . all patients were males with a mean age of 42  6.44 years ( range 30 - 59 years ) . out of a total of 96 hips , 76 were diagnosed as idiopathic avn , 8 were posttraumatic avn while 12 were post alcohol consumption avn . all the 96 hips were found to have mild to moderate acetabulae involvement . the bha was done in the posterolateral position under epidural anesthesia . the hip joint capsule was excised if diseased.results:in our study , intraoperatively on gross examination , all of the 96 hip ( fic at stage 3 and4 ) were found

valproic acid ( vpa ) is a short branched chain fatty acid used in the therapy of absence seizures , partial seizures , tonic - clonic seizures , bipolar disorder , schizoaffective disorder , social phobias , neuropathic pain and for the prophylaxis and treatment of migraine headaches . the use of vpa in the treatment of encephalopathy as a side - effect of depakote treatment remains controversial . the present case report is about a 25-year - old hispanic female with a history of depression since age of 12 years , borderline personality disorder , purging type bulimia and history of suicide attempts at age 12 was admitted to the intensive care unit after attempted suicide by consuming an unknown number of vPA pills . this patient had recently started taking vpa as a mood stabilizer at a dose of 250 mg at bed time about 3 days earlier , after a failed therapy . the patient was taking paroxetine 20 mg for depression ; this was incremented to 40 mg at the time vpa was started . she reported that the change to vpa made her listless ,  lowered  her mood , decreased  her motivation , worsened her depression and caused suicidal thoughts . she overdoses herself on vpa

abstractintramammary infection ( mastitis ) is the most common reason for the use of antimicrobials in dairy cows . consensus about the most efficient , safe , and economical treatment is still lacking . the aim of this article is to review current treatments of mastitis during lactation and seek for evidence - based , best practice treatment recommendations for bovine mastitis . mastitis caused by streptococci , coagulase - negative staphylococci and corynebacterium spp . has been treated intramammarily ( imm ) for more than 50 years , but resistance to penicillin and lincosamides has been detected . antimicrobial susceptibility of coliform bacteria varies but normally is not a limiting factor for therapy . the systemic route of administration has been suggested to be more efficient than imm for the treatment of clinical mastitis , but it is difficult to attain and maintain therapeutic concentrations in milk or udder tissue following systemic administration . many commonly used broad - spectrum antimicrobial preparations such as oxytetracycline , trimethoprim - sulphonamide and ceftiofurfur , have shown reasonable efficacy . the impact on public health  is discussed .

human immunodeficiency virus ( hiv ) is the most prevalent infectious disease in the world , with approximately 40 million people infected worldwide . initially , there were concerns that hiv might be vector - borne ( e.g. , transmitted via mosquitoes ) but it has since become widely accepted that such transmission does not occur at any significant level . given the current characteristics of hiv , these three features of the virus make it unlikely that vector transmission will occur . therefore , we are interested in understanding why hiv has evolved these particular features and not others instead . for example , why has hiv not evolved a replication strategy that results in viral concentrations high enough to allow vector transmission ? ( i ) why has the virus not evolved to be more durable and thus to survive longer outside of humans ? ( ii ) why have hiv - infected individuals not evolved the ability to replicate in arthropod vectors ? ( iii ) why does hiv have not evolved an ability to survive long enough to replicate within arthropods vectors ? in this article , we approach this question from an evolutionary perspective . in particular , we focus on the lack of vector transmission in hiv because of the profound significance of the epidemiological significance of its absence .

objectivein the normal kidney , immunohistochemical expression of contractile protein alpha - smooth muscle actin ( asma ) is limited to the vascular smooth muscle cells . in pathological conditions , the expression of asma in interstitial myofibroblasts helps to differentiate between minimal change disease and the early phase of idiopathic membranous glomerulonephritis ( gn ) . in patients with iga nephropathy , higher mesangial asma expression predicts a progressive decline in renal function . to our best knowledge , this is the first morphometrical study of the correlation between asma and renal function at the time of biopsy.materials and methodsin this retrospective study , 142 patients  82 adults and 60 children who underwent percutaneous renal biopsy in the department of internal medicine and department of pediatrics , university hospital , split , croatia in the period from 1994 to 2007 were analyzed . the absolute values of blood pressure ( bp ) , serum creatinine ( sc ) , 24-hour urine protein ( ccr ) and 24-hr urine protein , measured within 7 days before the biopsy , were collected from the hospital records . computer - assisted morphometric analysis was used to

chondroblastoma is a relatively rare bone tumor , accounting for < 1% of all primary bone tumors . it is difficult to differentiate it from other giant cell - rich lesions , as it exhibits predominantly benign behavior , though recurrences and metastasis are on record . fine needle aspiration cytology ( fnac ) has evolved as a simple , safe , and noninvasive preoperative tool in the diagnosis of bone tumors , and it has been widely used in the last decade . we report cytomorphology in two cases of chondroblasmoma confirmed by histopathology with a brief review of literature .

backgroundsuicide is a grave public health issue with a global mortality rate of 16 per 100,000 individuals . the objective of this study was to investigate whether parental bonding is associated with attitudes toward suicide among medical college students in japan.material/methodsthe parental bonding instrument ( pbi ) was administered to 226 medical students to measure the attitudes and behavior of the parents . the japanese version of the attitude toward suicide questionnaire ( atts ) was used to assess the participants 's attitudes to suicide.resultsthe pbi scores of parents were significantly higher than those of their children ( p<0.001 ) . the atts scores of students were significantly lower for parents who were more caring and/or protective than those who were less caring and / or protective ( p=0.002 ) . among medical students , the pbi score of parents who are more protective of children is significantly lower than that of parents whose parents are more caring or overprotective ( p>0.05).conclusionthis study suggests that parental bonding plays an important role in shaping the developmental trajectory of an individual , including his / her ability to adjust to stressful events .

backgroundcopd is characterized by persistent airway obstruction , usually of a progressive nature and associated with an enhanced chronic inflammatory response in airways to noxious particles or gases . the aim of this study is to investigate the adherence rates to gold 2010 recommendations for stable copd treatment by pulmonary physicians with a national survey in outpatient clinics of turkish pulmonary units from different regions of the country.material/methodsthis multi - center , cross - sectional study was conducted with the participation of eleven centers ( university hospitals , research and training hospitals , state hospitals , and private hospitals ) from eight cities ( adana , ankara , diyarbakir , erzurum , istanbul , izmir , kocaeli , and samsun ) across turkey . the study was approved by the institutional ethics committee of ankara university school of medicine ( november 21 , 2010 ; # 24,596 ) prior to its initiation and conducted in accordance with the latest version of declaration of helsinki and international conference of harmonization / good clinical practice guideline . the inclusion criteria included doctor s diagnosis of copd , age 40 years , available pulmonary function test ( pft ) results from within the past 2 years and copd data from any time

backgroundamphotericin b ( amb ) , an amphoteric polyene macrolide , has been considered one of the main options of antifungal therapy for serious and life - threatening mycotic infections for over 50 years despite its high safety profile . although it is a common event in clinical settings , different aspects of amb nephrotoxicity have not been studied well or in detail in our population . the purpose of this investigation was to specifically assess the frequency , time onset , and possible associated factors of amb - induced neephrotoxicity in hospitalized patients in hematology - oncology wards in the southwest of iran.methods and resultsa cross - sectional , observational study was performed over a period of 9 months from august 2015 to april 2016 in 2 hematological and 1 hematopoietic stem cell transplantation wards of namazi hospital , which is affiliated to shiraz university of medical sciences , shirazi , iran . the study population consisted of patients aged 15 years or older with no documented history of acute kidney injury defined by an increase in serum creatinine  0.3 mg / dl within 48 hours , or an increase  1.5 times the baseline in

o - glycans have been found to play an important role in protein stability and tertiary structure , stabilize protein conformation , modulate the activity of enzymes ( e.g. by reversible attachment of o - linked glcnac to cytoplasmic and nuclear proteins ) and signaling molecules , are essential for a number of recognition processes and are sorting determinants guiding the modified protein in the cell from the place of biosynthesis to its target location . in the present study we determine the main o - glycan structures in snails and characterize a common core - trisaccharide . the glycosylation potential of snails is important in general , because some of them are intermediate hosts of pathogens ( biomphalaria glabrata and lymnaea stagnalis ) or a pest in agriculture . here we present for the first time an overview on o-glycan structures of some species of the mollusk branch of the evolutionary tree .

objectiveadiponectin ( arcp 30 , adipoq , apm1 or gbp28 ) , secreted by adipose tissue , is a 247-amino acid peptide which was discovered in 1995 . recent findings suggest that high levels of this peptide are an independent predictor of mortality in chronic heart failure patients , chronic kidney patients , and elderly patients , who are at high risk for cardiovascular events.research design and methodsthe study population consisted of 82 consecutive patients who underwent coronary angiography for suspected or known coronary atherosclerosis at the university clinical center of kosovo . with the exception of acute coronary syndromes , the patients had to present in a stable clinical condition without major concomitant non - cardiovascular disease . the study protocol was approved by the ethical committee of the faculty of medicine , university of prishtina , and research was conducted in accordance with declaration of helsinki guidelines . blood pressure was measured by the european society of hypertension - international hypertension - hypertension protocol 2 ( ip2 ) . the 12-h fasting blood samples were taken in the morning and the sera were stored in the serum . the gensini score was calculated as the sum of the scores for all three

neurofibromatosis type - i ( nf-1 ) , also known as von recklinghausen syndrome , is caused by the mutation of a gene on the long arm of chromosome 17 , which encodes a protein known as neurofibromain . it is characterized by spots of increased skin pigmentation ( caf au lait spots ) , combined with peripheral nerve tumors and a variety of other dysplastic abnormalities of the skin , nervous system , bones , endocrine organs , and blood vessels . the localized form of nf - i , known as segmental neurofibia , is very rare . we report a case of a 26-year - old female patient who presented to the dental department complaining about the unesthetic appearance of her face , since eight years . on examination , the face of the patient appeared asymmetrical , bilaterally , with a growth on the right side of the face . there were numerous small and large , sessile and pedunculated nodular growths localized on the left side of her neck , with dark - colored underlying skin . the skin over the right cheek and chin area showed coffee - colored pigmentation . the right ear showed disfigurement and hypertrophy , with thickened cart

introductionthe zygomaticomaxillary complex is an important landmark of the midface , and it provides prominent cheek structure . however , unfortunately , unfortunately it is very vulnerable to injury because of its intrinsically prominent convexity . there are four bony attachments between zygoma and other facial bones : a superior attachment to the frontal bone ( frontozygomatic suture line ) , a medial attachment to maxilla , a lateral attachment to temporal bone , a deep attachment to sphenoid bone and a deep suture attachment to zygomaxilla . when blunt trauma results in fractures of all four suture lines , it is referred to as a tetrapod fracture . open reduction without surgical incisions has virtually been abandoned because judgment on the adequate reduction of fracture could not been made and it often resulted in disappointing cosmetic results . the more popular dingman 's approach has advantages in that it leaves no facial scars and is simple to perform . uably however , the fracture site is not directly visualized and access to the orbit is not possible , which may result in incomplete surgical reduction . since its first description , the transconjunctival approach has been described for the removal of herniated orbital fat , and there have been subsequent reports demonstrating

papurella multocida is a gram - negative coccobacillus that is normally found in the oral cavity of dogs and cats . it is a recognised cause of wound infection as a result of bites , licking or scratching . endocarditis is a rare complication of this bacterium and , from literature reviews , is found to be associated with prior animal trauma , a second source of infection ( e.g. cellulitis ) and high mortality rates . we herein report a case in which a 38-year - old man was admitted to our hospital with a 2 days history of fever , malaise and cellulitis around a mitrofanoff abdominal catheter . the patient was documented to be penicillin allergic ( previous skin rash ) , so was initiated on ertapenem iv 1 gr / od . transesophageal echocardiography revealed the presence of vegetation ( 0.57 0.2 cm ) above the atrium of the right coronary artery . laboratory isolation and identification of the isolates was performed using standard microbiological techniques and confirmed with 16s rrna sequencing and matrix - assisted laser desorption / ionization time - of - flight mass spectrometry ( maldi - tof

we present a case study of the development of a novel novel in the form of a short story in which we present the case of a young boy who was diagnosed with dyslexia and his mother who was also dyslexic . the young boy was unable to read or write and had to rely on the help of his mother to read and understand his work . his mother had to teach him how to read so that he would be able to understand what was going on in his head . the boy was also taught how to write and how to communicate with others . the aim of this study was to develop a novel in a young adult novel in order to test the limits of his knowledge of the literature and to show that he could read and communicate with other young people in a safe and secure environment . the main goal of the study is to provide a framework for the young person to understand the literature , the art of writing and the science of writing . the second goal is to understand how to use the literature to create a novel that can be used in the field of literature and art for the benefit of the young people and the general public . the third goal is the study of how to make the novel into a novel so that it can be read and understood by

purposethe purpose of this study was to compare parametric perfusable tissue index ( pti ) images derived from [ o]h2o pet / ct with late gadolinium - enhanced cardiovascular magnetic resonance ( lge - cmr ) imaging , an established method for quantifying scar size and a marker of myocardial viability.materials and methodsthe study population comprised 46 patients with documented or suspected coronary artery disease who had been studied using both lge-cmr and a single [ o]'s [ o2o ] scan within a 2-month period . parametric pti images of arterial and venous blood volume fractions were subtracted from normalized ct images , resulting in anatomic tissue fraction ( ptf ) and arterial ( ca(t ) and ventricle ( crv ) volume fractions . the ptf was calculated using a single - tissue compartment model for the ascending aorta using a three - dimensional row action maximum likelihood algorithm.resultsthe ptf of the lge cmr scan was significantly larger than that of the [ o'2o scan ( p<0.001 ) . the crv fraction was significantly smaller ( p=0.002 ) and the arterial volume

introductions of populations at low density and/or small size are often faced with inverse density dependent effects , attributed to demographic stochasticity or reduced cooperative interactions . this phenomenon is known as the allee effect . it is the case that introduced individuals may be maladapted to small population sizes where their survival and reproductive ability are significantly impacted , and these impacts on individual fitness combine to produce an overall decrease in abundance ( i.e. , demographic allee effects ) . the underlying traits that influence these effects may be subject to natural selection , but it is difficult to decipher the exact mechanism that manifests these effects . in this review , we focus our attention on population level demographic allees , with the underlying assumption that a component allees effect led to the demographic alleys effect .

chlamydia trachomatis infection ( cti ) is one of the most prevalent bacterial sexually transmitted infections ( stis ) . the available indian data show a wide variation in ct prevalence with infection rates in indian women ranging from 3.3% to 33% depending on the population sampled . the primary objective of this study was to establish the need for screening hiv seropositive women for cti . the study was conducted between july 2010 and january 2011 at the molecular department of microbiology , hiv / aids , maulana medical college , new delhi , india . real - time polymerase chain reaction ( pcr ) was used to detect cti in hiv - infected and hiv- uninfected women . the prevalence of cti was detected by using different diagnostic modalities like serology , culture method , elisa for antigen and antibody , direct fluorescence assay ( dfa ) and nucleic acid amplification tests ( naats ) but none of them are 100% sensitive . the pcr was the most sensitive and specific test available currently for diagnosing genital chlamydia infection .

objective : the aim of this study was to evaluate the efficacy and safety of tranexamic acid ( txa ) in controlling blood loss during bone grafting ( clf ) for the treatment of multilevel cervical spondylotic myelopathy ( mcsm).materials and methods : between november 2014 and april 2016 , 119 clfs fitting the inclusion criteria were performed in our center . the control group consisted of 46 patients who underwent clf for mcsm in our institution without being given txa . in both groups , we analyzed patient demographic trait ( age at surgery , gender , body weight , height , and body mass index ) and duration of surgery which was defined as the time from the initial incision to the completion of wound closure . in addition , the quantity of intraoperative and postoperative blood loss and the preoperative and day 1 postoperative hematological data for each patient were obtained in the 2 groups . retrospective comparative analysis was performed in patients with mcsm undergoing clf of vertebrae c3 to c6 . blood loss was calculated as the quantity during the 1st and 1st postoperative 1st 16  hours.results:in the clf group , there were no significant differences in the

introduction . hip fracture is a moderate musculoskeletal trauma that mainly affects the older population with comorbid conditions . myocardial injury may be difficult to diagnose because of impaired communication , limitations of clinical manifestation and non - specific electrocardiographic ( ecg ) changes . the isoenzyme myocardium - specific creatine kinase ( ck - mb ) and troponin t ( tnt ) have traditionally been analysed in plasma to distinguish between cardiac and skeletal muscle injury . in this study we wanted to test the hypothesis that high plasma troponins have an increased risk of fatal outcome following hip fracture . methods . a total of 302 consecutive patients over 75 years of age with dislocated hip fractures were enrolled in the study at elverum ( ech ) and buskerud ( bch ) central hospitals during the period 20052009 . a hemiprosthesis was inserted through a lateral incision and fixed with or without bone cement ( landos titan or landos corail , depuy , warsaw , in , usa ) . blood samples were collected preoperatively ( 1 ) and postoperatively [ within 24  hours ( 0 ) ] and on days one [ 2448  h ( + 1 )

acute retinal necrosis ( arn ) is one of the clinical presentations of herpes simplex virus ( hsv ) . it may present several years after a primary infection , or it may occur following systemic herpetic infection . arn may be associated with optic neuropathy , scleritis , and pain . in most patients , the diagnosis is made clinically . in this article , we report one case of unilateral arn 1 month after herpetetic encephalitis . the patient is a 25-year - old man who was admitted with a chief complaint of decreased vision and pain in left eye for the previous 4 days . he had multiple foci of retinitis in the retinal periphery associated with arteriolar involvement ( arteriolitis ) , blot retinal hemorrhage and optic disc hyperemia . brain mri and polymerase chain reaction analysis of cerebrospinal fluid revealed hsv-2 , so the patient was diagnosed with arn and received intravenous acyclovir . after 48 h of antiviral therapy , a systemic corticosteroid ( prednisolone 1 mg / kg / day ) was introduced and subsequently tapered over several weeks to treat active inflammation . prophactic barrier laser phot

rhomboid family proteins are a family of serine serine proteases that are represented in most of the sequenced bacterial , archaeal and eukaryotic genomes . the prototype of this family is the rhomboid ( rho ) protein from drosophila melanogaster , a developmental regulator involved in epidermal growth factor ( egf)-dependent signaling pathways . recently , rho was shown to cleave a membrane - bound precursor of the tgf-like growth factor spitz , enabling the released spitz to activate the egf receptor , and that a conserved serine and histidine in rho are essential for this cleavage . the newly discovered intramembrane proteolytic activity of rho places it within the framework of regulated intramenilin proteolysis ( rip ) , a new paradigm of signal transduction , which appears to be prominent in all forms of life .

in the mid - 1970s , i was the associate director of the adult sickle cell center at thomas jefferson university . the steady stream of patients with acute painful vaso - occlusive crises ( vocs ) to the emergency department ( ed ) and hospital were not welcome by most providers , hospital administration , the house and nursing staffs . accordingly , i issued an identification wallet - sized , plasticized card that was carried by patients and presented to the provider treating their voc in the ed , hospital or any other medical facility . information printed on both sides of the card included demographic data and a recent photograph ; hematological data including reticulocyte count ; medical data including the type of scd , its complications and co - morbidities if present ; all medications being taken by the patient and the recommended treatment of vocs including the name , dose , and the route of administration of the analgesics in question ; and my name and contact information for answering questions if needed . a polaroid camera available at that time and a laminator were the only equipments needed to issue these cards . later on , information on the card was computerized and a printed copy was given to the patient . the patients were very compliant in carrying it

lipid nanoparticles ( lnps ) are currently under clinical evaluation for hepatic gene silencing . however , delivery to extra - hepatic cells is challenging . in particular , immune cells such as cd4 + t cells are relatively resistant to uptake in vitro and in vivo . here we demonstrate the ability of a modified lnp delivery platform to modulate cd8 t - cell responses using a single - chain c - type lectin - specific antibody ( scf ) for the dc205 receptor . using this approach , we assessed the ability to knock down gene expression in cd8 + t - cells by targeting specific genes expressed in cd4+ t cells with a single lnp coated with specific scf for dec205 , containing sirnas . we demonstrate that the lnp - coated scf - specific dec205 dna delivery platform can modulate the expression of genes expressed by cd8+ t- and cd6 + t-cells . this approach has the potential to be a powerful tool for modulating immune responses and could be used as a vaccine component for a variety of diseases .

sevoflurane anaesthesia has been used for the treatment of cardiac arrhythmias in the past . despite all the research demonstrating its safety , adverse cardiac events have been reported in patients under its anaesthesia . we report two cases of isorhythmic atrioventricular dissociation ( isravd ) in children and a 60-year - old woman undergoing elective modified radical mastectomy . the first case was a 5-year old boy undergoing left inguinal hernia repair . the second case was an adult woman undergoing surgery for breast cancer . both patients were treated with sevofilurane and the postoperative period was uneventful .

in english common law relating to mental capacity , it is important not to confuse the question of mental capacity with the nature of the decision made by the patient , however grave the consequences . the aim of the capacity test is to assess whether a decision can be explained in terms of the patient 's particular motivating commitments ( their desires , values , projects , ideas about a good life ) ; or whether the decision is properly explained as a problem in decision - making , which is due to a dysfunction of mind or brain . in practice then , questions about mental capacity might be thought of as boiling down to a question about if a decision is understandable in the light of their particular commitments , or is the decision understandable only if it is explained in the context of a problem that reflects a difference in values rather than an absence of competence . this raises moral concerns because the possibility that a choice could be explained by idiosyncratic commitments plays a significant role in protecting the patient's right to make controversial decisions . the most straightforward approach to conceiving of commitments , and the one most likely to protect this right , is to say that the person 's commitments are their current commitments  those they recognize at the time of making the decision . however , this approach fails to recognise certain problems of practical

type iii dysbetalipoproteinaemia is a lipid metabolism disorder caused by apo - e deficiency , which leads to accumulation of chylomicrons and very low density lipoprotein remnants in the plasma . lipid metabolism disorders are mostly manifested by xanthomas , which are known as yellowish cholesterol - rich material in large foam cells accumulating in the skins and tendons . these yellowish lesions may appear all over the body , on the palm of the hands , sole of the foot , tendons , and even on the eyelids . these lesions firstly described with or without hyperlipidaemia in association with monoclonal immunoglobulin . based on this fact , there are three forms of xanthoma : hyperlipaemic , normolipaemic and necrobiotic xanthogranuloma . we present a case of a 43-year - old man who presented with xanthomatosis on the lower extremities , which was diagnosed as familial dysbetaemic xanthomyxanthoma ( fdl ) . although siblings of this case had the same disorder , they did not develop such lesions . therefore , we decided to present this case without revealing patient 's name , after taking his consent .

objectiveto investigate whether previous moderate - to - severe exacerbations are associated with future exacerbations in japanese patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease ( copd).research design and methodswe conducted a 1-year prospective observational trial in a daily - life setting involving 90 patients with copd . the patients were divided into three groups based on the total number of moderate and severe episodes within the last year , i.e., non- ( 0/year ) , infrequent ( one exacerbation / year ) , and frequent ( two or more exacerbations / year) exacerbator groups . clinical parameters included age , sex , body mass index , smoking habits , smoking index , comorbidities , duration of copd , 5-grade modified medical research council ( mmrc ) dyspnea scale score , total copd assessment test ( cat ) score , frequency scale for symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease ( fssg ) , center for epidemiologic studies depression ( cesd ) scale , medications , blood pressure and heart rate , lung function and blood parameters , and chest computed tomography.resultsthe mean age of the patients in the non- and infrequent exacerbation groups was 58.5 and 57.5

this is the first of the new wave of the modern era of music and culture to be released in the form of a new generation of electronic music and video games . it is a new era in music and visual culture , and it is not just about the music and the video games , it is also about the culture and the people who make this music and this culture . this new era is a time of great change in the way we think and feel about music and art . this is the era of new music and new culture that is not only about music but also about people and places . this era is also a time for people to discover new ways of expressing themselves and the way they feel about the world and the world around them . this article is one of the first in a series of articles that will be published in the next few weeks . the first article will be about the birth of new wave music and its influence on the modern music scene . the second article is about the evolution of modern music , and the third article is an overview of the current state of the art of music . the fourth article is a review of the history of music in this era and the influence of music on the culture of this era . the fifth article focuses on the evolution

objectivethis study examined observer variation in the subclassification of follicular neoplasms ( fn ) among 4 thyroid experts to validate the usefulness and limitations of this characteristic risk stratification of fn recommended in the japanese thyroid association ( jta ) guidelines.materials and methodsthe present study is a reproducibility study undertaken by 4 reviewers ( kennichi kakudo , kaori kameyama , mitsuyoshi hirokawa , and ryohei katoh ) who have special interest in thyroid pathology , all of whom are members of the clinical guideline committee of the jta . they were requested to subclassify fn following the new jta reporting system of thyroid cytology . twenty cases of cytological smear samples with follicular patterned lesions were randomly selected by one of the authors and they were further analyzed for subclassifications of fn and diagnoses by the 4 reviewers.resultsthe 4 reviewers examined cytological samples independently without exchanging opinions and were unaware of the original cytological diagnoses and final histologic diagnoses . there were 48 benign diagnoses , including 29 follicular adenomas ( fas ) , 19 adenomatous nodules ( an ) , and 43 malignant diagnoses including 13 follicular carcinomas ( ft

backgroundacquired hemophilia is a rare but life - threatening cause of unexplained recent onset or acute bleeding associated with a prolonged partial thromboplastin time ( aptt ) . a survey was conducted of physicians across a number of specialties to identify potential barriers to the effective recognition and management of this rare but important cause of serious bleeding.methodswe conducted a cross - sectional survey of physicians practicing in the united states to identify factors that hinder effective recognition , management , and follow - up of patients with coagulopathic bleeding . the survey was based on an actual case of bleeding that was found to be the result of acquired hemophiliac disease and focused on stepwise evaluation and management.resultsa total of 909 physicians participated in the survey . of these , 788 ( 52.5% ) were identified as having experienced a patient presenting to the hospital with a recent onset of bleeding , of which 588 ( 42.7% ) had experienced a case of acute bleeding with an aptt greater than 1 . the most common type of bleeding was postpartum hemorrhage , followed by menorrhagia , acute myocardial infarction , trauma , and surgery . a significant proportion of the respondents had a history

plant cells need to maintain the functional integrity of their walls during cell morphogenesis and exposure to biotic / abiotic stress . the available evidence suggests that a dedicated plant cell wall integrity ( cwi ) maintenance mechanism exists . while our understanding of the mechanisms regulating stress responses and morphogenesis has increased significantly , our knowledge regarding the processes maintaining cwi is still limited . in this review , the available knowledge regarding cwi maintenance during plant development and in yeast will be covered here only briefly to provide a conceptual framework regarding cellular processes involved and to illustrate the degree of functional conservation between species .

backgroundgenomic dna hypomethylation has been observed in the peripheral blood mononuclear cells of leukemia patients and in tumor cells of patients with b - cell lymphoproliferative diseases . however , the differential factors through which they produce a different type of leukemia are not yet completely understood . in order to gain insight into the differential epigenetic alterations in leukemia , we investigated the methylation statuses at selected locus of these genes using a methylation - specific polymerase chain reaction ( msp ) in patients with acute myeloid leukemia ( aml ) and chronic myeloids leukemia ( cml).material/methodsthe msp was performed on 23 patients with aml and 21 patients with cml . as controls , 22 normal peripheral bloods were obtained from 22 healthy volunteers ( 11 males and 10 females ) ranging from 20 to 78 yr of age . the msp results were analyzed as a dichotomous variable based on the presence or absence of methylation.resultsthe methylation status of dna methylation was significantly higher in aml patients than in cml patients ( p < 0.001 ) . the methylated dna was significantly lower at the sites of dlc-1 , dap kinase

patellar dislocations with vertical axis rotation following dislocation are extremely rare . we report a case of a 32-year old gentleman who presented with an irreducible patellar dislocation following an unusual atraumatic mechanism . following attempts at closed reduction under sedation and regional nerve block , eventual open reduction and soft tissue reconstruction was required under general anesthetic . during the open reduction procedure , it was noted that the patella had dislocated into a lateral extra - articular position and rotated around its vertical axis . the patient was subsequently taken to theatre where one final attempt of closed reduction was carried out under general anaesthetic and muscle relaxation . ultimately , this failed and an open reduction was performed . complete rupture of the medial patellofemoral ligament ( mpfl ) was identified and repaired . after 3 months follow - up , the patient had no further episodes of dislocation , full range of knee extension and flexion , and normal tracking .

graphene oxide ( go ) solution was synthesized from natural graphite powder by a modification of hummers method . cu2o / rgo composites were fabricated by a microwave - assisted hydrothermal reaction . after stirring for 2 h , the homogeneous solution was heated with stirring in the microwave system at 150 c for 3 h . after the product was cooled to rt , the final product was collected by centrifugation , washed with water five times , and dried at 70 c . the morphologies of the products were characterized by field - emission scanning electron microscopy ( fesem ) and tem ( jeol-2010f ) .                  the co2 reduction reaction was performed in batches by using a septum - sealed glass chamber with a volume of 120 ml , which was heated at 160 for 1 h prior to measurement . to remove possible trace organic contaminants ,                  a typical photocatalytic experiment was conducted by using 0.5 g of photocatalyst and 3 ml of deionized water in a co2-purged 120 ml reactor . the reaction product was monitored by periodical sampling of the gas phase from the glass chamber and analyzed by gc ( varian gc-450 ) .

objectivethe aim of this study was to assess the possibility of the clinical application of the diagnosis of occlusal caries using digital imaging by examining the sensitivity , the specificity , and the accuracy in comparison with the diagnodent values and the dentists ' diagnoses.materials and methodsa total of one hundred teeth ( 36 premolars and 64 molars ) with pit - and - fissure discoloration from 19 outpatients were examined at the clinic of oral diagnosis and general dentistry , dental hospital , tokyo medical and dental university . dentists preliminarily diagnosed each tooth using visual inspection and tactile examination to decide which treatment plan would be appropriate ( preventive or operative ) . the mean scores of the teeth were used as the diagnostic values ( dd ) . then , each tooth was photographed as large as possible with an intraoral digital camera ( penscope , morita , japan).resultsthe mean scores were 0.62 by visual inspection , 0.82 by probing , and 0.81 by diagnoden . the proportion of the area of pit - fission discolored teeth to the area obtained by digital imaging was significantly correlated with the depth of the lesion and the diagnostic value ( p < 0.001

craniofacial defects are rare , disfiguring facial anomalies , with an incidence of about 1.4 to 4.9 cases per 100,000 live births . median clefts of the upper lip , or tessier 0 class , have a variety of presentations ranging from a minimal notching of the lip , vermilion , and nose to a wide cleft that divides craniof facial structures . the median cleft can be classified into true and false . true clefts are due to failure of the medial nasal process to fuse . false clefts occur when the two medial nasal processes fail to fuse at midline . we report using a pfeifer incision to correct a moderate true median clect involving the white roll in a five - year - old boy . a diamond incision was made over the vermillion and the labial mucosa , intra - orally extending just beyond the cleft margin . subsequently , a wavy incision line was made starting from the deepest point and extending over the philtrum . the cupid 's bow was properly aligned , with equal height on both sides , and the fullness and continuity of the orbicularis oris were maintained .

objective . to develop a simple laboratory - free risk score to identify people with undiagnosed diabetes and incident diabetes in peru , a latin american country that spans coastal , andean , and rainforest settings . methods . the national survey of nutritional and biochemical indicators for noncommunicable diseases ( eninbsc in spanish ) was used to develop our predictive model . this was complemented with the cronicas cohort study , whose baseline and longitudinal information was used for external validation . results . the prevalence of diabetes mellitus and diabetes - related hospitalizations in the study area was 1.1% and 1.6% , respectively . the risk score was based on the national prevalence of hypertension and diabetes in the crony cohort study ( p < 0.001 ) . the adjusted hazard ratio ( hr ) was 0.93 ( 95% confidence interval [ ci ] : 0.96 to 1.01 ) . a total of 1.2 million people were enrolled in the national study area . the mean age of the study population was 35.5 years . the rate of diabetes was 2.2% and the incidence of hospitalizations was 1% . the overall risk score did not differ significantly between the study areas . conclusion . the

backgroundcancer is one of the leading causes of death worldwide and accounted for 7.6 million cases ( 13% of all deaths ) in 2008 ; in 2013 in the united states there are 12,340 estimated new cases and 4,030 deaths by cervical cancer.objectivesthe present study was designed to investigate the potential use of p53sirna to block p53 expression , as well as the subsequent effect on cell invasion and gene expression on the human cervical cancer cell.material/methods510 hela cells were used for reverse - transfection of 50 nm p53 sirna using siport neofx as transfector agent ( ambion , usa ) . at 24 hours post treatment , the total rna for the cells treated and the control group respectively was extracted using trireagent ( sigma - aldrich , bucharest romania ) based on the producer recommended protocol for each triplicate specimen . the 8 genes selected were assessed using specific primer and upl based on in silico design . the data analysis was carried out to compare the gene expression values for the treated and untreated groups using ct gene expression method.resultsthe treatment group showed significantly higher gene expression levels at 48 hours post - treatment

background : chlamydophila pneumoniae is an obligatory intracellular organism , and is responsible for at least 10% of community - acquired pneumonias . the aim of this study was to demonstrate whether there is any difference between two groups of patients with coronary atherosclerotic plaque and subjects without atherosclerosis for the presence of c. pneumoniae dna by polymerase chain reaction ( pcr ) method.materials and methods : this case - control study was carried out on 60 formalin - fixed paraffin - embedded ( ffpe ) coronary artery biopsies in the molecular pathology laboratory , ghaem hospital , mashhad , iran in 2010 . the study group consisted of 30 coronary artery tissues with atherosclercotic plaques ( study group ) , and the control group comprised 30 coronary arteries without atherogenic plaque ( control group ) . dna extraction was performed using pcr kits ( technology ( jsc ) . the dna content was determined by using the thermo scientific nanodrop 2000 spectrophotometer , and specimens with low dna concentration ( < 20 ng/l ) were excluded from the study.results:there was no significant difference between the two groups except for positive and negative c.

background : the aim of this study was to identify the spiritual health of patients with defects caused by diabetes.materials and methods : this was a qualitative - phenomenological and descriptive study and the participants were selected from rehabilitation centers in isfahan and valiasr hospital in zanjanjan . the study population consisted of 15 participants ( 8 men and 7 women at 50 years of age ) . all interviews were fully recorded and all participants were given a code based on the interviews.results:the concepts that patients had experienced as hindering factors of the treatment process were disappointment and hopelessness , guilt , feeling distant from god , quitting obligatory acts and knowing god as cruel.conclusion:although the mortality rate in diabetic patients due to ketoacidosis and infection is declining , deaths from diabetes complications including diabetic foot complications has increased dramatically . the importance of spiritual and religious care as one of the tasks of nurses , as the key members of health team , they should respect the patients beliefs and values in addition to considering their physical and mental conditions .

objectiveto examine the relationship between vitamin d status and parameters of insulin resistance and sensitivity in asian - indian children and adolescents.research design and methodsthe study was a prospective observational study . subjects were selected from the children between the ages of 617 years and adolescents attending the endocrinology outdoor services at the all india institute of medical sciences ( aiims ) , new delhi . blood samples were collected in fasting state ( minimum 9 hours fasting ) , followed by oral glucose tolerance test ( anhydrous glucose = 1.75  gm / kg bodyweight , maximum of 75  gram , dissolved in 250  ml of water , ingested over 5 minutes ) with sample collection at 60 and 120 minutes after glucose ingestion.resultsthe prevalence of both insulin resistance ( 64.8% of normal weight children had at least one cardiometabolic abnormality ) and vitamin d deficiency ( 8492% children in the age group of 1018 ) was significantly higher in south - asian children ( p < 0.001 ) than in age and bmi matched european adolescents ( p = 0.002 ) . the prevalence of insulin sensitivity was significantly lower in children with a higher prevalence of a deficiency of vitamin d ( p

a 21-year - old man underwent a bilateral tonsillectomy and uvulopalatopharyngoplasty for obstructive sleep apnea . postoperatively , the patient developed acute bleeding from an aberrant arterial blood supply in the left inferior pole of the left upper thoracic tonsil . the patient was extubated successfully and transferred to the recovery room with no specific findings . the surgeon attempted to control bleeding at the ambulatory otolaryngology clinic but was unable to do so . a third round of general anesthesia was induced with propofol 120 mg and rocuronium 40 mg by the on - call resident . at the end of the procedure , the neuromuscular block was antagonized with glycopyrrolate 0.4 mg and pyridostigmine 10 mg iv with a train - of - four ( tof ) stimulation ( model ns-3cc , houston , tx , usa ) at 12:40 pm . tof stimulation was then reversed with sugammadex 200 mg iv and a tof = 0 at 20:00 pm . after extubation , a large amount of blood was found on the operating table ( large amount on the table

in this article , we present a list of some of the most famous and well - known works of art and science that have been published in the english language over the past two decades . the list includes works by the likes of art historians , philosophers , scientists , artists , and scientists , as well as books and articles written on the same topic . we also provide a brief overview of the literature that has been published on the topic of the same subject in the past and present .

this study is part of a larger project examining the possible health effects of lifetime exposure to marine food and pollutants . to examine subjects aged 7074 years , we conducted a cross - sectional study of 1,131 faroese citizens born between 2 january 1934 and 31 august 1937 . the participants were asked to fast and arrive before breakfast to undergo blood sampling and a thorough clinical examination . all participants traveled to the same examination location and were examined by the same research nurses and a single physician . participants completed current health , past medical history , and lifestyle questionnaires , including summary information on the frequency of fish dinners during the last year . fasting capillary blood sample was used to determine blood glucose concentration using a direct - reading glucose biosensor ( precision xceed and abbott precision xtra plus strips ; abbott diabetes care , copenhagen , denmark ) . insulin resistance was estimated by the homeostasis model assessment ( homa - ir ) and calculated as fpi  fpg / 22.5 ( 10 ) , where fpi is fasting plasma insulin and fpg is fast plasma glucose . serum 25(oh)d3 concentrations were assessed using liquid chromatography - tandem mass spectrometry ( lc - ms / ms ) .

we have developed a leaf senescence database ( lsd ) ( http://www.eplantsenescence.org/ ) by retrieving and integrating information from research papers and public databases . at present , 1145 sags from 21 species were manually curated and categorized into several groups according to their function . users can browse the entries in the database to obtain information including literatures , mutants , phenotypes , expression profiles , mirna interactions , orthologs in other plants and cross links to protein domain and family databases . we have implemented the blast tool kit for sequence similarity search against nucleotide or protein sequences of these sags . a major feature of the lsd is the integration of a bioinformatics platform weblab , which allows users to perform extensive sequence analysis of the sags they are interested in . all the sag sequences are freely available for downloading . help information including user guides , a tutorials and faqs are also available online . we plan to improve the user interface based on user feedback , provide more help documents with case studies , add more links to other useful sites , and update the database in time with more data .

objectiveto compare bone mineral density ( bmd ) in young women with type 1 diabetes and age - matched community control subjects.research design and methodsfemale study participants were recruited from participants in a cross - sectional study assessing bmd in young female type 1 diabetic case subjects and nondiabetic control subjects ( n = 138 ) . bmd was measured in the anterior / posterior ( l14 ) spine , total hip , femoral neck , total forearm , and whole body using hologic dexa ( hologic qdr-4500a ; hologic , waltham , ma ) . the baseline study enrolled 72 case subjects ( diabetes duration > 2 years ) and 91 control subjects aged 1337 years who were at least 2 years postmenarchal ( 7 ) . all case and control subjects were recruited 2 years later to assess change in bmd over time ( stratum cay cay ) . demographic , lifestyle , metabolic , and bmd characteristics were compared between diabetic and control participants at baseline and 2 years after enrollment.resultsthere were no significant differences between the two groups at baseline ( p = 0.001 ) , but there were significant differences at 2 years ( p < 0.05 ) . at baseline , there were no

aim . to assess the enamel cracks after debonding of brackets bonded with two different adhesive systems and also to evaluate enamel cracking after removing adhesive remnants and enamel polishing . methods . sixty upper central incisors were randomly divided into two equal groups . in the first group , a standard edgewise twin metal bracket was bonded with transbond xt ( 3 m , usa ) to the center of the labial surface of each tooth ; in the second group , the same brackets were bonded with self - adhesive composite cement . all the brackets were debonded with i-546 remover pliers and a gentle squeezing force was applied to the pliers at a speed of 0.5 mm / min until bond failure occurred . the number of visible cracks ( i.e. , visible cracks visible to the naked eye ) and the length and direction of each crack were assessed with the aid of adobe photoshop cs software . results . there were no significant differences between the two groups in the amount of enamel that remained visible after removal of the adhesive and after polishing ( p < 0.001 ) . there was no statistically significant difference between the groups in terms of number of cracks visible after removing the adhesive ( p = 0.

sensitive skin syndrome ( sss ) is a common and challenging condition , yet little is known about its underlying pathophysiology . sss is a state of hyperreactivity to environmental stimuli , presenting with a clear clinical picture and resulting from a single underlying pathology or a combination of pathologies . it is difficult to diagnose due to the subjective nature of the symptoms and the lack of an objective screening test for sss . in order to formulate a systematic clinical approach , the medical and cosmetic communities have attempted to characterize the condition . in this article , we review the literature on the clinical presentation of sss and the current state of research on the subject .

bilateral spontaneous pneumothorax ( bpsp ) is a very rare clinical condition with an occurrence ranging from 1.3 to 1.9% of all cases of spontaneous pneumonia . we present a case of a 20-year - old overweight , non - smoker , greek male patient who presented with a bilateral bp . the patient was managed with bilateral tube thoracostomy through the fifth intercostal spaces . during hospitalization , both lungs were gradually expanded ; the use of continuous aspiration was necessary for a couple of days , in order to achieve complete expansion of the left lung . chest computed tomography ( ct ) was performed and revealed the presence of bilateral blebs / bullae at the apices of both upper lungs . three years after the operation , no recurrence of bp occurred and the patient remains healthy .

introductionfamilial adenomatous polyposis accounts for 1% of colorectal cancers , and provides a model of apc inactivation as an early genetic event for the approximately 80%85% of cancers that develop from sporadic polyps . the aim of this study was to compare the effect of rofibecal treatment with a cyclooxygenase-2 inhibitor on the composition of fatty acids in the feces of patients with familial adenomegaly . methodswe analysed the fecal content of long chained fatty acids , including very high levels of arachidonic acid and docosahexaenoic acid , and low levels of linoleic acid and alpha - linolenic acid in serum phospholipids and duodenal lesions from a double - blind , randomized , placebo - controlled , double - blinded , and double - multicenter study . the results were compared with those of a previous study in patients with ileostomy and ileal - pouch - anal anastomosis . resultsfecal fatty acid content was significantly higher in the colectomized group than in the ileoanal group ( p < 0.001 ) , and significantly lower in the anast

purposethe purpose of this study was to evaluate the efficacy of computerized local anesthesia ( wand ) and a traditional syringe for the control of pain in maxillary maxillofacial surgery.materials and methodstwand is a computer - controlled local anesthesia delivery system that drives local anesthetic through plastic microtubing into a plastic handle to which a luer - lok needle is attached . the computer compensates for variation in resistance to flow . during needle insertion , continuous positive pressure delivers an anesthetic drip that precedes the needle . the combination of a controlled flow rate and anesthetic pathway results in a virtually imperceptible injection and rapid onset of profound anesthesia . the aim of the present study was the comparison of pain response to the different phases of local anaesthetic delivery by the wand and a conventional syringe.resultstwo hundred and fifty maxillary premolars were selected for the study . the mean age of the maxillary dentition was 67.5 years ( range , 35 to 80 ) . the median duration of maxillary and mandibular surgery was 6.5 months ( range : 6 to 12 months ) . pain was significantly lower in the wand group than in the traditional group ( p < 0.001 ) . in

introductionchronic obstructive pulmonary disease ( copd ) is a progressive lung disease which is characterized by airway obstruction and lung parenchyma destruction . patients may experience dyspnoea or shortness of breath , a persistent cough with sputum production , decreased exercise tolerance and decreased quality of life . this study aimed to develop and verify the use of a remote pulse oximetry system on healthy individuals to determine the validity of remote measurements when used for exercise rehabilitation purposes.methodsthe pulse oximeter is a noninvasive device which continuously monitors a person 's peripheral blood oxygen saturation ( spo2 ) and heart rate ( hr ) . it is used clinically to measure exercise intensity and warn of exercise - induced desaturation . the concept of remote monitoring of spo2 data including hr for exercise purposes for rehabilitation purposes has received attention in the literature . the aim of this study was to establish the technical feasibility of transmitting pulse data remotely during a home - exercise program.resultsthere were no significant differences in spo2 and hr between healthy individuals using the pulse oximeters ( p = 0.001 , p < 0.01 ) and those using a conventional pulse monitor ( p > 0.05 ) . there was no statistically significant difference between the

in order to clarify the relative contribution of ams , hypoxia or exercise to ngal levels , we also studied a cohort pre- and postexercise at near sea level , and a further cohort exposed to acute normobaric hypoventilation at 3 study altitudes and a subset of the c - reactive protein ( hscrp ) . in all of these studies , the percentage of ngal would increase at these various stimuli and that the magnitude of any increase might relate to the presence of the ngal . we therefore studied a combined cohort of t- and t-vendors from 2 expeditions to ha . we found that the proportion of ngals that would increase was significantly higher at the t - and t - vendors than at the hscrs . this was due to the fact that the t- , t - , and tv - t - t- samples were collected immediately following a day 2 ( 3400  m ) , day 6 ( 4270 and 5150 ) , and day 10 ( 5643 and 5364 ) of the study . we also found that a t- or t- test was taken immediately after a t - test and collected immediately after the second t-test . the

the epitaxial growth of 2,2:6,2-ternaphthalene                  ( nnn ) on muscovite mica(001 ) is reported . the growth of nnn on the mica substrate was performed using the hot wall epitaxy ( hwe ) technique , which allows us to perform the growth process in a relatively low vacuum and in close equilibrium with the substrate . the final product was obtained as a colorless product , which appears to be highly insoluble in common solvents and could only be recrystallized from 1,2,4-trichlorobenzene ( colorless platelets ) .

adult onset still disease ( aosd ) is a rare disease of unknown etiology . we report a case of a 38-year - old female patient who presented with fever , sore throat , coughing , shivering , chills , joint , and muscle pain , headache , chest pain , and herpetic rashes around her lips for nearly 2 weeks and she was hospitalized to investigate the etiology of fever . on thoracal imagings , bilateral hilar lymph nodes of which the largest being 2 cm in diameter , bilateral pleural effusion , and atelectasic areas in the parenchyma surrounding the effusion were observed . on abdominal tomography , no bacterial growth was detected in urine , throat , csf , and blood cultures . on follow - up period , rashes , and swelling on her right and left wrists were detected , so she was consulted to rheumatology clinic , and naproxen sodium therapy was started . on physical examination dullness over traube s space was suspected to be infective endocarditis . however her hyperfebrile state persisted , echocardiograms obtained for the differential diagnosis of infectiveendocarditis did not reveal any abnormal cells in csf . biochemical analysis of the

objectivethe purpose of this study was to evaluate the degree of conversion ( dc ) of two commercially dual - cured resin cements in different viscosities ( low and high ) when they were light - activated or when the materials were allowed to self - cure solely , after 5 minutes and 24 hours from the mixture of base and catalyst.materials and methodsvariolink ii ( ivoclar vivadent , schaan , liechtenstein ) and nexus 2 ( kerr corp . , orange , ca , usa ) were evaluated in high and low viscosity . the dc was measured by fourier transform infrared spectroscopy ( ftir ) at 45c at room temperature and after 24 hours of self - curing ( autopolymerizing mode ) or light - cured ( dual - polymerizing mode).resultsthe dc was significantly higher in high viscos than in low ( p<0.001 ) when the resin was allowed to cure alone ( 5 minutes or 24 hours ) . the difference in the dc between the two groups was statistically significant ( p=0.002 ) . in addition , the dc of variolink i was significantly lower than that of nexus 2 when the material self - cured alone ( p

despite 15 years of the global  safe motherhood and child health goals , many women and children in resource - poor countries continue to die and suffer from ill health . the challenges of achieving these goals are to provide known interventions to the full population and at the right time when most known adverse outcomes can be prevented . one emerging policy is the role of preconception care as a complement to prenatal care . this report highlights examples of national programs and projects indicating that such approaches are feasible and acceptable to the population . these case studies that were presented at the first national summit on preconception services in 2005 , range from a mature program in hong kong , to emerging programs in korea and belgium , and select program enhancement and recovery efforts in china .

objective . to compare interleukin ( il ) levels in exudative and transudative pleural fluids by measuring il levels in pleural effusions developed  usernameiously owing to various etiological factors in childhood . methods . the study group consisted of 26 patients admitted with a diagnosis of childhood                 fluid effusion . all samples  username were obtained by thoracocentesis at the   diagnosis time . the first  username samples were centrifuged immediately for the measurement of il concentration and the supernatant layers were stored at                 70c until testing for cytokines . the second samples username were inoculated in aerobic , anaerobic , and lwenstein - jensen media and stained with gram , giemsa , and ziehl - nielsen                 dyes . the third samples were analysed for protein , glucose , and lactate dehydrogenase ( ldh ) as indicators of infection . results . the results showed a significant difference between the levels of il-1 , il-6 , and il-8 in the exudates ( p < 0.001 ) and the transudates  username ( p > 0.05 ) in the samples of patients with higher ldh concentrations

introductionliterature analysis showed few articles about this complication , but no publication has described the management of open patella fracture around total knee arthroplasty.case reportwe report a unique case of an open periprosthetic patellar fracture above a total knee total arthropathy , sustained by a 56-year - old female patient.conclusiondespite the poor outcome of operative management in patellars periprosthetic fracture , this approach should be considered for acute and post traumatic fractures in young patients with a good remaining bone stock .

purposeto investigate the effects of near - infrared ( nir ) radiation on gene expression in human corneal epithelial cells after nir irradiation simulating solar nir radiation that reaches human tissues.materials and methodsthe three - dimensional reconstructed human cornea epithelial model ( labcyte cornea - model ) prepared from enzymatically digested normal human human epithelial tissues was used as an in vitro model of cornea tissue to simulate the human eye , and we evaluated dna microarray and real - time polymerase chain reaction ( pcr ) analysis results.resultsthe total rna was extracted from the cells and the quality of the rna samples was measured using uv absorbance , and the quantity and quality was also assessed using an agilent 2100 bioanalyzer series ii.conclusionsthe results of this study suggest that nir exposure to solar - like radiation can induce changes in gene expression .

introductionhypertension is a common chronic medical condition affecting people in pakistan and the rest of the world . it is an important modifiable risk factor for cardiovascular morbidity and mortality , particularly for stroke ( accounting for 51% of all stroke deaths worldwide ) , ischemic heart disease , chronic kidney disease , congestive heart failure , aortic aneurysm , and peripheral arterial disease . the increasing prevalence of hypertension together with a deficient control makes this one of the frequent conditions that require urgent medical attention . therefore , we conducted this study to determine the prevalence of hypertensive crisis , its management , and its outcome in patients presenting to a tertiary care center in karachi.materials and methodsthis was a retrospective study conducted at the aga khan university hospital ( akuh ) from 2005 till 2010 . a sample of 1336 consecutive patients fulfilling the inclusion criterion was selected . the sample was drawn using computerized medical record system international classification of diseases-9-coordination and maintenance ( icd-9cm ) at the information management system . the patients admitted with primary diagnosis of hypertension crisis , uncontrolled hypertension , hypertensive urgency , and hypertensive emergency were selected through the icd9-cm ( i-10 :
