p2y12 , a g protein - coupled receptor subtype of the ionotropic p2x family , is involved in the regulation of inflammatory responses in the central nervous system ( cns ) . in the nervous system , the tissue- and cellular - selective expression of this receptor exhibits a pattern throughout white and gray matter consistent with astrocyte expression , suggesting high conserved tissue - homogeneity and phenotype - specificity , and a hypothesized role in myelination . in contrast , the expression of p2y in oligodendrocytes and myelin sheaths of rat cerebral cortex , subcortical areas , and periventricular white matter is highly heterogeneous . the present work provides comparative evidence about the cell - to - cell specificity of the p2ry12 receptor in microglia under neuroinflammatory conditions such as those sustained during amyotrophic lateral sclerosis ( als ) and multiple sclerosis ( ms ) . the results of this study suggest that microgliosis is a distinct cell type in the healthy and diseased cns , and that p2ypr12-immunoreactive antibodies recognize the c - terminus or the second intracellular loop of the receptor . in addition , the results suggest that the p

objective . acute kidney injury ( aki ) requiring dialysis is a serious complication in critically ill patients , bringing increased morbidity , mortality , and costs of care . to date , there are limited studies that have examined the course or progression of aki after icu admission until the point of dialysis initiation . whether the degree of severity of kidney injury prior to dialysis requirement independently influences mortality risk in aki is not known . in order to facilitate risk stratification that may be useful for the development of prospective studies , which are geared towards early diagnosis or intervention in severe aki , we characterized the spectrum of severity in a cohort of icu patients with aki who required dialysis . methods . we examined a cohort derived from the veterans affairs ( va ) inpatient evaluation center ( ipec ) ; a national quality improvement program which reports risk - adjusted mortality , length of stay , and adherence to evidence - based practices in all va icus by collecting data electronically from va computer databases . we specifically tested the association of creatinine elevation during icu stay with the risk of hospital mortality . results . a total of 617,927 patients ( n = 324,999 ) admitted to all 191 va inpatient rehabilitation centers between october 2001

background . parents of children with intellectual disabilities ( ids ) are more likely to show signs of psychological distress or depressive symptoms and to exhibit lower well - being than parents of typically developing children . the purpose of this study was to obtain a clear perspective on the situation facing these parents through a facility - based nationwide survey . methods . a total of 1,102 institutions and consultation centers for children with developmental disabilities distributing in all prefectures in japan were included in this study . a questionnaire was sent to the parents of these children . results . of 460 facilities , the response rate was 41.7% . according to the clients ' main disabilities , each facility was classified into the following categories : i d , pervasive developmental disorder ( pdd ) , profound intellectual and multiple disabilities ( pimd ) , physical disabilities , and others . the parents ' mental health was found to be poor from both the patients ' and caregivers ' point of view . there were no significant differences between the parents and the clients in terms of the level of mental health distress . conclusions . it is assumed that the level and quality of psychiatric services in these facilities are not adequate to meet the mental health needs of parents of people with i d .

in may 2001 , 24 cases of salmonella typhimurium dt160 salmonellosis were reported in the auckland region , compared with an average of four sporadic cases each month in the previous 7 months . a case - control study and environmental investigation were undertaken to identify the vehicle of infection and source of the outbreak . from may 2001 to august 2001 , 170 case - patients meeting the case definition were identified . of these , 119 agreed to participate and were enrolled in the study , along with 235 matched controls . the most frequently reported symptoms were diarrhea ( 97% ) , abdominal pain ( 77% ) and excessive tiredness ( 67% ) . the median duration of illness was 7 days ( range 144 days ) ; 17 ( 15% ) patients were hospitalized , and none died . direct handling of dead wild birds , consumption of fast food , and consumption of food at a large gathering , such as at a party or large barbecue , were associated with illness .

non - crystallographic symmetry ( ncs ) is common in macromolecular structures , occurring in about 1/3 or more of structures in the protein data bank . in most cases it brings with it information on relationships between density in different parts of the crystal and between coordinates in different part of the structure . these relationships greatly improve density modification and refinement , two key steps in structure determination . here we present an approach to find ncs by directly searching for patterns of density that are present in more than one place in an electron density map . we find that this is a rapid and accurate way to identify ncs , even in a case where the map is poor and the correlation of ncs - related density is low .

objective . to identify specific behavioral risk factors for overweight in young children and to assess their quantitative contribution to the prevalence of overweight in the general population and in high risk sub groups . methods . in a large population - based birth cohort , we prospectively investigated associations between dietary habits , screen time and physical activity when the children were 5 and 7 years old and overweight at the age of 8 years . we also assessed the role of these behavioral factors separately in children of overweight mothers and children of mothers with low education . we estimated the reduction of overweight prevalence that could be achieved if these risk factors would be reduced . results . of the 3963 children ( 47% ) in the study cohort at birth , 1871 were overweight at 8 years old . dietary habits and ( in)activities associated with overweight risk in specific age groups need to be identified and their contribution to overweight prevalence in the population needs to be assessed . in addition , we need to know whether the importance of specific behaviors for the development of overweight differs between sub groups of the population . children from families with a low socio - economic status and children with overweight parents are at increased risk to develop overweight . overweight prevalence was significantly higher in children with low socioeconomic status than in those with high socioeconomic status ( p =

the department of defense ( dod ) is developing a rapid field test that would determine if field supplies of water are safe to drink by the warfighter during a military operation . antibody - based handheld assays ( hhas ) have proven effective in detecting biological warfare agents ( bwas ) in the air and in powders . in the present study , hhas from multiple manufacturers were evaluated for their effectiveness at detecting two specific targets , staphylococcal enterotoxin b ( seb ) and ricin , in multiple water matrices . the hhas were not modified in any way to optimize for the detection of toxins within water supplies despite the fact that they were initially developed to test for concentrated agent collected from high - efficiency aerosol collectors and impinged into a phosphate buffered saline ( pbs ) solution rather than to test unbuffered water supplies . the ability of hhas to detect seb in water was evaluated in four different matrices , including formulated tap water , formulated water with 1 mg / l free available chlorine ( fac ) , water treated by reverse osmosis ( ro ) with 1mg / l fac , and ro water with 2 mg / ml residual residual bromine . the results demonstrated that hhas for detecting seb

osteosarcoma is a common primary malignant bone tumor with complicated pathogenesis and frequent distal metastasis , thus causing higher mortality rates , especially in the teenagers . this study aimed to describe the mechanism of p15 gene mutation in the occurrence and lung metastasis of osteosaroma in a rat model , as previously described . a total of 60 sd rats ( both males and females , ages 4~5 weeks , body weight 150~170 g ) from the laboratory animal center of harbin medical university , were randomly divided into control and model groups ( n=30 each ) . after 0 , 1 , 2 , 4 , and 6 weeks of the inoculation , 5 rats from model or control groups were sacrificed to separate the osteo and pulmonary tissues . pcr - ihc was used to detect the positive and metastatic conditions for a quantitative analysis . p15 mutation was found to be significantly associated with the occurrence of osteo ( p<0.05 ) , lung ( p=0.0005 ) , and lung cancer ( p>0.001 ) in the model group , whereas it was not found in the control group . in addition , p15 mutations were found to significantly correlate with the progression of pulmonary metastasis in

objective : trichoscopy is a noninvasive technique for differential diagnosis of various hair and scalp diseases . here , authors evaluated trichoscopic patterns in ttm and authors believe that these patterns are specific to ttm , which can aide in early diagnosis of this chronic condition.materials and methods : this study was carried out on 10 patients attending department of dermatology in a tertiary hospital attached to s. nijalingappa medical college at bagalkot , south india between january 2014 and july 2014.results:ten patients with clinical features of ttm were subjected for a complete history and dermatological examination . the mean age of the patients was 34 years ( minimum age 13 years and maximum age 55 years ) . mean duration of disease was 19 months ( minimum 2 months and maximum 36 months ) . the most common symptom was patchy loss of hair over the scalp ( 100% ) and one patient ( 10% ) had tonsure pattern of hair loss . four patients agreed to knowingly pulling of hair , and 10% gave a history of eating them ( trichophagia).conclusion:trichoscopic analysis of scalp and hair is a useful technique for the diagnosis of trichotillomania ( ttm

sexual activity has become more acceptable in our culture recently , initiating various forms of sex , and as a result , many different clinical conditions of sexually transmitted disease ( std ) have appeared . in western countries the incidence of syphilis has risen , mainly in the 20 - 24 age group and its clinical manifestations have been various . in korea it is usually asymptomatic and less frequently presents as proctitis , ulcer and pseudotumors . therefore , it is difficult to diagnose and physicians occasionally might prescribe inappropriate treatments . in this paper we describe a case of primary rectal syphilis which was suspected to be rectal cancer . the patient complained of bowel habit change with tenesmus , mucous discharge , anal pain and intermittent presence of blood in stool . he had a 2 cm sized , firm , non - tender and indurated nodule on the left side of the inguinal area and multiple condyloma acuminatum around the anus . sigmoidoscopy in our gastrointestinal endoscopy unit showed two indurated masses of 1.5 to 2 cm on the posterior wall of the middle and lower rectum . the masses had a coin like appearance , and each of them had a slightly depressed and ulcerated surface .

backgroundthe protective effects of vagus nerve during ischemia / reperfusion ( i / r ) have been suggested . the present study was performed to examine the interaction between vagal nerve and melatonin during gastric injury and peptic ulcer.material/methodstwenty-two male wistar rats weighing 180 - 220 g were divided into two groups : control group and experimental group . the experimental group was treated with vagotomy and vagotomy plus melatonin administration for 12 hr . the control group received melatonin injection intra - lumen and the intervention group received vagotomy alone or in combination with melatonin.resultsthe experimental group showed a significant increase in the secretion of bicarbonate from duodenum mucosa through an increase in melatonin secretion ( p < 0.001 ) . the vagotomy group showed an increase of the release of gastric amylase ( p > 0.05 ) and the melatonin levels ( p = 0.01 ) . in contrast , the experimental groups showed a decrease in the levels of the released pancreatic enzymes ( p<0.05).conclusionthis study shows that melatonin is involved in the protective effect of vagal nerves in the healing of acute gastric injuries and

this study is a cross - sectional study to determine the influence of local and global influences on the development and evolution of the world health organization ( who ) health care system ( hsa ) in developing countries . the study is based on a series of interviews with experts in hsa and the hsa . hsa is an international organization of hsa , a non governmental organization that promotes hsa education and awareness of the health and well - being of people living in developing and developed countries . it was established that hsa has the potential to improve the quality of health care services and to improve quality of life for people living and working in developing nations . this study provides a unique opportunity for hsa to study the impact of local influences on hsa health care and the development of the global health insurance system ( gis ) .

genome - wide case - control association studies ( gwass ) have identified single nucleotide polymorphisms ( snps ) with small effects on type 2 diabetes ( t2d ) risk , but few of the associated snps , identified primarily in european ancestry populations , are replicated in african american ( aa ) populations and few snps have been identified in aa populations . here we present linkage and association analyses on the aa subset of the genetics of niddm ( gennid ) study using genotypes on 9,203 fine - mapping snps added to the genome scan snps used previously . our first goal was to test for association of the gene - based snps with chromosome 2 , chromosome 7 , chromosome 14 , chromosome 13 , and chromosome 18 , and age of diagnosis ( aod ) in order to identify genes related to t2D in aas . in total , 1,496 aa members of 580 pedigrees were studied at 10 sites . the strongest signal for t2 d occurred on chromosome 2 at 95121 megabases ( mb ) , with weaker support for aod in the same region . linkage was limited to chromosome 7 at 5079 mb and to aod on chromosome 18

the circadian system is a supraphysiological system that regulates biological functions every 24 h , such as sleep / activity , temperature , heart rate , glucose level , cortisol production , and oxidative stress . in mammals , this system is organized by a central clock localized in the suprachiasmatic nucleus of the hypothalamus ( scn ) and in a series of peripheral oscillators such as the liver , lung , adrenal gland , fibroblast cells , and others tissues . at the molecular level , the complex clock - bmal1 stimulates the expression of negative regulators per13 and cry1 - 2 , and their protein inhibits the effect of the heterodimer clock / bmal-1 . moreover , the molecular clock has different modulators which give fine tuning of output signals such as rev - erb , sirt1 , and pgc1 . this review will primarily focus on the hypothesis which states that the variation of genetic components of the circadian system , similar to environmental changes , can affect several physiological systems and produce an elevation of the risk of developing a disease . in recent years , there has been a significant increase in available information from polymorphic variations and epigenetic modification over clock genes and their risk of diseases . curiously , a large quantity of polymorphisms

protein phosphatase 2a ( pp2a ) is a highly conserved serine / threonine phosphotrimer that is composed of a catalytic subunit ( c ) and one of the four families of regulatory subunits ( b ) . the majority of phosphorylation occurs on serine and threonines , with the majority occurring on the phospho - serine residues . despite a shared catalytic mechanism , substrate specificity within the ppp family remains elusive . recent studies suggest that substrate specificity arises from additive effects of multiple discrete interactions between the b subunit and the substrate . this review will focus on our emerging understanding of the emerging mechanisms by which the substrate specificity of the ubiquitously expressed ppp2a complex is mediated .

schizophrenia is a cognitive disorder characterized by the breakdown of large - scale cortico - cerebellar - thalamic - cortical circuits , or more generally by the inability to integrate neural processes in different brain areas , a syndrome termed dysconnectivity . this condition may pare down , in particular , the input to pyramidal cells of the dorsolateral prefrontal and temporal cortex , and hence is likely to be involved in higher - level cognition . reduced connectivity may thus lead to fragmentation , a loss of coherence in cognitive activity . although many previous studies have examined cortical network connectivity in schizophrenia and other disorders , none to our knowledge has employed the concept of percolation , an issue that we presently redress . the presence of a critical threshold motivates us to revisit the notion of cortical dysconnective as a sudden breakdown of cortical connectivity . we propose an alternative theoretical possibility that , instead of brain pathologies are associated with a breakdown in the local organization .

dysphagia is the subjective awareness of difficulty in the passage of solids or liquids from the oropharynx to the stomach . mechanical dysphagia can be caused by neuromuscular motility disorders and mechanical obstruction , and it is classically seen in elderly women with short stature who have hypertension and kyphosis . we report herein a case of an elderly woman who presented with aortic aneurysm associated with progressive dysphagosia to solids for the last 6 months . computed tomography ( ct ) of the chest demonstrated an enlarged , tortuous aorta . the upper gastrointestinal barium study revealed marked extrinsic compression of the distal esophagus just above the esophagogastric junction . we concluded that the symptoms and the results of the imaging studies were consistent with dysphagias aortica . although we recommended surgical correction of the aortal aneurysem or percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy , the patient declined any invasive procedures .

flibanserin the 4th edition of the diagnostic and statistical manual - text revision ( dsm - iv - tr ) , female sexual interest / arousal disorder ( fsiad ) , which has both properties of hypoactive sexual desire disorder ( hsdd ) and female sexual arousal disorder , has been defined as chara or absence of sexual fantasy and sexual desire associated with personal pain in the dsm-5 . in the present study , the arizona sexual experiences scale ( asex ) was used for making a comparison of the sexual function effectiveness of antidepressant vs. placebo proven in stage 2b studies among four depression programs . the two central phase 3 studies ( violet6 , daisy7 ) all used e - diary and did not show big statistical improvement compared to placebo in the pre - specified co - primary efficacy endpoint that assessed daily sexual desire . these studies show a statistically significant improvement in secondary end points that measured sexual desire with other tools known as female sexual function index ( fsfi ) and reduction in female sexual distress scale - revised ( fsds - r ) . in both studies , the most commonly reported adverse effects include drowsiness ( 11.8% ) , vertigo ( 10.5% ) and fatigue ( 10

keratoacanthoma centrifugum marginatum ( kcm ) is a very rare keratotic tumor of the hair follicles of the scalp . it is a distinct type of keratoma which usually occurs over extremities and scalp is unusual site for development of lesion . we report a rare case of kcm occurring on scalp which is an unusual site . a 62-year - old male , watchman by occupation presented with asymptomatic raised lesion on scalp since one year . cutaneous examination revealed a single , irregular , 12  15 cm , yellowish plaque on vertices of scalp with nodular surface and a central crater . deep punch biopsy revealed exoendophytic , globular , well circumscribed central cup shaped crater , epidermal invagination with marked hyperkeratosis and horn cyst with rich keratin filled crater imparting glassy appearance and well demarcated regular base epidermis forming buttress over the pseudhorn cyst dense nodular lymphocytic infiltrate in the upper dermis . surgical excision with grafting was done successfully with dramatic improvement with no recurrence at 9 months .

hydroxyapatite biomaterials are materials that are very widely used in several health purposes , including as a source of calcium for the manufacture of toothpaste and as an important material in the formation / bone repair . among the abundant raw materials are the shells of crabs , which are one of indonesia 's main export commodities . the raw material for the production of hydroxyapatites is very easily available and abundant in indonesian . the production process was easy and the cost is also relatively inexpensive if done on a large scale . in this study , we have investigated the crystal structure of calcium carbonate ( cao ) and carbonate apatite ( ca10(po4)6co3 ) using scanning electron microscopy combined with the ability to generate localized chemical information ( sem - edxa ) variant .

purposecognitive impairment due to brain injury is an important factor affecting patients independent functioning and participation in activities and interfering with their return to daily living and work . this systematic review investigated the use of computer - based virtual reality ( vr ) programs in cognitive rehabilitation of patients with brain injury and the overall effect of these programs on cognition.materials and methodsa systematic review was conducted of studies that used computer based vr programs for cognitive evaluation and interventions for patients with traumatic brain injury.resultsa total of 10 studies were included in the review .                  , a randomized controlled trial , was used for cognitive assessment and intervention . the most common cognitive assessments were memory and attention , followed by executive function and attention . a total of five studies were randomized controlled trials ( rcts ) and one was a crossover study . excepting the case report , four studies                  were assessed using the physiotherapy                  cognition evidence database ( pedro ) scale , and the remaining two studies were scored on the pico ( patient , intervention , and outcome ) method.conclusioncognitive rehabilitation of brain injury patients using computer based                  programs using vr technology is safe and effective in reducing cognitive impairment and improving functional outcomes .

despite a considerable progress in malaria control in iran over the past few years , the disease still remains a major health problem in south and southeastern parts of the country . identification of anopheles species responsible for malaria transmission remains challenging . the aim of this study was to evaluate cytochrome oxidase i ( coi ) gene alongside 28s - d3 as a diagnostic tool for identification of species of an .                 cytochus fluviatilis . the coi gene was amplified using universal primers ubc6 ( 5- gga gga ttt gga aat tga tta gtt cc -3 ) and ubc9 and was sequenced using the same primers as used for amplification of 28s-d3 . all the dna samples were subjected to allele specific ( as)-pcr based on allele specific pcr for identifying species . the results showed that all the samples were from fars province in south iran . the as-pcr results confirmed the identity of species y , which is presumably species t , while individuals from other areas exhibited heterozygocity at the only base pair position that identifies species u and t . in addition , in a separate study , species t and species u were

schwannoma of the great omentum is an extremely rare tumor of the peripheral nerve sheath , of which schwannnoma and neurofibroma are the most frequent . we report a case of a 55-year - old man referred to our department for the treatment of a tumor detected close to the stomach by ultrasound . although he had no complaints and symptoms , the tumor had been increasing in size over one year . abdominal and endoscopic ultrasound showed a 2.0  1.3 cm cystic mass lateral to the wall of the stomach . under a perioperative diagnosis of cystic tumor in the abdomen and cholelithiasis , we performed laparoscopic resection with vessel sealing system . the tumor was configured by a well - encapsulated round mass measuring 30  18  15 mm in diameter . histologically , the spindle - shaped cells were arranged in interlacing bundles and fascicles , together with varying numbers of tumor cells containing various amounts of light brown or grayish pigment . immunohistochemically , most of the neoplastic cells reacted moderately to nse , ncam and s-100 protein .

backgrounddespite advances in risk factor management on an epidemiological level , various blood markers associated with increased risk for death and cardiovascular endpoints have been identified , but currently very few have been shown to have a diagnostic impact or important clinical implications that would affect patient management . in this study , we sought to compare microrna ( mirna ) expression in whole blood of patients with coronary artery disease ( cad ) to that of healthy aged - matched controls.material/methodswe performed an initial exploratory microarray analysis in 5 cases and controls and then further examined the most highly expressed mirnas in an additional 15 cases and 15 healthy subjects . we also collected whole blood samples from patients with 2 risk factors for cad , but did not have angiographically significant cad.resultsthe incidence of cad was significantly higher in patients with higher levels of mirna compared with healthy subjects ( p < 0.001 ) . in addition , patients with significant cad had significantly higher expression of mirnas compared with controls ( p = 0.002 ) . the highest levels of expression were found in the heart and lungs , whereas the lowest levels were seen in the cerebrospinal fluid ( p > 0.05).conclusionalthough mirna expression signatures in tissues and blood have

polybia emaciata , a genus of the genus polybia , is a typical swarm - founding polistine wasp ( hymenoptera : vespidae : polistinae ) in the genus epiponini . the nest of this species is hard and durable , with a single small entrance hole near the bottom of the nest , and it uses mud as the main raw material for nest construction . we hypothesized that the high durability of the mud - encased polybia nest may reduce the need for behavioral defense by p. emaciATA workers , relative to species with similar colony and nest sizes . we tested this prediction by disturbing the nests of p. occidentalis , a similar - sized polybia species , and comparing the workers ' responses to vertebrate attacks with those of simultaneously observed paper - nesting species in the same location . our results suggest that the protective properties of mud - enclosed polybia nests may be adaptive , and that the behavior of the wasps may have changed to take advantage of nest durability .

human gliomas represent 50% to 60% of all intracranial tumors . early diagnosis and prolonging survival in glioma patients remains a great challenge for clinicians in the field of neurooncology . the dna - base excision repair ( ber ) pathway is responsible for the repair of exogenous and endogenous alkylating and oxidative dna damage , which may lead to carcinogenesis , cell death , and aging if left unrepaired . n - methylpurine - dna glycosylase ( mpg ) as a dna repair enzyme is a main component in the ber pathway . because of the potential role of dna base lesions in mutagenesis , a number of studies have been performed to investigate the association of mpg with various human cancers . in this paper , we focus on the involvement of the mpg gene and protein expression in the cellular responses to chemotherapeutic agents . the aim of this study was to investigate mpg - overexpressing cells in human glioblastoma multiforme ( gbm ) , ovarian cancer , osteosarcoma , and breast cancer . mpg overexpression may be a more sensitive approach to sensitize the cell to the chemother

objectivethe prevalence of overweight and obesity among children and adolescents has widely increased worldwide , making it one of the most common chronic disorders in this age group and in adulthood . although valuable information has been appearing in the literature or online , such as works from the health behaviour of school - aged children study which is mainly related to social determinants of health and well - being among young people , no systematic review has been conducted to understand the worldwide magnitude of the overweight / obesity problem among the adolescent population . thus , the objective of this study was to systematically review the literature regarding the prevalence of being overweight and obese in adolescents ( 1019 years old ) of both sexes published in the past 12 academic years ( 19992011).materials and methodsa systematic literature search was performed which ended on june 7 , 2012 . in total , 2537 articles were selected . the most widely used growth charts are the centers for disease control and prevention ( cdc-2000 ) , the international task force ( iotf ) , and the 2007 growth references for 5 to 19 year olds produced by the world health organization ( who-2007).resultsthe final number of articles included in this review was 39 articles related to overweight to obesity and also a study on the latest statistics on

sacubitril / valsartan is a combination of a neprilysin inhibitor and an angiotensin ii receptor blocker , indicated to decrease the risk of cardiovascular death and hospitalization for heart failure in patients with chronic heart failure . rhabdomyolysis is a syndrome characterized by skeletal muscle cell damage , leading to elevated creatine kinase ( ck ) , lactate dehydrogenase ( ldh ) , and aspartate aminotranferase ( ast ) , which can result in severe sequelae , including renal failure , cardiac arrhythmia , hyperthermia and death . we report the case of a 63-year - old guyanese woman who developed severe rabdomyolyssis and an elevation of transaminases within 26  days of initiation of sacubitil / valartan . the patient was treated with a regimen of atorvastatin , carvedilol , digoxin , furosemide , spironolactone , rivaroxaban , and a low dose of entresto .

purinergic receptor p2x ligand - gated ion channel 7 and caspase activation and recruitment domain 8 ( card8 ) polymorphisms are known to play a role in the pathogenesis of inflammatory diseases , such as rheumatoid arthritis ( ra ) and systemic lupus erythematosus ( sle ) . however , there are no available data linking card8 rs2043211 polymorphisms with gout . here , we investigated the association of the card8 and px2x7r rs3751142 single - nucleotide polymorphisms ( snps ) with the susceptibility and clinical manifestations of gout in the male korean population . a total of 242 male gout patients and 280 healthy male controls were consecutively enrolled in this study . the genotypic distribution of card8 polymorphisms was significantly different between gout and control patients ( p < 0.05 ) . gout was associated with a significantly higher prevalence of rs375201142 ( p = 0.001 ) and a significantly lower prevalence of the rs2043111 ( p > 0.01 ) polymorphism in gout compared with controls ( p=0.002 ) . card8 genotyping was significantly associated with the risk of g

ileocaecal crohn 's disease is a chronic inflammatory condition that may involve the entire gi tract . isolated involvement of the appendix is reported to be 0.2% to 1.8% , and is usually associated with ileocarcinoma in 25% of ileal and 50% of caecal disease . appendicectomy alone when performed for appendicitis in a patient who was previously diagnosed to have ileococcal crohn's disease could lead to postoperative complications including enterocutaneous fistula . we report a case of a 24-year - old female patient who presented to us with a 6-week history of abdominal pain , confined to the right lower abdomen . a computerized tomography scan of the abdomen showed pericaecal collection with an inflammatory mass in the front of the caecum . the appendix was surgically excised through the previous incision after excising the fistula , and histopathology of the resected specimen confirmed crohn s disease .

objectivethe aim of this study was to investigate the association between milk intake and body mass index ( bmi ) in preadolescents and adolescents of a population belonging to the swedish part of the european youth heart study ( eyhs).research design and methodsa random sample of 267 caucasian preadolescent ( 121 girls , 146 boys ) and 330 adolescents ( 164 girls , 166 boys ) of the eyhs population in sweden participated in the study . milk intake was assessed by an interviewer - mediated 24-hour recall . lp and lnp genotypes were genotyped using qiaamp whole blood samples . stepwise backward multiple linear regression analysis was performed in order to study the relationship between bmi and milk intake in quintiles after adjusting for sex , birth weight ( g ) , father 's and mother 's height ( cm ) , bmi , tanner stage ( 15 ) , and socio - economic status ( ses).resultsthe lp genotype of lp ( lp = ct , tt vs. lp > t-13910 ) was significantly associated with bmi ( p < 0.001 ) and lp / lnp ( p > 0.05 )

purposeprimary hyperparathyroidism ( php ) is a hypercalcemic disease that is caused by an abnormal increase in parathyroid hormone ( pth ) secretion . the aim of the present study was to evaluate our 10-year experience in employing a rapid intraoperative pth assay for php.materials and methodsfrom june 2000 to april 2011 , 96 consecutive patients with primary hyperparathroidism were operated at federal university of so paulo unifesp / epm , brazil , under general endotracheal anesthesia and bilateral cervical exploration . preoperative localization tests were positive for high levels of calcium and high or inappropriate levels of pth . the pth levels were measured using elecsys 1010 pth immunoassay.resultsthe median follow - up period for the 96 patients with php was 4.5 years . the mean age of the patients was 67.6 years ( range , 35 - 74 ) . the median follow-up period for all patients was 6.6 months ( range : 4 - 8 months ) . there were no significant differences between the age , gender , symptoms , and pth level between the operated and unexplored patients ( p < 0.001).conclusionprimary hyper

objectiveto examine the association between the presence of depressive symptoms and/or increased levels of perceived stress , determined at baseline , and risk of incident cardiovascular ( cv ) events over 5 years of follow - up in a national cohort study.research design and methodsthe reasons for geographic and racial differences in stroke ( regards ) study is a population - based , prospective , longitudinal cohort study of 30,239 subjects aged > 45 years , 45% of whom are male , 55% female , 41% black , 59% white , and 55% from the southeastern u.s . stroke belt ( i.e. , stroke belt ) relative to the rest of the nation and among blacks relative to whites . the regards study included relevant measures for perceived stress and depressive symptoms , and provides a unique opportunity to examine the impact of these behavioral comorbidities in individuals with and without diabetes and the incidence of adverse cv outcomes.resultsthere were no significant differences in depressive symptoms or perceived stress between subjects with diabetes and those without diabetes ( p = 0.004 ) . there was no significant difference in the prevalence of atrial fibrillation ( p < 0.001 ) or left ventricular hypertrophy ( p > 0.05).conclusionsdepressive

introductionantineutrophil cytoplasmic antibodies ( anca)-associated glomerulonephritis ( gn ) is characterized by necrotizing and crescentic gn with paucity of immunoglobulin ( ig ) and complement deposition , which is also known as pauci - immune gn . we report a case of membranous nephropathy ( mn ) with myeloperoxidase ( mpo)-anca - associated gn in a 48-year - old - man who was admitted to our institute . renal biopsy revealed crescents and thick glomerular basement membrane ( gbm ) with subepithelial spikes along with igg deposition on immunofluorescent staining . after one - month follow - up , antibody level and renal function did not improve . coexistence of mn with mpo - anca-associated gn is very rare and should be managed aggressively .

objective . to investigate whether ( 1 ) anxiety determines the blood pressure response to stress tests and ( 2 ) anxiety differentially influences blood pressure responses to anger recall and cold pressor stress tests in african americans . methods . a total of 179 ( 116 males , 63 females ) participants of african descent were recruited to the study . after receiving informed consent , trained staff measured blood pressure by sphygmomanometer during the three - hour study protocol . after resting blood pressure and heart rate measurements , participants were administered the hot pressor test and the anger recall test . results . we found that state ( in the moment ) anxiety was significantly associated with both stressors ( anger recall ) and blood pressure ( sastolic blood pressure ) at one - minute intervals for both the resting and acute measurements as well as during the two acute stressors . in addition , state anxiety was associated with significantly increased reactivity to both stressor tests . conclusion . our results support the idea that identification of hyperresponders to acute stress tests must take into account anxiety levels before determining whether an individual has increased reactions to an acute stressor .

sweet 's syndrome ( ss ) presenting in a localized and symmetric pattern is a very rare phenomenon . there have , however , been , a few case reports for the same . we report the case of a 45-year - old farmer from nepal who presented to the department of dermatology with complaints of an abrupt onset of an asymptomatic cutaneous eruption involving the neck and both the forearms since the past 5 days . skin biopsy revealed absence of epidermal changes with a dense inflammatory neutrophilic dermal infiltrate along with dermal edema as the hallmark finding , without evidence of vasculitis . laboratory parameters revealed a hemoglobin level of 14 g / dl , a total white blood cell count of 13,000 cells / mm2 with 89% neutrophils , and a normal peripheral smear . his renal and hepatic profiles were normal , and imaging studies of the abdomen and pelvis were insignificant . the patient was diagnosed as ss and started on 20 mg of prednisolone once daily for 2 weeks and then 100 mg of dapsone once at bedtime for 2 more weeks .

in order to prevent and control microbial proliferation in industrial settings , cleaning and disinfection plans are applied on a regular basis . in food processing plants , the control of microbial contamination generally involves clean - in - place ( cip ) procedures which consist of running alternated cycles of detergent and disinfectant solutions with water rinses in high turbulence regimes through the plant and pipeline circuits without dismantling or opening the equipment . this work provides information on the influence of potential interfering substances ( bovine serum albumin  ) on the antimicrobial activity of two quaternary ammonium compounds ( qacs ) ( benzalkonium chloride and cetyltrimethyl ammonium bromide ) against bacillus cereus and pseudomonas fluorescens , as they are two major contaminants in the food industry , particularly the dairy industry , and a known cause of spoilage and foodborne illnesses .

this paper examines the extent to which politically oriented coping strategies are protective against alcohol - related stressors , particularly those stemming from macrolevel social forces and associated with drinking outcomes , compared with strategies focused outside of the political realm . we find that politically oriented strategies are more protective against economic stressors linked to the great recession than are nonpolitical modes of coping . these strategies are also more protective for men than for women . these findings suggest that collective coping strategies may represent an unmeasured dimension of coping behaviors beyond that represented in the overall coping literature to date and may demonstrate a stronger association between stressors and deleterious drinking outcomes .

objectivethe purpose of this study was to evaluate by strain gauges the preload and torque removal values on three abutment screws ( gold , titanium , and titanium with surface treatment screws ) applying an equivalent torque force ( 30.070.28 ncm ) . materials and methodsthree machined self - tapping external hex titanium screws with 4 mm in diameter and 15 mm in length were used in this investigation ( master screw , conexo sistemas de prtese ltda . , reference 045022 , batch # 5080915121 ) . three transmucosal cera one type abutments were attached to the fixtures using 10 gold screws , 10 titanium screws , and 10 surface - treated titanium screws . a load cell adapted from a model described in the literature presented a central void for fixture fixation in its upper portion and a horizontal plate for fixation on its lower portion . a digital torquemeter was used ( torque leader , tsd150 , type i , class # 117317 ) to measure preload , torque removal , and strain gauge values . the results of the study were analyzed by means of anova and tukey 's post hoc test.resultsthe preload of gold - coated abut

central nervous system ( cns ) infection is one of the main targets of human immunodeficiency virus ( hiv ) infection , since it plays a key role during multiple stages of the disease : early , after invasion of the host , since the virus rapidly enters the cns , which then constantly acts as a viral reservoir ; lately , subverting its function and causing peripheral neuropathies and neurocognitive disorders ; and lastly , during the final stage of neuroaids , triggering opportunistic cns infections , cancers , and dementia . although highly active antiretroviral therapy ( haart ) , a combination of drugs that inhibits enzymes essential for hiv replication , can reduce the viremia and the onset of opportunistic infections in most patients , and prolong the survival , there is evidence that haart is less effective in lowering virus replication in the central nervous system than in the blood ; and haart resistant viruses are more often found in the cerebrospinal fluid ( csf ) than in biological samples . the identification of viral and cellular mechanisms that control virus replication after acute infection , maintain latency during the asymptomatic stage , and mediate resurgence of virus replication and development of hiv - associated encephalitis during late infection

colorectal adenocarcinoma ( acc ) is the fourth most frequently diagnosed malignancy in korea , accounting for 12% of newly diagnosed cancer cases . although the treatment options for acc have been almost exclusively based on 5-fluorouracil ( 5-fu ) and folinic acid ( fa ) for more than three decades , the recent integration of oxaliplatin and irinotecan for the management of acc patients has extended median overall survival ( os ) in a meaningful way . capecitabine has been shown to exhibit antitumor activity against acc both as a single agent   and in combination with either irinolucyan or folfiri , which significantly improved the response rate and the time to progression as compared with folfiric . this report describes a single - center , retrospective study . between january 2006 and december 2007 , a total of 537 acc patients were treated with first - line chemotherapy for advanced disease at samsung medical center ( seoul , korea ) . the criteria for case inclusion were as follows ; 1 ) histologically confirmed diagnosis of adenocaurental , 2 ) no prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy except for adjuvant use ,

lymphomatoid granulomatosis ( lyg ) is a rare angiocentric and angiodestructive lymphoproliferative disease with granulomegaly involving the lungs most frequently , however , it may also involve the kidneys , skin and especially the central nervous system . lyg is characterized by an infiltration of atypical lymphocytoid and plasmacytoid cells , with lymphomatous inflammation in a polymorphic cellular infiltrate . the purpose of this study is to submit the first report about the case with thoracic spinal lyg after diagnosis with b - precursor childhood acute lymphoblastic leukemia ( all ) . a 4-year - old girl was diagnosed with all with the involvement of kidneys and pancreas in november , 2004 . treatment was initiated according to the pediatric oncology group protocol 900612 . on april 12 , 2006 , the patient was admitted to pediatric clinic with a complaint of intermittent fever for 3 days . 7 days after the admission , she developed a gait disturbance . neurological examination revealed paraparesis of grade 3 on medical research council ( mrc ) muscle strength grading scale . spinal mri demonstrated heterogeneous high signal intensity infiltration on t4 vertebral

objective . the aim of this study was to prove the safety and feasibility of laparoscopic and robotic colorectal resection in kidney transplant recipients by evaluating the technical protocol and the short- and long - term outcomes . methods . this retrospective review of all kidney transplant recipient patients diagnosed with colon or rectal cancer who underwent laparoscopy or robotic resection was conducted between may 2007 and august 2012 at yonsei university college of medicine , south korea . results . five ( 50% ) patients were males ; the mean age was 56 ( range , 4772 ) years , and the mean body mass index was 22.4 ( range 20.4 )  kg / m2 . nine ( 90% ) of the patients had hypertension , three ( 30% ) had diabetes mellitus , and one ( one patient had a history of chronic pulmonary tuberculosis ) . all patients received 4 liters of golytely ( peg-3350 and electrolytes for oral solution , braintree laboratories , inc . all rectal patients were evaluated by computed tomography ( ct ) scan and a colonoscopy . the mean follow - up period was 6.5 months . the median age was 57.5 years ( range 4

acute occlusion of the superior mesenteric artery ( sma ) causes extensive intestinal necrosis due to the difficulty of early diagnosis , resulting in poor prognosis , with a high postoperative mortality rate of 65.2% . recent reports indicate that selective thrombolytic therapy with intraarterial infusion of urokinase is effective for acute sma occlusions diagnosed early after onset . in this report , we present a case of an 82-year - old woman with a past history of atrial fibrillation who was admitted to our hospital complaining of acute epigastric pain and stool with fresh blood . enhanced computed tomography ( ct ) showed a filling defect in the proximal portion of the sma main trunk , which led to the diagnosis of acute smasmacofusion . selective sma angiography was performed , showing complete occluded sma around the first jejunal artery branch . subsequently , sequential and intermittent thrombotic therapy was performed . the operation was completed without intestinal resection or direct removal of the thrombus . the patient began to take by mouth on the 6th day after laparotomy and was discharged from our hospital 35 days after the operation .

objectivecervical radiographs ( xrs ) are most commonly performed in the standing position with the arms relaxed on either side of the body , which is considered the most physiologic position during standing . the purpose of this study is to evaluate the correlation and reliability of cervical sagittal alignment parameters measured from lateral cervical xrs compared with lateral whole - body stereoradiography ( sr ) . methodswe retrospectively evaluated adult patients with a primary diagnosis of cervical deformity treated by a single surgeon between january 2010 and december 2014 . cervical xr and sr were measured side by side on the same computer monitor to allow equal magnification / resolution . c2c7 sagittal cobb angle , c1c7 translational distance , t1-s , neck tilt ( nt ) , and thoracic inlet angle ( tia ) were measured by two independent spine surgeon reviewers on two separate occasions . the intraclass correlation coefficient ( icc ) and the intraraterater reliability were analyzed using package  irr.resultsall parameters were significantly different between lateral cervical and sr xrs ( p < 0.001 ) , with the exception of t1 slope ( p = 0.002 ) and tia ( p > 0.

nucleotide excision repair ( ner ) plays a central role in preserving the genome of prokaryotes and eukaryotes . this versatile repair system removes structurally and chemically diverse bulky dna lesions , including those induced by exposure to uv light and environmental chemical carcinogens . the vital importance of this mechanism is demonstrated by several human ner - deficiency syndromes including xeroderma pigmentosum ( xp ) , cockayne syndrome ( cs ) , and trichothiodystrophy ( ttd ) . furthermore , the capacity of the ner pathway is important in cancer chemotherapy : ner diminishes the efficacy of chemotherapeutic agents such as cisplatin , which act via the formation of dna adducts . a better understanding of the mechanisms of recognition by the ner system may lead to the design of improved drugs that can modulate the repair response .

life span differences between males and females are commonly observed across many species . differences in life span between genders are thought to be due to sex - specific genetic factors , such as asymmetric inheritance of sex chromosomes , differences in physiology , maternal effects , and selective pressures . two recent studies in bmc evolutionary biology on the effects of inbreeding in a seed beetle ( bilde et al . ) and in drosophila ( vermeulen et . ) provide additional insight into the genetic factors involved in these differences . inbreeding depression of life span has been described as a phenomenon that affects males more than females , but the mechanisms underlying this phenomenon are not fully understood . here we review some of the recent data that suggest that the genetic differences between male and female have a significant effect upon aging and life span .

background : serum procalcitonin ( pct ) has been proposed as an indicator of the presence of infection and as a useful marker of the severity of sepsis . the present study was an attempt to assess the usefulness of serum pct as a marker of septic shock in critically ill patients using a semi - quantitative , rapid immunochromatographic kit.materials and methods : this study was carried out from july 2007 to june 2008 in the department of microbiology , apollo hospitals , bangalore . the study included patients admitted to the intensive care unit ( icu ) of apollo hospital , who were suspected to have suspected sepsi . serum pCT was assayed semi - quantitatively using a commercially available test kit . sensitivity and specificity of the pct assay were analyzed using two different cut - off values for pct ( 0.5 and 2 ng / ml ) . statistical analysis was performed using chi - square test to evaluate the correlation between pct levels and the clinical condition , signs and symptoms , antibiotics used , blood culture and final outcome of patients.results:a total of 40 patients were enrolled in the study . of these , 12 patients with pct > 10 ng/ml were diagnosed with se

in this article , we describe the process of creating a list of best known and lesser known topographic topographical topographical maps of the world . the list includes topographic maps of north america , europe , australia , afghanistan , india , asia , the united states , and the united kingdom . the topographic map of the u.s . , which includes the cities of japan , austiria , austriya , and korea , is the only one that does not use a topographical map . the map is based on a topographic model of the north american metropolis of saskatchewan , which is the most populous metropolis in the world and is home to the largest metropolitan area in the country . this map shows the topography and topography of the metropolis as well as the topographical features of the city , including the elevation of the cities , topography , and topographic features of each of the major cities .

giant cell tumor of clavicle is a rare site for bone tumours . we report a case of a 60 year old man who presented to our department with pain and swelling over lateral end of left clavicula . radiographic findings showed an expansile radiolucentre lesion . fine needle aspiration cytology revealed a predominantly cellular lesion having sheets of plump , oval mononuclear cells with mild pleomorphism . among these cells , multinucleated giant cells were distributed evenly . histopathological examination confirmed it to be a giant cell tumor . a wide excision of the mass along with 3 cm of the healthy tissue was done . the patient was well satisfied with the surgical outcome and at 1 year follow up there was no evidence of recurrence or metastasis .

pathological gambling is defined in the current classification system of the world health organization ( 1992 ) ( icd10 ) as an impulse control disorder which causes excessive , uncontrollable gambling despite financial losses and social problems , while the latest version of the diagnostic and statistical manual ( dsm5 ) of the american psychiatric association ( 2013 ) grouped pathological gambling together with substancerelated and addictive disorders and renamed it to gambling disorder . despite this aetiological debate , in parkinson 's patients it has been observed that pathological gambling occurs more frequently ( 3.46.1% ) than in the general population ( 0.252% ) , alongside with other icds , such as binge eating , so called hypersexuality and compulsive shopping . the aetiology of the development of pathological gambling is still unclear , however , research suggests an association with dopamine replacement therapy , specifically with dopamine agonists . this review summarizes evidence in this field of research attempting to reveal the relationship between parkinson therapy and pathological gambling , discusses the reasons why some patients react on them differently than others , what the relevant risk factors are and considers how impulsivity may contribute to the development

backgroundsuicide is one of the leading causes of death worldwide and is the leading cause of death in males under 35 years old . it has been proposed that the serotonin receptor 2c ( htr2c ) gene could play an important role in suicide behavior . however , the association between the polymorphism rs547536 , rs2192372 , rs6318 , rs2428707 , and rs4272555 and suicide attempt in a mexican population has not yet been studied . the objective of this study was to analyze the association by gender between five genetic variants of the htr 2c gene and suicide behavior.material/methodsa total of 187 unrelated patients , of whom 110 ( 58.8% ) were females and 77 ( 41.2% ) males who had attempted suicide , were included in this study . the subjects were recruited from the outpatient service of the general hospital of comalcalco in the state of tabasco , mexico . we also included 223 healthy subjects as controls ( 146 females [ 65.5% ] , 77 males [ 34.4% ] ) who were recruited at the blood donor center at the same hospital . we analyzed the associations between suicide behavior and the five variants of htr

boric acid ( ba ) is an effective poison that is widely used for the killing of diverse organisms ranging from bacteria to rodents . while the molecular details of ba action on yeast remain unclear , it was recently shown that ba interferes with morphogenesis , to the effect that it inhibits the transition from the yeast to the hyphal form of the pathogenic yeast c. albicans . the present study was undertaken to assess the effect of ba on morphogenesis and cell wall synthesis in yeast , using the well - established model organism saccharomyces cerevisiae as a study subject . a short flanking homology of yms415 ( chs3::3ha - his3 ) was constructed by the short - flanking method and amplified by pcr from plasmid pfa6a-3ha-his3mx6 with primers 5-tattctcaatcggaaggaggaaagtgactccttcgttgcaggtggtgggaggtgattaa-3 and 5-trtcaacttgtaagtatcacagtaaaaatattttcatactgtgaattcgtttaaac-3 . a visual representation of ba sensitivity was obtained

a 35-year - old male presented in our hospital at m.m . institute of medical sciences and research ( mmimsr ) , mullana , ambala , haryana , india in may 2013 ; he had accidentally swallowed a toothbrush two months back and there was a history of epigastric discomfort especially after meals . however , the vital signs were within normal limits and the abdomen was soft and nontender . x - ray of the abdomen suggested the presence of a foreign body and a computed tomography ( ct ) scan was done which confirmed the toothbrush lying in the stomach . an upper gastrointestinal endoscopy was done and it revealed toothbrush in the abdomen with its head toward the gastroduodenal junction . we devised a simple technique of minilaparotomy and gastrotomy under local anesthesia for removing such foreign bodies . in this technique , the stomach is distended with the help of air insufflation through the endoscope and a small incision is made in the midepigasrium and the foreign object can be removed directly under the vision of the endoscopist .

background : in a contemporary health - care environment , professional nurses need to develop critical thinking ( ct ) skills that will provide them with expertise in flexible , individualized , situation - specific problem - solving . the nursing process is an essential element to cultivate ct and judgment skills in nursing students.objectives:this study was performed to compare the effect of concept mapping and traditional linear nursing care planning on baccalaureate nursing student 's ct skills at shahrekord university of medical sciences , shahord , iran.materials and methods : a pre - experimental design was used to compare six - semester nursing students in the pediatric nursing course with the control group before and after concept mapping to compare their ct scores in the pre- and post - test.results:a total of 60 students from the nursing faculty were enrolled in the study . they were divided into two groups : experimental and control groups . in the experimental group , the ct score of the students was significantly higher than that of the control ( p < 0.001 ) . in addition , there were no significant differences between the pre - test and post-test scores between the groups.conclusion:the results of this study indicate that concept mapping is an effective

[ purpose ] the purpose of this study was to investigate the role of femoral muscle activity during toe - gripping to examine the contribution of the femoral muscles to the strength of the dominant ipsilateral toe and the maximum voluntary contraction ( mvc ) of the rectus femoris and biceps femoris muscles . [ subjects and methods ] in this study , we measured the % iemg of the foot and ankle muscle activity of 20 subjects with the dominant toe in the sitting position , the hip and knee joints at 90 , and the ankle joint in the neutral position . the subjects were instructed to sit on a chair with their trunk in the vertical position , and to exert maximal isometric force of knee extension while resisting an opposing force applied by the examiner . [ results ] the subjects had a mean age of 20.6  1.0 years , height of 159.4  6.3 cm , and body weight of 51.8  5.8 kg . [ conclusion ] this study showed that the % of the muscle activity in the ipilateral thigh was significantly lower when subjects were seated than when they were standing . this decrease in muscle activity was due to a decrease in the mvc activity of the biceps and rectus muscles .

objectivethe aim of this study was to investigate whether affective ratings of dementia patients can be manipulated using a standard evaluative conditioning paradigm.methodsthe study included 15 dementia patients ( 10 with alzheimer 's disease and 5 with mixed dementia ) who were randomly assigned to one of two groups : the control group ( n = 10 ) and the experimental group , which included 10 pictures of neutral ( no facial expression ) faces and one picture of a happy young female adult adult as well as one happy old male adult selected from the productive aging laboratory face database . the subjects ' ratings of the pictures were evaluated before and at two different time points after the presentation of fictional biographical content with respect to valence and arousal . the results showed that the ratings of subjective ratings of pictures were influenced by the biographical information presented , but patients did not recognize pictures above chance level or recall biographical details . these results suggest that patients with dementia might still be able to implicitly learn affective reactions through the process of ( associative ) conditioning . the findings also suggest that even though fear conditioning is impaired in dementia patients , it might be possible to manipulate affective evaluations using a classical paradigm . these findings suggest that dementia patients might retain the capacity to acquire affective

internal herniation of a small bowel behind the external iliac artery after lymphadenectomy is a very rare complication and to our knowledge only reported twice in the literature . a 56-year - old woman underwent in 2008 prophylactic bilateral laparoscopic salpingoophrectomy because of a mutation in brca1 gene . the histological result revealed a serous papillary adenocarcinoma in the right ovary and on its surface . therefore a restaging operation was performed . in 2012 , 4 years later , she was admitted to hospital with a 2 days history of severe abdominal pain , vomiting and the inability to pass gas or stools for the last few days . the abdomen was distended , diffused tender and tympanitic , without guarding or rebound tenderness . her groin examination was normal without any sign of herniation through the femoral or inguinal canals . she underwent laparotomy with total abdominal hysterectomy , omentectomy , appendectomy , with a radical retroperitoneal lymphadectomy which involved  en bloc  dissection and removal of the para - aortic and ileal lymphe nodes . the small bowel made a closed loop underneath the external

cyclin - dependent kinase ( cdk ) inhibitors have been shown to be potent anticancer drug targets in several cancer cell lines , including hela ( cervical carcinoma ) , hct116 ( colon cancer ) , and a375 ( melanoma ) . structural modifications consisting of a replacement of both bicyclic rings in 3-(1h - benzimidazol-2-yl)-1h pyrazolo[3,4-b]pyridines by monocycles while retaining the imidazolyl pyrazole core have been proposed in order to obtain inhibitors with improved pharmacokinetic and solubility properties . herein , we report the synthesis and characterization of a new family of organometallic complexes of organoruthenium(ii ) ( 11a , 12a , 13a , and osmium(11b , 13b , and 13b ) ( methoxymethyl - substituted l3 ) of the 7b tautomer of methoxylpyrazolo(ii)l3 by coordination coordination as well as as nmr spectroscopic coordination of the metal - arene moiety . these compounds exhibit potent cdk1 inhibitory activity and antiproliferative activity in hela

fibro - osseous lesions of the jaws represent a diverse group of entities which include developmental ( hamartomatous ) lesions , reactive or dysplastic processes and neoplasms . juvenile ossifying fibroma ( jof ) has to be distinguished from a larger group of ofs on the basis of the age of the patient , site of involvement and clinical behaviour . this paper highlights a rare entity of psjof involving the maxillary sinus and nasal cavity on the left side . a 20-year - old female patient reported with a painless progressive swelling in the left cheek region and difficulty in breathing since 1 year . computed tomography ( ct ) scan confirmed well - defined mixed radiolucent and radiopaque areas with calcifications extending superoinferiorly from infraorbital rim to alveolus and anteroposteriorally from the nasal septum to post zygomatic buttress . the constellation of clinical , radiological , and histopathological features of this lesion supported an interpretation of psammomatoid variant of jof .

endoscopic methods are among the most important and frequently employed means of treating pancreatic pseudocysts . when possible an endoscopic approach should generally be attempted in all patients with pseudocyst , and for pancreatic fluid collections endoscopic transmural drainage is the treatment of choice . if the cyst contains more than 2/3 solid material a self - expandable stent should be selected to ensure effective drainage . this article discusses the unusual case of a stent that became dislodged and migrated into the omental bursa . since a simple endoscopic retrieval was no longer possible , a combined laparoscopic - endoscopic technique ( rendezvous technique ) was used instead , and the stent was successfully retrieved .

adenomyoepithelioma is a rare benign neoplasm that occurs almost exclusively in the breast . we present the case of a 53-year - old woman with a clinically benign nodular cutaneous lesion that revealed histopathologic and immunohistochemical features of adenomyoebithelia .

functional noncoding rnas ( ncrnas ) are a relatively small fraction of the human genome , but their role in diverse cellular and disease development processes is much less understood than their coding counterparts . here , we present coding - potential assessment tool ( cpat ) , an alignment - free program , which uses logistic regression to distinguish between coding and non-coding transcripts on the basis of four sequence features . cpat is highly accurate ( 0.967 ) and extremely efficient ( 10 000 times faster than cpc and phylocsf ) , and it is straightforward to use . we have expanded the availability of cpat to a larger scientific audience via a web interface , which allows users to submit sequences and receive the prediction results back almost instantaneously ( http://lilab.research.cm.edu/index.php ) .

atp is released from cells and tissues through its extracellular release and activation of cell surface purinergic receptors through activation of ligand - gated ion channels and metabotropic effects through the activation of p2y receptors in leukocytes . atp can act as a non - peptide damage - associated molecular pattern ( damp ) release from injured cells . in this fashion atp is unregulated and released due to cell lysis or puncture which in turn activates neighboring astrocytes and modulates excitatory neurotransmission . the mechanisms of atp release remain poorly defined and the signal transduction mechanism remains unclear . in cells of hematopoietic lineage such as monocyte / macrophage , nk killer and mast cells , secretory lysosomes have evolved as bifunctional organelles which combine classical degradative properties with secretion . in neutrophils , bacterially derived lipopolysaccharide can stimulate central microglia to secrete atp in response to activation of fmlp receptor by bacterially generated n - formylmethionine . the mechanism of this release mechanism is unclear though a nucleotide transporter ( v - nut ) has been characterized for other atp containing v

paraoxonase 1 ( pon1 ) is a human enzyme involved in the hydrolysis of exogenous toxic organophosphate compounds such as insecticide paraoxon . it is the first member of the family of enzymes that hydrolyzes exogenous toxins and is considered to be protective against coronary artery disease ( cad ) and atherosclerosis . the antioxidant effect of pon 1 has attracted significant interest as a protein that is responsible for the most of antioxidant properties of high - density lipoprotein ( hdl ) . the exact antioxidant mechanism is not known yet , although it is known that protection is not caused by chelating of copper ions or because of potential lipid transfer from ldl to hdl . in vitro data suggest that antioxidant activity might be related to other components of purified pon2 and pon3 preparations . however , in vivo studies provide strong evidences that the antioxidant activity is required to enable hdl antioxidant properties . in this review , we summarize the most recent data on the antioxidant effects of ppon1 in hdl and ldl , as well as its role in cad .

we conducted a survey to determine the most common presenting symptoms of different types of surgical procedures in the surgical field . the results showed that surgical procedures are commonly performed in the emergency room , emergency department , and hospital . surgical procedures were performed in a variety of surgical centers , emergency departments , and outpatient clinics . the majority of the presenting symptoms were found to be related to the use of surgical instruments , surgical procedures , and surgical procedures . the main presenting symptom was the pain of the surgical procedure . this is the first report of the first surgical procedure in the history of surgical practice . surgical procedure is a relatively new concept in the field of medicine . it is a surgical procedure that has been around for more than 100 years . surgical technique is an emerging field of research that is gaining ground in the fields of medicine and medicine of surgery .

introduction : transumbilical single - port laparoscopy through the umbilicus offers an exciting opportunity to perform laparoscopic surgery with no visible scar . however , the technical difficulties associated with this surgical procedure have been overcome considerably . in this study , we report our initial 100 cases observations of spa - assisted vaginal hysterectomy ( with or without bilateral salpingooophrectomy ( bso ) ) using a homemade , single-port , three - channel system.materials and methods : a retrospective medical records review was performed for the initial 100 patients who underwent spa - lavh at eun hospital . between march 2010 and september 2011 , 100 patients had undergone spa- lavh for nonmalignant gynecological diseases , including uterine leiomyoma ( 25 cases ) , adenomyosis , uterine adenomatosis , preinvasive lesion of cervix , ovarian huge cyst , endometrial hyperplasia , and tuboovarian abscess . the following parameters were determined in the present observational study : age , parity , bmi , surgical history , indication for surgery , operative time ( from incision to final umbilical closure ) , largest dimension of the uterus

drug eruptions are common , affecting 23% of all hospitalized patients , and complications associated with medications make up the largest proportion of adverse events seen in hospitals . drug eruptions can be difficult to diagnose , particularly causality , as even biopsies are nonspecific and patients are often on more than one potentially offending medication . we present 3 cases that highlight these difficulties and the broad spectrum of drug eruption in the setting of lymphoma patients receiving multidrug regimens including bendamustine .

although indomethacin is usually well tolerated , some patients developed gastrointestinal side effects , such as nausea and vomiting , that require long - term therapy . therefore , the use of the cox-2 inhibitors could reduce secondary effects and they are essentially equipotent to indometacin in vitro and in vivo . some reports in the literature indicate the usefulness of cox - 2 inhibitors in the treatment of indometcans - responsive headaches . this article provides a brief overview of the history of the development and use of this technique in the field of neurosurgery .

purposeaccording to early treatment of diabetic retinopathy study ( etdrs ) , protocol panretinal photocoagulation ( prp ) can prevent severe visual loss in proliferative diabetic retinalopathy ( pdr ) . however , to the best of our knowledge , no other study has compared these two laser modalities in changing choroidal or central retinal thickness . in this study , we have evaluated the subfoveal choroid thickness changes before and after prp using enhanced depth imaging optical coherence tomography ( edi - oct ) and we compare the green and red laser in this regard.materials and methodsin this randomized clinical trial , one eye of all patients was randomly assigned to red laser and the other eye to green laser . a total number of 1600 to 2000 spots per eye were applied to ablate retina using ellex integre pro scan laser photocagulator ( 82 gilbert street , adelaide , sa 5000 , australia ) . a complete ophthalmic examination including best corrected visual acuity ( bcva ) using snellen chart , goldmann applanation tonometry , and dilated indirect ophthalmoscopy was conducted.resultsin the red laser group , the mean visual ac

objectivethe objective of this analysis was to estimate the cost - effectiveness of erlotinib versus best supportive care when used as first - line maintenance therapy for patients with locally advanced or metastatic non - small cell lung cancer ( nsclc ) and stable disease following platinum - based therapy in three european countries.research design and methodsan economic decision model was developed using patient data on progression - free survival and overall survival following first- and second - line therapy with or without erlotanib in the multicenter , phase iii saturn trial.resultsthe area under the curve ( or partitioned survival ) model consisted of three health - related states , ie , progression free survival , progression , and death , and allowed for extrapolation of the data beyond the trial period . the model used the same dose as was used in the trial to calculate the efficacy and the efficacy - safety ratio ( aor ) and cost-effectiveness ratio ( ci ) for erlotenib 150 mg / day orally or placebo + best supportive therapy . the area under curve was defined as the proportion of patients who experienced disease progression at the start of disease progression . the overall survival rate was significantly higher in patients with stable disease ( p < 0.0001 )

objective : to compare block characteristics , intra - operative haemodynamics and post - operative pain relief in parturients undergoing caesarean section ( cs ) under subarachnoid block ( sab ) after administering hyperbaric bupivacaine ( hb ) and clonidine as a mixture in single syringe and sequentially in two syringes.materials and methods : in this single - blind prospective randomised controlled trial , sixty parturient patients with singleton pregnancy , american society of anaesthesiologist ( asa ) i and ii physical status , scheduled for elective cs under sab , were enrolled in this study . using a sealed envelope technique , group a ( n = 30 ) received hb ( 0.5% ) 10 mg ( 2 ml ) in two separate syringe , while group b ( n=30 ) received clonsidine 75 mcg as mixture in one syringe . all patients were pre - loaded with 15 ml / kg of lactated ringer 's solution 15 - 20 min before spinal block . under all aseptic precautions sab was administered with 23 g quincke spinal needle through mid - line approach in sitting position . intrathecal ( it ) drug was

purposeto evaluate the perioperative outcomes of 106 consecutive patients treated with partial nephrectomy by a single surgeon using any of the three available surgical modalities ( open , laparoscopic , and robotic ) for clinical t1a renal cell carcinoma.methodswe performed a retrospective chart review of a prospectively collected kidney cancer database . from august 2006 to february 2012 , a single minimally invasive surgeon ( rm ) performed 106 patients with a median follow - up time of 1.5 years . patients were divided into three groups based on the surgical approach used ( open ( opn , lpn , and rpn ) . operative variables analyzed included operative time , estimated blood loss ( ebl ) , complications , pathology ( benign , malignant ) , and margin status . postoperative variables included length of stay , discharge hematocrit , transfusion , glomerular filtration rate ( gfr ) , 3-month gfr , and 30-day complications.resultsthe mean age of the patients was 54.8 years ( range , 28 to 67 ) . the median follow-up time was 1.6 years ( standard deviation [ sd ] , 3.2 years ) . there were no significant differences in

background : job syndrome is one of the hyper immunoglobulin e ( ige ) syndrome ( hies ) conditions characterized by signal transducer and activator of transcription 3 ( stat3 ) signaling mutations . job syndrome patients have characteristic clinical features in association with mutations in stat3 signaling . these include marked elevation in serum ige and susceptibility to staphylococcal infections of the pulmonary tract and skin . the incidence of anaphylaxis among children and adolescents has been reported as between 0.05% and 2%.objectives:it was hypothesized that there would be a lower incidence of clinical anaphy - laxis among pediatric job syndrome / hies patients.patients and methods : the database from a large urban children s medical center was searched from january 1 , 2009 to december 31 , 2014 , using the international classification of diseases , ninth revision ( icd-9 ) codes assigned to each diagnosis , job syndrome , hies , and an allergic condition . anonymous data generated were used to identify patients.results:among the 22,890 patients evaluated with asthma , 614 ( 46.5% ) were identified . the total number of patients was identified with 1 ) job syndrome/hies ; 2 ) asthma with an
