leishmania parasites are heteroxenous kinetoplastid protozoa , which undergo complete differentiation upon a cycle of proliferation / differentiation in the midgut of phlebotomine sand flies followed by the transmission of infective metacyclic promastigotes to mammalian hosts during the insect blood meal . once infecting mammalian hosts , these organisms become obligate intracellular parasites , residing and proliferating inside phagolysosomes of mononuclear phagocytic cells as amastigote forms . in humans , leishmaniasis parasites can cause a broad spectrum of clinical manifestations from mild , self - resolving skin diseases to potentially fatal , disseminated visceral diseases . the outcome of the infection is dependent on multiple , interdependent factors , such as vector species , parasite species and strain , genetic background , and immunological status of the host . in addition , the participation of factors associated with the parasite has been shown by some authors . in this context , the exhibition of markers of apoptosis by the parasite could be a contributing factor during host - parasite interactions as a possible immunosuppressive mechanism of dcl .

background : despite of presentation of medical ethics as a new science in academic teaching , the ethical concepts have been alongside the medicine , and its antiquity back to the medicine history . patient rights are the very expectations he has from the health care services and must encompass his physical , mental , spiritual and social needs which are manifested as criteria , standards , rules and laws.objectives:the aim of this study was to determine the nurses level of knowledge about patients rights in one of the teaching hospitals of tehran city.materials and methods : this was a descriptive cross - sectional study in which the nurses knowledge about patient rights was determined . the study samples consisted of 156 nurses who have been selected randomly randomly from the teaching teaching hospitals . the data collection instrument was a two - part questionnaire which included the demographic information ( age , gender , marital status , work experience , educational level , etc . ) ; the second part consisted of 10 questions according to the 10th section of the patients rights charter of iran ( 10th ) . this part determined the following areas about the patient rights : right to receive essential information about health care providers , rate of tariff , target insurance coverage , if sent to the other medical centers .                  right to have respectful and quick treatment and

objectivesthe aim of this study was to determine how physical ( weight , volume , and density ) and morphological ( mesiodistal ( md ) and buccolingual ( bl ) dimensions ) properties affect the fracture resistance of roots , and which criteria are important for standardization in fracture resistance evaluated studies.materials and methodsseventy-five human canine teeth extracted for periodontal reasons with completed apices and similar lengths were used in this study . the root canal shaping procedures were performed with protaper universal rotary files ( dentsply maillefer , ballaigues , switzerland ) , and apical region was prepared to size # 30 ( f3 ) in all specimens . the specimens were decoronated with a diamond disc under a water coolant to obtain a standardized root length of 16 mm . md and bl dimensions at 16 mm ( the coronal end of the root ) from the apex of the each root were recorded with root number . a size 10 k - file was inserted into the canal until it was visible at the apical foramen , and the working length was determined to be 1 mm short of this position . a universal testing machine was loaded with vertical force at a speed of 1 mm /

in this article , we present a selection of the most recent works of artworks that have been published in the english language in the past few years . the work includes works by the likes of art directors , artists , and musicians . we also present some of the best - known pieces of art that have never been published before . in addition , we provide a brief overview of the current state of the art in art in the field of art and design .

a 33 year old healthy male of afro - caribbean origin presented to the emergency department with acute generalised abdominal pain of three hours duration . on examination he had a heart rate of 110 beats per minute , temperature of 37.2c , stable blood pressure but he was cold and clammy . rectal exam revealed an empty rectum and no palpable masses . blood tests revealed a neutrophilia of 25,000 but urea and electrolytes were within normal range . he was resuscitated with intravenous fluids and an emergency laparotomy was performed . during this procedure , a 0.5 cm perforation was noted in the ileum . histological examination of the resected specimen from the first laporotomy revealed mesenteric fibromatosis ( desmoid tumour ) incompletely excised . although estrogen receptor status was not reported , the patient was commenced on oral tamoxifen , 20 mg once a day . the pain eventually resolved and after making a good recovery .

objectivethe aim of this study was to evaluate the efficacy of selective arteriography in the management of recurrent arterial bleeding after liver dissection ( ldlt ) in patients with hepatitis b - related liver cirrhosis and fulminant hepatitis.materials and methodsbetween april 1999 and march 2001 , 32 ( 26 men and 6 women ; age range , 7 - 62 years ; mean age , 43 years ) of 195 patients who had undergone ldl underwent 42 sessions of conventional arterial imaging in search of bleeding foci of arterial origin within 6 months of the operation . the underlying causes of hepatic failure were hepatitis b-related liver failure , fulminants hepatitis due to wilson 's disease , and biliary atresia . in all patients , the bleeding occurred within two months ( mean , 14 days ; range , 1 - 56 days ) after the operation.resultsthere were no significant differences in the number of foci found on the arteriographies of the same patient . however , the foci were different in each of the sessions conducted for the same patients . in the patients with abnormally increased drainage of fresh blood through the jackson - pratt ( jp ) tube , several specific arteries were selected

fibrodysplasia ossificans progressiva ( fop ) is a rare genetic disoder characterized by bone formation within muscles tendons and ligaments . we hereby report a case of fop in a four year old male child from a tribal family in orissa . the diagnosis was delayed due as the parents tried all sorts of indigenous medications and even manipulations by traditional bonesetters whose influence has been deep rooted in minds of the tribal population in this part of the country .

introduction : delivery of exogenous nucleic acids into cells ( transfection ) holds great promise for disease prevention and therapy ( aka : gene therapy ) . however , the selection of appropriate dna carriers is a common problem for gene therapy , and better gene delivery agents are still being sought . although viral vectors are efficient carriers , they have also often been shown to provoke undesirable immune responses , thus limiting their usefulness . additionally , the cytocompatibility of nonviral vectors must be well understood before they can be further optimized and become a viable option for gene transfer.materials and methods : herein we report a new class of water - soluble c60 vectors prepared using the hirschbingel reaction which is one of the simplest , highest - yielding , and most versatile c60 functionalization methods known.results:the structurally derivatized c60 vector reagents reported herein were synthesized to yield either positively charged , negatively charged , or neutral chemical structures when solvated under physiological conditions . these vectors offer the opportunity to elucidate the roles that hydrophobicity , hydrophilicity ( due to the water soluble substituents ) , and overall charge state displayed by the c60 derivatives have on dna transfections , gene expression ,

objective . neutral endopeptidase ( nep ) is a vasoactive peptide that degrades many vaso- and neuroactive peptides including natriuretic peptides , adrenomedullin , bradykinin , and calcitonin - gene - related peptides . nephrotic nerve conduction velocity and thermal response latency were measured in the hindpaw of c57bl/6j mice and mice deficient in nep treated with ilepatril , enalapril , angiotensin - converting enzyme ( ace ) inhibitor , or candoxatril , an inhibitor of nep . we examined the effect of diet - induced obesity on peripheral nerve dysfunction associated with obesity . methods . mice were divided into five groups : control , high - fat diet ( 24 gm% fat ) , high-fat diet ( 4.25 gm ) , nephropathy ( 500 mg / kg in the diet ) , ilePatril , ace inhibitor , and a nephrophosphatril ( 30 mg/kg ) . c57 bl/6jj and nephritic hindpaws of mice treated with nephthyroidal nephritis ( neph ) or nephrocalcin (

tetracyclines are widely used in the treatment of bacterial infections and are effective against gram - positive and gram - negative bacteria . resistance to these agents is often mediated through the acquisition of dna encoding proteins that confer resistance by one of three main mechanisms : atp - dependent efflux , enzymatic inactivation or ribosomal protection . to date , a total of 60 different classes of resistance genes have been identified , of which 12 have been designated as tet(m ) . however , a subgroup of rpp genes has been identified that consist of regions of different , already characterized ribosome protection proteins ( rpps ) that appear to have undergone recombination forming a mosaic gene . this mosaic gene , tet(c ) , has been shown to confer resistance to penicillin at levels comparable to those of non - mosaic tet(a ) . the new guideline for determining a new class of mosaic resistance gene is tet(u ) . in this article , we review the current status of this subgroup and discuss its potential role in the development of new antibiotics .

follicular thyroid cancer ( ftc ) metastasizes most commonly to the lungs and non - cranial bones , but skull and skin are uncommon sites and usually manifest well after the diagnosis of primary malignancy . metastasis to the skull as the presenting feature of ftc is infrequently reported in the literature . we report a case of a 65-year - old woman with a history of thyroid nodule who presented to dermatology clinic with the complaint of rapidly growing skull nodules . on physical examination , she had two non - tender gray cystic lesions one on her left temporal region and the other on the right parietal region . on magnetic resonance imaging of the brain , two large extra - axial enhancing masses centered in the posterior and left anterior lateral calvarium with intracranial and extracranial extensions but without any vasogenic edema or mass effect on the brain . positron emission tomography ( pet ) of the skull lesions demonstrated the two skull lesions to be lytic . two foci ofmetabolic activity were also seen in the thyroid gland in the left lobe and isthmus . an immunohistochemical panel showed positive staining for ck7 , thyroglobulin , and hbme

purposethe purpose of this study was to investigate whether metal interferes with the surface hardness and roughness of a visible light - polymerized acrylic resin and to compare these values to those of water - bath- and microwave - polymerization resins.materials and methodstwelve disc - shaped specimens , 30 mm in diameter and 4 mm thick , were fabricated for each resin according to the manufacturers ' directions . all specimens were made using a silicone ( optosil , heraeus kulzer , hanau , germany ) matrix ( 30  0.05 mm ) . the matrix was flasked in type iii dental stone ( herodent soli - rock ; vigodent , rio de janeiro , rj , brazil ) using standard metal dental flasks ( uraby ; dlc , so paulo , sr brazil).the resin samples were polymerized in a microwave oven ( continental aw-42 , with 2,450 hz frequency and 900w maximum potency ; bosch , manaus , am ; bos ch , braz , baul , bergdorf , usa ) at 360 wt / wt and at 8 min at 360 and 4 min at 4 and

background : there is limited data upon skin cancer screening program in western asia region and especially iran . therefore , this study was planned as a pilot one - week screening campaign in tehran , iran to evaluate its profit and failure and design further large - scale screening program more definitely.materials and methods : the participants of this study were invited to the public health centers of shahid beheshti medical university in a one-week period for complete skin examination in october 2008 . an illustrated educational pamphlet in the case of the risk factors especially sun exposure , ways of prevention and early detection of skin cancer published and distributed in the health centers for general patient education . a board certified dermatologist performed the whole - body skin examination for the referred patients and further evaluation particularly skin biopsy was done whenever needed.results:out of 1314 patients , 194 males ( 14.8% ) and 1.120 females ( 85.2% ) , with age of 51.28.81 years were attended the allied health centers and underwent the whole-body skin examination and 2 - 3 minute visual inspection of the skin . the most common skin lesions were basal cell carcinoma ( bcc ) and squamous cell carcinomas ( scc ) .

objectivethe aim of this study was to compare the effects of clopidogrel in combination with prostacycline analog as an arterial vasodilator and additional antiaggregant drug on venous distensibility and arterial elasticity after autogenous arteriovenous fistula ( avf ) creation in hemodialysis patients with diabetes mellitus.materials and methodsthe study group consisted of 96 patients with diabetes mellitus who underwent autogenous avf creation in a single center . eligible participants were randomly assigned to receive clop idogrel ( 75 mg daily ) or an oral prostacyclerine analog ( 200 mg orally daily ) 710 days prior to the surgery and continued postoperatively . venous quality and diameter were measured preoperatively using doppler ultrasonography ( usg ) and spectral usg , respectively.resultsthe mean age of the patients in the control and study groups was 67.5 and 67.6 years , respectively . the mean arterial diameter was significantly smaller in the study group ( p < 0.001 ) . the median arterial diastolic velocity was 70 cm / s / s in both groups ( p = 0.002 ) . ven

tension - free vaginal tape ( tvt ) is now the treatment of choice for female stress urinary incontinence because of its ease of use , minimal invasiveness , and high success rate . this study was conducted to determine the efficacy and complication associated with transurethral removal ( tur ) of the intravesral or intraurethroral mesh following surgical intervention . the aim of the study was to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of surgical intervention in the management of stress urinary tract infection . the study included the use of a combination of surgical and non - surgical methods to reduce the risk of urinary tract infections and urinary tract obstruction . the main outcome of this study is the reduction of the risk to the patient by reducing the incidence of urinary infections , urinary tract obstructions , and the reduction in the number of urinary incidences . the second outcome is the decrease in urinary incestion and urinary incision . the third outcome is a reduction of urinary incidence , urinary incisions and urinary excitation . the fourth outcome was a reduction in urine incision and urinary incidence . the fifth outcome was an increase in urinary incidence and reduction in urinary excision . in this study , we found that surgical intervention was the most effective method to reduce urinary incission and

generalized anxiety disorder ( gad ) is one of the most prevalent anxiety disorders , with a lifetime prevalence estimated to be of 5.7% in the united states and 2.8% in europe . gad is difficult to diagnose by primary care physicians and psychiatrists . in people who suffer gad , commonly occurring comorbid conditions ( e.g. , depression , alcoholism , other anxiety disorders ) often mask the underlying anxiety and result in a missed diagnosis of gad . in an attempt to elucidate the consequences of broadening criteria for generalized anxiety disorder , the current study examined prospectively the evolution of gad symptoms in two groups of diagnosed patients in public and private mental health settings ; one group according to the existing dsm - iv criteria and the other according to broader criteria ( at least 1 month but less than 6 months of excessive worry or non - excessive worry for gad diagnosis ) . by using the total ham - a score , changes in anxiety symptoms were studied in both groups under the basis of routine medical care in a 6 month period . finally , self - reported quality of life , disability , and other - reported outcomes were analyzed using the chi - square test and the student 's t - test , respectively .

backgroundrenal clear cell carcinomas represent about 3% of all visceral cancers and account for 85% of renal cancers in adults . in western countries the frequency of renal cancer remains relatively high , there being approximately 30,000 new cases and 12,000 deaths per year from the disease . the identification of genetic predispositions to renal cell cancer remains a priority since knowledge about the underlying molecular genetic basis of the disease will allow for a better understanding of the mechanisms giving rise to the disease and , perhaps more importantly , allow for identification of individuals who are at risk of disease development.objectivesthe aim of this study was to determine whether a minimum set of criteria could be established to identify suspected hereditary renal cell carcinoma patients when there is restricted information about the familial occurrence of disease . methodsa total of 146 clear cell renal carcinoma ( ccrc ) patients comprising 3 groups were enrolled in the study . group a ( familial renal cancer ) comprised 46 patients affected by ccrc from 22 randomly selected families with at least two renal cancers among first or second degree relatives , independent of age at diagnosis of tumours . group b comprised 100 individuals diagnosed with ccrc between the years 1993 and 1997 irrespective of family history . the following inclusion features ( if ) for the identification

intracranial hypotension syndrome ( ihs ) is characterized by a postural headache that is aggravated in the erect or sitting position and relieved in the supine position . it is often associated with one or more of the following symptoms : nausea , vomiting , dizziness , diplopia , photophobia , hearing impairment , neck stiffness , and blurred vision . the mechanism by which an epidural blood patch ( ebp ) relieves postdural puncture headache ( pdph ) is not known . the " plug for symptom resolution " theory proposes that the blood injected during ebp forms a gelatinous plug , sealing the dural hole and preventing further csf leak . in the absence of continued loss of csf , regeneration restores csf pressure and alleviates the headache . we report a case of a 54-year - old man who was successfully treated with ebp after l4 - 5 laminectomy and discectomy due to csf leakage into the lumbosacral area .

xanthogranuloma of the sellar region is a rare condition in childhood . we report the cases of two children who were diagnosed with                 cyst of the salear region presenting central diabetes insipidus . one of the patients had a 6-mo history of polyuria and polydipsia . the other patient had a 9-yr - old japanese boy who was referred to our clinic because of a history of central diabetes . the growth hormone releasing hormone ( gh ) and the thyroid stimulating hormone - like hormone ( tsh ) tolerance tests showed subnormal responses of gh and tsh , respectively . magnetic resonance imaging ( mri ) showed an intrllar mass , which was high on the t1-weighted brain mri . histological examination of the tumor showed fibrous tissue with cholesterol clefts , macrophages , multinucleated giant cells , and lymphocyte infiltration . the patient underwent surgery and the tumor was successfully removed . postoperatively , di remains and has been treated with desmopessin �� acetate ( ddavp ) .

a 52-year - old female patient visited the hospital complaining of pain in both breasts . the pain started immediately after breast reduction surgery she underwent 3 years previously at a private plastic surgery hospital , and it had continued despite a subsequent surgery . the pattern of the pain included burning , shooting and itching sensations at the operation site as well as unbearable burning sensations when the patient was in the places where the temperature was higher than normal . before coming to the hospital , the patient had taken aceclofenac ( airtal , daewoong pharmaceutical ) 200 mg , pregabalin ( lyrica , pfizer ) 300 mg , acetaminophen 1,300 mg and tramadol hcl 150 mg ( ultracet , janssen korea ) per day , but the pain was not relieved . subsequently , she was treated with drug treatment , but there was no effect . because the previous treatments were not effective in improving the symptoms and because the patient wanted another method of treatment due to the continued pain , it was decided that a thoracic epidural block would be performed , and informed consent was obtained from the patient . for the diagnosis , selective nerve root block ( snb ) was performed at t3 , t4 and t5 , one level

the generation of expressed sequence tags ( ests ) has become one of the most widely employed methods used for gene discovery and genome characterization . to maximize the value of these ests , ncbi has built unigenes that incorporate est data for a number of species and the institute for genomics research ( tigr ) has been working on the gene indices for more than 70 species . two tools that have been designed for locating protein - coding regions in cdna and est sequences are orffinder ( ) and estscan ( ) , respectively . the estscan server is designed for processing a batch of est sequences for identification of the protein coding regions with a function for correcting insertions or deletions , but it is only trained for mammals and yeast . we tested estscan with our aspergillus niger est sequences and found that the results were not satisfactory . for example , using a full - length cdna sequence encoding glucoamylase , a well - characterized enzyme in a.niger , we found that estscan could not identify its correct coding region and had the undesired side effect of inserting nucleotides even when the test sequence was correct . we have implemented a web server called orfpredictor for the prediction of protein

microspectrophotometry is a simple apparatus consisting of a modified microscope that can measure absorption or emission spectra of very small areas inside a cell . a high quality microscope is equipped with a polychromator , i.e. a flat field concave grating , which is connected to the slit - shaped exit pupil of a 19 light - guides probe . the dispersion image of the probe is focused onto a digital slow scan cooled ccd camera . iously the main feature of this instrumentation is the possibility to measure in vivo absorption or emitted spectra at the same time on different sub - cellular compartments using extremely low light intensities . we tested our apparatus by measuring absorption spectra on the eyespot and chloroplast of the unicellular alga dunaliella spp . and on the photoreceptor and chloroplasmal of the alga euglena gracilis . the spectra were acquired with a combination of a uv - blue filter set ( 8 nm band pass excitation filter , 365 nm ; chromatic beam splitter , 395 nm ; barrier filter , 397 nm ; irradiance 800 w / cm ) , and a blue - violet filter set . the absorption spectrum obtained on the chlorop

rapamycin ( mtor ) is a serine - threonine protein kinase that regulates several intracellular processes in response to various extracellular signals and thereby modulates mrna translation . mtor plays a critical role in the modulation of long - term plasticity and memory processes . we hypothesize that 5-htergic spinally projecting neurons in the rostral ventromedial medulla ( rvm ) contain mtor , the activation of which could potentiate the descending pain control pathway and exaggerate neuropathic pain conditions . accordingly , we used a spared nerve injury ( sni ) model to evaluate the role of mtor in the rvm in the development of nociceptive behavior in rats . rats were divided into two groups : a sham - operated group and a sni group , except that the nerves were not lesioned . the rats in the sham group were treated with intrathecal administration of the mtor inhibitor rapamycin , whereas the sni rats were not treated with mtor . rats in both groups showed increased excitability of the 5-hemispheric serotoninergic ( 5h ) neurons and decreased excitability in the dorsal spinal dorsal horn ( sdh ) , suggesting that m

background : traditional diagnostic methods and liberal indications for surgery have resulted in negative laparotomy rates ranging from 12 - 40% . prospective evaluation of the role of diagnostic laparoscopy in the evaluation of suspected penetrating abdominal injury was undertaken in september 1995 to identify those patients who could safely avoid surgical intervention , with the expectation that the rate of negative trauma celiotomy would markedly decrease.materials and methods : a prospective study of patients with penetrating injuries to the lower thorax and anterior abdomen admitted to the bellevue hospital trauma and shock unit , a level i trauma center in new york city , was performed from january 1995 through november 1997 . patients who were hemodynamically stable without obvious evidence of peritoneal penetration or free intra - abdominal air on upright chest radiograph were entered into the protocol . in patients with thoracoabdominal injuries and suspected diaphragmatic tears , a low pressure pneumoperitoneum of 8 - 10 mm hg was created , and if no diaphagmatic injuries were noted , a 10 mm laparoscope with attached video camera was inserted into the peritone cavity , and exploration performed . if peritonitis was noted , the procedure was terminated , and the patient subsequently discharged from

selenium ( se ) has been recognized as an essential nutrient for plant , animal , and human body , but at high concentration it can become toxic . the aim of this work was to use a simple , rapid , and sensitive spectrophotometric method ( dithizone as a chromogenic reagent ) for determination of trace amounts of selenium ( 5100  ng  ml ) in micellar medium ( triton x-100 ) . to improve the detection limit of this method , cloud point ( cpe ) extraction ( 99.9% ) was obtained from the ried de la haenen ( haen ) . the results showed that the concentration of se in the micelles was 5100 ng / ml . the se concentration was determined by atomic absorption spectrometry ( hydride generation and electrothermal atomization ) , molecular ( 15 , 16 ) and atomic fluorescence spectroscopy ( 1719 ) , high - performance liquid chromatography ( hplc ) , voltammetry ( 23 , 24 ) , inductively coupled plasma ( icp )   and induction - coupled plasma - mass spectraetry ( ikp - ms ) , and spectroscopometry methods ( 2528 ) . se

objectiveto determine the incidence of diabetes in children and young people in west yorkshire.research design and methodsdata on individuals aged 029 years diagnosed with any type of diabetes ( excluding gestational ) from 1 january 1991 to 31 december 2006 were extracted from clinical notes by a dedicated register manager and entered onto the population - based yorkshire register of diabetes . type 1 , type 2 , maturity - onset diabetes of the young ( mody ) , uncertain / unclassified and j-type diabetes were reclassified as type 1 and type 2 . ethnicity was assigned as south asian ( pakistani , indian , bangladeshi ) or all other ethnicities ( all other races ) using the south - asian names analysis program ( nam pehchan ) and the south-asian name and group recognition algorithm ( sangra ) . all analyses were done in stata 11 software ( statacorp lp , college station , tx ) . resultstype 1 diabetes was diagnosed in 2,889 children ( 83% ) , 12% ( n = 337 ) type 2 ( 0.7% of mody , 0.1% of j - type or other ) , and 4% ( 4% of uncertain - unclassified )

familial adenomatous polyposis ( fap ) is a rare cause of colorectal cancer and is caused by a wide spectrum of mutations in the apc gene . fap usually presents with a 100 to 1,000 of precancerous colonic polyps . although pathogenic apc mutations have a complete penetrance in the colon , this is not the case in extracolonic manifestations . gardner fibromas ( gf ) have been defined only recently as superficial and poorly circumscribed tumor - like lesions , which consist mainly of thick haphazardly arranged collagen bundles with few interspersed spindle cells of fibroblast type . gf may precede the development of colonic adenomas and therefore lead to an early detection of fap in otherwise asymptomatic patients . although it is most frequently observed in the first decade of life , with 78% of the cases being diagnosed before 10 years of age , gf has only been reported in a single case report so far . in our report , we relate the cases of two patients with neonatal gf and very different findings .

down 's syndrome ( ds ) is one of the common chromosomal disorders and the most common cause of mental retardation . it is well - known that patients with ds have an increased prevalence of autoimmune disorders affecting both endocrine and non - endocrine organs . although cases of ds associated with diabetes mellitus ( dm ) have been reported in the past , invariably all cases were associated with type 1 dm . in this report , we present two cases of type 2 dm associated with down s syndrome and believe that this is the first report of type dm with down ds .

mller glia ( mg ) , a glial progenitor cell in the mammalian retina , is implicated in the pathogenesis of many neurodegenerative diseases , including those that affect the retina to cause blindness . recent observations suggest that mg possess neural stem cell ( nsc ) properties and are able to generate retinal neurons in vitro and upon transplantation in vivo . however , in vivo , these cells maintain their glial phenotype and do not convert to neurons . to address this challenge , the neurogenic potentials of mg need to be evaluated against the backdrop of two models : ( 1 ) svz / sgz model , where mg have nsc properties and thus the capacity for neuronal differentiation like radial glia - derived nscs in the sub - ventricular zone ( svz ) and sub - granular zone ( sgz ) of the lateral ventricle , and ( 2 ) extrasvz/sgz model where mg are unable to differentiate along neuronal lineage , presumably under the influence of the non - neurogenic niche . in both models , knowledge of the developmental mechanism for the lineage switch , the reaction of mg to different kinds of injuries , phenotypes of the activated mg in spatial and temporal contexts , transcriptional and epigenetic

diazaethene ligands are increasingly important in coordination chemistry due to their flexible coordination modes and redox properties . recently , we reported the synthesis of the dimetalated diamine ( tmeda)li[(ipr)nch2ch2nh(ip r ) = chn(ip ) = tbu(tbu ) 1 , which displays a new mode of hydrogen activation previously unseen within zincate chemistry . we now report a detailed mechanistic overview of this surprising transformation from both experimental and theoretical perspectives . in a fashion similar to that of the two - step mechanism for monoamido zincates originally proposed by uchiyama et al .                  , the amino n  h group of 3 is rapidly deprotonated by a tbu ligand to form the dimediamine dimer . the structure of this dimer has been characterized by multinuclear nmr spectroscopy , which confirms the fully saturated nature of the monolithiated diamine . the crystal data are of insufficient quality to allow discussion of the geometric parameters of the structure , but the connectivity is definite .

we reviewed the literature for reports of e. sakazakii disease in infants . using medical subject heading terms " newborn , " " infant , " or " meningitis ,  finally , we reviewed case consultations conducted by the centers for disease control and prevention ( cdc ) from 1998 to 2005 and reviewed results of a french outbreak reported to cdc in 2005 . forty - six infants met the case definition . twenty - nine ( 69% ) of 42 infants experienced disease onset within a hospital . the median gestational duration was available for 38 infants ; 21 ( 55% ) were born prematurely , and the median birthweight was 2,063 g ( range 5403,401 ) . although the proportion of infants who experienced nosocomial disease onset was not significantly different between the meneditis and bacteremia groups , other infant characteristics differed by site of infection .

background : the purpose of this study was to evaluate the effect of sterilization with dry heat and steam on load - deflection characteristics of -titanium ( tma ) wires , with different brands.materials and methods : in the present study five different types of tma wires with a cross - section of 0.43 0.64 mm ( 0.017  0.025 inch ) were evaluated . group i was assigned as the control group . group ii was sterilized by dry heat in an oven ( 12 bahman digital sterilizer , dsl 40 , isfahan , iran ) at 160c for 1 hour . group iii and iv were sterilized in an autoclave ( melag euroclav , 23 v - s , berlin , germany ) at 121c and 15 psi for 15 minutes , respectively . the wire samples were left to gradually cool to temperatures near the ambient temperature in the same sterilizers . then the samples underwent a three - point load- deflection test using a specially designed mechanical testing machine ( model 1122 , instron corporation , canton , ma , usa ) in the dry state . the test was carried out on two metallic half - cylindrical structures consisting of

most pituitary adenomas are clinically inactive . in patients with long - standing compression of the optic chiasm , ganglion cells may undergo axonal degeneration . spectral domain optical coherence tomography ( sd - oct ) is able to identify retinal nerve fiber layer ( rnfl ) loss in the retina . in this study , we present a case in which the oct was used to diagnose an asymptomatic macroadenoma . a 48-year - old female presented with progressive vision loss in both eyes for several months . her past ocular , medical , and family history was non - contributory with no fatigue , loss of libido , no mood change , and no weight change . on examination , best - corrected visual acuity was hand motion in the right eye and 2/3 in the left eye . the optic nerve fibers were symmetrically lost and the retinal optic nerve layer was also lost . the histopathological examination of the retina demonstrated diffuse atrophy of the nerve layers , with preservation of outer layers bilaterally . magnetic resonance imaging of the brain was performed and a pituitaroma with suprasellar extension was observed .

percutaneous nephrolithotomy ( pcnl ) is a widely accepted treatment for urinary calculi > 2 cm or resistant to other modalities . complications of this procedure are well reported ; however , to our knowledge no mention has occurred in the literature of a retained nephrostomy tube after pcnl . thus , we will present the management of a retention of a council tip catheter following pcnl.a 76-year - old gentleman underwent a pcnl for a 2.2x1.4-cm lower pole calculus via an uneventful upper pole puncture . incomplete stone fragmentation was obtained using the ultrasonic lithotripter ; therefore , a 24-french council tip nephropathy tube was placed , the balloon was inflated with 3 ml of sterile water , and the patient was scheduled for a second look nephroscopy when a holmium laser was available . one week later , attempts to deflate the catheter balloon were unsuccessful . after transection of the balloon - port valve , attempted passage of a 0.038 movable core bentson guidewire down the balloon port was unsuccessful . with the c - arm in a vertical orientation , an 18-gauge chiba needle was advanced

purposeto investigate the histopathologic evaluation outcomes of anterior periprostatic fat ( appf ) tissues that were separately sent for pathologic evaluation in order to examine presence of pelvic lymph nodes ( lns ) and metastasis after radical prostatectomy ( rp ) in patients with localized prostate cancer ( pca).methodsa total of 129 patients who underwent rp between january 2012 and october 2014 were included in our study . during the rarp procedures , anterior appf covering prostate , endopelvic fascia , dorsal venous complex , prostatic apex , puboprostatic ligaments , prostate - vesical junction and the bladder neck was dissected en bloc laterally and cephalad . if metastatic ln(s ) was /were detected , immunohistochemistry for prostate specific antigen ( psa ) and prostate specific acid phosphatase were performed.resultsthe mean age , serum psa , prostate weight , body mass index ( bmi ) , positive surgical margins ( psm ) and gleason score ( gs ) were 62.2 5.5 ( range 4574 ) , 9.3 6.3ng / dl ( range 0.330.3 ) , 60

introduction programs should take into account the presence of parasites which are pathogenic or to which the species of concern is not adapted . although some information exists regarding the prevalence of endoparasitism in american marten in north america , relatively little information is available for the species in michigan . the aim of this parasite survey was to describe the presence and prevalence of parasites in the huron - manistee national forest of the northern lower peninsula ( nlp ) and to determine whether there are differences in the presence or prevalence of these parasites between the nlp and the up populations . live - trapped american mertens ( martes martes americana ) were captured and immobilized from 2011 to 2015 . faecal samples from nine individuals were examined for parasites and a possible association between hookworm infection and anaemia in affected animals was identified based on haematocrit measurements .

[ purpose ] the purpose of this study was to investigate the effects of osteoarthritis on the flexion - relaxation phenomenon ( frp ) of the erector spinae during trunk flexion and trunk extension motions in both healthy , young females and elderly females with chronic knee osteoarthropathy . [ subjects and methods ] the present study was a randomized controlled trial , which included healthy adult females ( n=17 ) , elderly females aged at least 65 years , and healthy young females aged n=13 . [ results ] the mean age of the subjects was 68.5 years . the mean duration of pain in the elderly subjects was significantly longer than that in the young subjects ( p<0.001 ) . [ conclusion ] this study showed that the frp - relaxation rate of erector spine muscles in elderly females was significantly higher than that of young females . this study suggests that elderly patients suffering from chronic knee pain are at risk for developing abnormal frp of the                 thirty-one - jointed spina bilaterally , which could be used as a pain scale to improve the quality of life .

purpose . to evaluate the efficacy and safety of sitagliptin as a first - line oral antihyperglycemic agent for the treatment of new - onset diabetes after transplantation ( nodat ) in kidney transplant recipients . methods . between october 2006 and december 2012 , we performed a retrospective analysis of patients who received a kidney transplant at the university of nebraska medical center ( unmc ) between 2009 and 2013 . inclusion criteria included diagnosis of nodat by the 2003 international consensus guidelines criteria ( fasting plasma glucose 126  mg / dl or 7.0  mmol / l after no caloric intake for at least 8 hours or random plasma glucose 200 or 11.1  mmol/l with symptoms of diabetes or 2  h plasma glucose in a 75  g oral glucose tolerance test ( ogtt ) 200 or more ) and diabetes care received at the unmc diabetes center . patients were followed up until the end of followup 12 months after initiation of the therapy . results . the mean age of the study subjects was 54.5 years ( range , 28 to 67 ) . the median followup duration was 12 months ( range : 0 to 24 months ) . there was no significant difference in the mean change in a1c ( p = 0

in this case report , the authors have attempted to present the case of a hiv - free hiv patient who was admitted to the hospital because of hiv . the patient was initially treated for hiv at the time of admission , but later , he was shown to have a histopathological examination in the ileum . the major objective of the case report is to highlight the challenges in diagnosis and management of the hiv , especially in the setting of recent initiation of art . hiv is a frequent complication that is most often associated with exaggerated inflammatory responses in the central nervous system . in this case study , hiv was diagnosed and treated in hiv hospital . he was managed as a possible atypical pneumonia with doxymorphic pneumonia . however , the patient still had the same complaints with almost unchanged cd4 of 24  cells/l and copy machine of 23,755  copies / ml . this second ultrasound revealed a thickened ichthyosis of 6 mm , with adjacent 1  cm lymph nodes . at day + 21 of art , he reported abdominal pain with hypogastric , and in view of the persistent abdominal pain and an occasional cough , another chest radiograph was performed to exclude tb , which was also unrem

background : dermatophytic infections are a common clinical problem encountered in more than 50% of patients attending the dermatology outpatient departments . overcrowding , poor hygiene , low standards of living along with high humidity environments are contributing to the increased prevalence of these fungal infections.objectives:the present study was conducted to know the prevalence , etiology and common clinical presentations of dermatophytosis involving skin and hair.materials and methods : a total of 110 samples which included 101 skin samples and 9 hair samples , from clinically suspected to have dermatophyte infections were collected . a detailed history regarding age , sex , occupation , social status , duration of complaint and others were taken.results:tinea corporis was the most common clinical presentation followed by tinea cruris , tinea capitis and tinea faciei.conclusion:tuberculous dermatophytes are the most prevalent fungal infection in dermatology .

gemcitabine is a 2-deoxyribonucleotide reductase inhibitor that has been widely used to treat pancreatic cancer for nearly 20 years . it has been proposed that the inhibition of the rnr enzyme activity by this drug is achieved via radical                 cytidine cation radical formation at the c3 site by direct h - atom abstraction by thiyl radicals . in this work , we report the formation of c2 from a likely c3 via a mechanism similar to that proposed by stubbe et al . , in which the two highly electronegative f - atoms at c2 substantially increase the acidity of h3                  through the inductive effect of the two f - atom substituents substituent on radical site formation . in the case of one - electron oxidized mefdc , the lowest energy path is the rapid formation of a c3 from c2 via deprotonation of the c2 intermediate via f loss . this c3 intermediate is stabilized by an oxy radical resonance                 c2 .

purposethe aim of this study was to report our intermediate experience in men who underwent implantation of adjustable perineal male sling using a tissue expander for post - prostatectomy urinary incontinence ( ppi).materials and methodsbetween september 2007 and may 2013 , a total of 21 men with ppi underwent an adjustable male sling implantation . the device contains a silicone balloon expander , a connecting tube and an inflation component ( injection port ) that allows the expander to gradually fill up to a volume of 25 ml saline solution ( figure-1b ) . all the patients underwent a standard urodynamic study including both cystometry and pressure - flow study to evaluate bladder storage and voiding capabilities , and to exclude overactive bladder . all patients received prophylactic antibiotics ( 3rd generation cephalosporin ) before the induction of anesthesia . under general or regional anesthesia , after the insertion of a urethral catheter , an incision of 40 to 50 mm was made . a standard polypropylene mesh of 100 x 100 mm was used as the sling material . during this procedure the mesh was stapled on both sides of the lower border of the pubic bone using an autosuture stat

objectivefibromyalgia syndrome ( fms ) is a challenging disorder to diagnose and assess . the purpose of this study was to compare the turkish version of the fibromyalgia rapid screening tool ( first ) with the american college of rheumatology ( acr ) alternative diagnostic criteria ( 2013altcr ) and the hospital anxiety and depression scale ( hads ) in patients with fms.materials and methodsa total of two hundred and sixty - nine patients ( 257 females and 12 males ) with an average age ( years ) of 48.29  12.78 ( range 2279 ) were included in the study . they were recruited from outpatient physical medicine and rehabilitation clinics from 9 medical faculty hospitals and the ministry of health and training and training inessa convenience sample of patients with chronic pain was used for the study.resultsoverall , the first iky version of iky was significantly more sensitive and more sensitive than the 2011 modified criteria ( 2011modcr ) ( p < 0.001 ) and hads ( p = 0.002 ) . the first version of first hads was also more sensitive , more specific , and more specific than the acr 2013alt cr ( p > 0.05 ) . it

introductionoverweight and obesity are conditions that have been increasing in the united states and these risk factors for diseases are estimated to affect more than one - half of those over the age of 20 . public health officials have begun to monitor the health status of school - aged children because it is believed that many of them exhibit risk factors that create the potential for developing chronic disease as adults . this study was designed to examine the students possible role in changing their own negative practices , by first assessing their perceptions of the obstacles to positive dietary practices and increased physical activity . the study also solicited students recommendations for reducing the negative practices that are associated with the rise in obesity and the development of cardiovascular diseases.methodsthe sample for this study included 300 students from a high school , located in rural , mississippi . the students were first asked to complete an assent form and to have their parents sign a consent form before they could participate in the survey . only students who returned completed assent and consent forms were permitted to participate in this study . the data for the study were obtained from the administration of the project health high school survey ( phhss ) . the survey was modified to fulfill the objectives of this study by including additional questions that served additional objectives of the objectives

vascular diseases are among the most common causes of morbidity and mortality , and both number and severity of morbid vascular conditions increase with age . it is now well established that disruption of endothelial integrity represents a crucial event in the initiation and development of cardiovascular ( cv ) diseases . numerous studies have reported that microparticles ( mps ) play an important role in endothelial dysfunction . furthermore , mps originating from various cells have been found to be associated with several vascular related diseases . moreover , exposed procoagulant phospholipids and specific receptors at the surface of mps act as biomessengers linking inflammation , coagulation , and angiogenesis . mps may transfer part of their components and content to the selected target cells , thus mediating cell activation , phenotypic modification , and reprogramming of cell function . although 70% to 90% of all circulating mps in the peripheral blood of healthy individuals are derived from platelets , marked elevations of all kinds of mp have been observed in many vascular diseases . this review focuses on the role of endothelium - derived microparticle ( emps ) in vascular diseases and the contribution of estrogen to these diseases .

background : medicinal herbs with antihyperglycemic activities are increasingly sought by diabetic patients and health care professionals . in this study , we assessed the effects of eight weeks of three grams per day cinnamon supplementation ( cinnamomum zeylanicum ) on glycemic status , lipid profiles , blood pressure and body composition in type 2 diabetic patients.materials and methods : a randomized , placebo controlled , double blind study was performed in 44 patients with type 2 diabetes . the subjects were randomly assigned to either cinnamon ( n=22 ) or placebo group . cinnamon and wheat flour were ground finely and put into capsules which could not be distinguished by color , odor , or taste . subjects were instructed to ingest two capsules at each main meal ( breakfast , lunch and dinner ) for eight weeks . compliance to the supplementation protocol was monitored by counting the unconsumed capsules each week . food records were analyzed by nutritionist iv at baseline and at the end of the study.results:fifty-four individuals were enrolled in the study . the mean hba1c was 6.9% and the mean fbg was 126.5 mg / dl . there was no significant difference between cinnamon and placebo groups at baseline ( p<0.001 ) . there were no significant differences

a difficult airway has been described in patients with pituitary endocrine disorders such as acromegaly and dwarfism ; however , airway problems with hypogonadism have not been well identified in the literature . anesthesiologists are occasionally faced and challenged with this significant problem in practice . the management consists of laryngeal mask ventilation , gum - elastic bougie and video laryngoscopy - assisted intubation . we report a case of a 53-year - old male patient who presented with a history of nasal obstruction for two years . the patient was admitted to the operating theater , and following the induction of anesthesia with a dose of 5 mg / kg intravenous thiopental , bag - mask ventilation was barely sustained . while the patient s head was in the sniffing position , direct larygoscopy was attempted three times with different sizes of macintosh and miller blades by an assistant professor of anesthesiology with 5 years experience . however , unfortunately , the first attempt failed . the lungs were then ventilated with 100% oxygen via a face mask in order to avoid desaturation . the glottic visualization was assessed with cook s modification of the cormack lehane classification grade 3

we reviewed the medical records of patients who had human listeriosis isolated from blood and body fluids from sterile sites during 19962008 at the national taiwan university hospital ( ntuh ) , a 2,500-bed hospital in tailand . the incidence of nontyphiodal salmonella bacteremia ( ntsb ) during 20002008 was calculated for trend comparison of the 2 foodborne illnesses . during the study period , 48 patients were diagnosed with human l. monocytogenes , and 46 nonduplicated isolates were obtained for further microbiological analysis . the annual incidence increased from 0.0287 cases per 1,000 admissions during 19962004 to 0.118 cases per 1000 admissions during 20052008 . the increase in annual incidence of listeria was significantly correlated with years ( p = 0.0045 ) . among the 46 isolates , serotype 1/2b was identified most frequently ( 46% ) , followed by 1/1a ( 28% ) and 4b ( 26% ) . the presence of solid - organ - malignancies was a significant negative prognostic factor for 14-day mortality in the univariate univariate analysis ( 95% confidence interval [ ci ] 1

digital flexor tendon contracture in feline is a rare clinical entity . it is characterized by a permanent flexion of the proximal interphalangeal joint of one or more digits , associated with a difficult and painful extension of this joint . it should be considered in a differential diagnosis of feline lameness whenever onychectomy has been performed in the past . this case series reports three different clinical presentations of this condition in three cats , all successfully treated by tenectomy .

backgroundadiponectin is a 244-amino acid protein with 30 kda molecular weight that circulates in the serum in different multimeric forms . the aim of this study was to investigate the expression of the adiponectins gene by real - time pcr in the ovaries of swine and to compare gene and protein expression levels during different stages of the oestrous cycle in pigs.material/methodsthe experimental material comprised mature gilts ( large white and polish landrace ) from a private breeding farm , aged 7 - 8 months and weighing 130140 kg . twenty gilts were divided into four experimental groups as follows : days 2 - 3 , 1012 , 1416 , and 1719 of the second oestrus , and the day of the follicular phase was designated as day 0 of the evaluation . the level of progesterone , oestradiol , testosterone , and androstenedione was determined in ovarian lutea , granulosa and theca interna cells of sexually mature swine.resultsthe expression of adiponectorin gene and its protein were significantly higher in ovary follicular luteal cells than in the ca interna cell ( p < 0.05 )

molecular and morphological evidence together support the idea that the anterior end of the amphioxus nerve cord contains regions homologous with vertebrate forebrain and hindbrain 1 , though it is generally the ventral portion of these regions that are best represented . its most probable position , as defined by gene expression patterns , would be from somewhere forward of the caudal limit of otx expression , which in vertebrates extends through the midbrain , to a point close to the beginning of the zone of hox gene expression . however , there is no clear molecular evidence for a focal center in the anterior cord with the expression profile of an mhb . in fact , except for a pineal homolog , amphioxuus appears to lack all of the dorsal structures of the vertebrate brain . this is perhaps not surprising , considering that the structures organized by the mhbs of vertebrates are primarily dorsal ones not present in Amphioxus . further , because the amphoxinis nerve cord is of uniform dimension along its length , there are no morphological constrictions to separate sub - domains from one another in the way vertebrate isthmus separates midbrain from hindbrain . the available molecular data thus argues against the presence of precise

a seventy five year old libyan man was seen in the urology department of tripoli medical centre , tripoli , libya with six month history of left loin pain . the patient was suspected clinically to have renal tuberculosis and was later found to have a renal squamous cell carcinoma with possible lung secondaries . this case demonstrates the transition from calyseal urothelium to squamous metaplasia , dysplasia and invasive squamous malignancy .

peppermint ( m. piperita ) essential oils are valuable natural products used as raw materials in many fields including perfumes , cosmetics , aromatherapy , phototherapy , spices , and nutrition . the aim of this study was to investigate the chemical composition and in vitro antifungal and antibiofilm activities of essential oils of the leaves and flowers of the plant in the region of fars from iran . the analysis was carried out on a thermoquest - finnigan trace gc - ms instrument equipped with a db-5 fused silica column ( 60  m  0.25  mm i.d . , film thickness 0.5 mm ) . the presence and concentration of certain chemical constituents of the essential oils also fluctuated according to the season , climatic condition , and site of plant growth . the antibacterial activity of the extract was investigated by gas chromatography - mass spectrometry and methanolic extracts of herbal parts and callus cultures of the plants . the results showed that the essential oil ( eo ) and the extract of the flowers ( eos ) of the tree were the most effective in inhibiting the growth of candida and aspergillus species . the eos of m.

falls of the elderly always lead to serious health issues as the decline of their physical fitness . the fall process is the main source of injury because of the high impact . this paper presents a fall detection system based on a single triaxial accelerometer and gps / gsm module . the device uses an efficient fall detection algorithm with less resource and power consumption , which means that it is a proper design for outdoor application . it will get the elderly 's geographic position and send fall alarm short message to caregivers so the elderly who has fallen can get timely help to minimize the negative influence .

objectivethe aim of this study was to evaluate the effect of preganglionic ttransforaminal epidural steroid injection ( tfesi ) at the level of the supra - adjacent intervertebral disc for lumbar radiculopathy.methodswe retrospectively reviewed the medical records of the patients who received tfesi using the conventional approach at our department during a 1-year period , from june 2003 to may 2004 . after a consensus meeting between three radiologists who were unaware of the clinical results after tfesi , we selected those cases treated by conventional tfesi with supra- adjacent nerve root impingement . using the same inclusion criteria as mentioned above , a total of 20 patients were consecutively enrolled and they were referred to as the pre - ganglionic tfesi group . all the treatments were performed as outpatient procedures , and written informed consents were obtained from all the patients . the patients were followed - up for a 2-week follow - up . the level and cause of nerve root compression on ct or mr imaging were documented in the medical charts before performing tfesi.resultsof the 20 patients enrolled , 13 met the inclusion criteria and were included in the study . the mean age of the group was 57

photochemical photochemical and thermal steps in the unidirectional rotational rotation of a molecular rotary motor are governed by the excited - state properties of the central c = c group of the motor , which are dictated by the potential energy of the excited states that are accessed upon electronic excitation . in the present work we show that a combination of picosecond time - resolved vibrational spectroscopy of the electronically excited states and quantum - mechanical calculations can obtain a detailed understanding of the dynamics of the rotational phase transition of the electronic structure of a single carbon carbon double bond in an intrinsically chiral environment . the results of this study are presented in the journal of photochemistry and photochemistry engineering ( http://www.bioinfo.com/doi : 10.1303/bioengineering.2012.049.0 ) .

objectivethe patellofemoral joint is one of the most common sources of knee pain in younger people . the objective of our study was to determine the impact of knee position and quadriceps contraction on patella indices measured on sagittal magnetic resonance imaging ( mri ) of the knee.materials and methodstwenty patients undergoing mri scan of their knee were selected from a pool of referrals to the orthopaedic department at our institution . axial and sagittal mri scans were performed with the knee fully extended and quadriplegic ; knee flexed and relaxed ; and knee flexion and extension with quadricep contraction . the insall - salvati index and caton - deschamps index were measured on the sagittal view using the slice showing the widest view of the patellar articular surface and the bisect offset was measured on axial mri images . patell ofemoral indices were measured using the axial view of mri and on the transverse view of sagittal images.resultsthe mean age of the patients was 2540 years with a body mass index less than 30 and a clinical diagnosis of non - patelloarthritis or patellomorbidity .

objectivethe knowledge of mechanical materials behaviour is of great importance particularly for some innovative engineering applications , which involve a single material , bimaterials , or multiple materials.materials and methodsthe present work refers to the hot - dip galvanizing treatment applied as a treatment to fight against corrosion . the role of this treatment consists in the deposition of a protective external layer of metallic zinc obtained by immersing the steel in a zinc bath at a temperature of around 460c .  when the material ( i.e. , steel ) is introduced into the zinc bath and then removed , several changes in the chemical composition and in the mechanical structure can occur . these changes produce a new structural arrangement on zinc substrate and are usually revealed by the generation of cracks in the zinc layer . the behaviour of materials may change as a result of various events such as fatigue , fracture , wear , fretting fatigue , creep , hydrogen embrittlement , thermal shock processes , or atmospheric attacks .    the elastic fracture theory is a suitable tool to compute the mechanical behaviour in case of coatings , composites , and welded structures.resultsaccording to the study made by panin et al . , the interface of solid materials covers a sinusoidal surface layer due

microrna-134 ( mir-134 ) is a small noncoding rna that controls dendrite spine morphogenesis , via repression of lim domain kinase 1 and other targets , and is upregulated in areas of the hippocampus following status epilepticus induced by intra - amygdala kainic acid ( ka ) in mice . here , we tested the effect of antagomir - based treatment in mice in a pentylenetrazol ( ptz ) model of acquired epilepsy , a toxin - free , nonconvulsant , nonperforant stimulation model that avoids the influence of brain chemvulsants , and we used the perforant pathway ( pps ) model to screen for anticonvulsant effects in mice , a popular method for screening for putative seizures via -amino butyric acid ( gaba ) blockade . we found that ant-134 significantly suppressed the later occurrence of spontaneous recurrent seizures in the intra- amygdala ka model in mice and that the dendritic spine volume was increased in the ptz model after antagomycin - treated mice . we conclude that the mirna-134-based treatment of ptz mice has seizure - suppressive effects similar to those observed in

objective . to estimate the prevalence of headache among adults in jordan . methods . a self - administered questionnaire was administered to 4,836 participants ( mean age 27    0.4 years , and male : female ratio of 59:41 ) . results . about half of the participants ( 49.2% ) were university students and 78.3% were jordanian citizens . headache was the most common complaint ( > 15 days per month ) with a prevalence rate of 25% . analgesic use was associated with headache ( p < 0.001 ) . the frequency of headache increased with increasing age ( p = 0.002 ) and with increasing painkiller dose ( p > 0.05 ) . there was no significant association between headache frequency and painkiller use . conclusion . the study of headache epidemiology can address a number of important questions such as variation in the occurrence and severity of headache in the population , and the relationship between headache and other medical disorders .

a 55-year - old woman visited the emergency clinic of hanyang university seoul hospital with chest pain that had persisted for one month and had become aggravated two days earlier . under cardiopulmonary bypass and fibrillation , a defect measuring 15 mm in diameter was identified in the sub - mitral left ventricular wall with calcification but with no infection . coronary computed tomographic angiography revealed three large lobulated aneurysms with calcified walls bulging from the base of the left ventricle , and based on this finding , we diagnosed the patient with a pseudoaneurysm . the patient underwent surgical repair and peri - guard pericardium patching . she has remained disease - free for six years postoperatively with regular coumadin anticoagulation therapy .

purposeto evaluate any possible relationship between preoperative clinical and imaging findings of patients with posterior urethral valve ( puv ) and remnant leaflets after their valve ablation to identify high - risk patients and achieve purposive follow - up.materials and methodswe evaluated 64 patients with clinical evidence of posterior puv , confirmed by voiding cystourethrography ( vcug ) , who were admitted between 2008 and 2012 at the shiraz university of medical sciences . we divided the patients into two groups on the basis of observation of obstructive residual leaflets in a second cystoscopy to analyze the possible preoperative factors that were related to obstructive remnant leaflets.resultsa total of 37 patients ( 67.3% ) had no significant remnant leaflets ( a group ) , whereas 18 boys ( 32.7% ) required a second ablation of a second valve as a result of remnant leaflets . the median age at the time of diagnosis was 10.0 months ( range , 5 days to 120 months ) . in group a , group a had no evidence of obstruction , and in group b , group b a second procedure was done owing to residual residual leaflets.conclusionour results suggest that the presence of residual remnant leaflets

objectiveto determine whether optical coherence tomography ( oct ) and polarized raman spectroscopy ( prs ) can be used to differentiate white spot lesions ( wsls ) from sound enamel and demineralized enamel.materials and methodsthe objective of this study was to determine whether calculus , hypocalcification , and stain are confounding factors affecting wsl detection with oct and prs.resultspatients were divided into two groups according to the type of white spot lesion ( sound and white spot ) and whether they were treated with calculus or not . in the sound group , there were no significant differences between sound and wsl lesions ( p < 0.001 ) , whereas there were significant differences in the number of white spots ( p > 0.05 ) and the width of the wsl ( p = 0.01 ) . in contrast , there was a significant difference in the amount of calcification in the white spot group compared to the other two groups ( p<0.05).conclusionsthe results from this study demonstrate that the results from optical coherent tomography and polarized spectroscopic studies are not affected by factors in the oral environment , thereby increasing the sensitivity and specificity of the new

introductionhematoxylin and eosin - stained slides and formalin fixed paraffin embedded blocks of eight cases of hepatocellular adenocarcinoma ( hca ) were retrieved from the archives of the department of pathology , korea university medical center and seoul national university hospital . the histologic characteristics of the tumors were evaluated with emphasis on steatosis , sinusoidal dilatation , inflammatory infiltrate , cytological atypia , acinar growth pattern , telangiectasia , and peliosis . immunohistochemical stainings were performed using the following antibodies : l - fabp , -catenin , saa , and glutamine synthetase.resultsthe histological characteristics of hca were classified into hca subtypes based on clinical , histologic , and immunoregulatory parameters.conclusionhepatients with hca without genetic changes in the previously mentioned markers are categorized into this group into h - hca and hca with complete or near - complete necrosis can also be classified in this group .

microglia account for approximately 10% of the adult brain cell population and represent the first and main form of immune defense in the central nervous system ( cns ; lawson et al . ) . microglia initiate immune responses by enhancing the expression of toll - like receptors ( tlr ) and a wide range of pro - inflammatory mediators such as tumor necrosis factor - alpha ( tnf ) , interleukin ( il)-1 and il-6 for the removal of the cns threat . although reactive astrocytes , in vivo , do not significantly contribute to the c - pk11195 signal , the increased pk 11195 binding is likely to indicate the transition of microglial from a resting to an activated state , and is due to an increase in the number , rather than the affinity , of tspo . although tspo has several biological functions including the control of cholesterol transport and neurosteroid synthesis , it is also associated with the motor symptoms of tremor , bradykinesia and rigidity .

n - feruloylserotonin ( n - f-5ht ) is a conjugated serotonin , a member of the indole hydroxycinnamic acid amides , with serotonin ( 5-ht ) and ferulic acid ( fa ) as representative components of its structure . it is used as a natural polyphenol in the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis ( ra ) , a chronic systemic inflammatory disease that affects predominantly joints , synovial membranes , articular cartilages , and subchondral bones . ra progression is attributed to increases in reactive oxygen species ( ros ) and oxidative stress ( os ) in the lesion sites . proinflammatory cytokines , such as tumor necrosis factor- ( tnf- ) , interleukin-1 ( il-1 ) , and il-6 , regulate the inflammatory and immune responses and play a pivotal role in the disease . increasing evidence is pointing to the critical role of the liver in modulating the immune response in autoimmune and chronic inflammatory diseases including ra . in addition , the hepatic biochemical and immunological alterations are associated with and influenced by changes in the oxidative state of liver cells . in this review , we summarize the current knowledge of n - fer

background : children commonly use computers for playing games , completing school assignments , sending / receiving e - mails , and connecting to the internet . this may sometimes interfere with other activities such as homework or normal social interchange . internet addiction is excessive or compulsive use of the internet , computer , and video games that may interfere with daily life . in this study we tried to investigate the prevalence of internet addiction among high school students in southern iran , shiraz , and its relation to depression and anxiety.materials and methods : in a cross - sectional study 1020 students ( males and females ) were selected randomly by area and cluster sampling from different areas of south iran . demographic data ( age , sex , and economic status according to income of the family ) , duration of internet usage , computer games playing , tv and satellite watching , dvd and video cd usage , and the reasons of the usages were asked at the interviews .                  the students were interviewed for addiction , generalized anxiety disorder , and major depressive disorder according to the diagnostic and statistical manual of mental disorders , 4th edition ( dsm - iv ) criteria . the obtained data were analyzed using descriptive indices and the analytic methods of the chi - squared and pearson regression.results:the

objective . to characterize the changes in serum cytokine concentrations during pregnancy in a cohort of primigravid women . methods . we used multiplex cytokine arrays to measure 42 cytokines and growth factors in 16 pregnant women . results . serum cytokines , chemokines , growth factors , and protein levels were measured using the milliplex human cytokine map / chemokine kit ( millipore , billerica , ma , usa ) . the median change in cytokine levels from the first to the last trimester of pregnancy was 1.2 pg / ml ( p < 0.001 ) , with a median change of 0.5 pg / l ( p = 0.002 ) . in contrast , the mean change in chemokin levels was 0.9 pg / m2 ( p > 0.05 ) . changes in growth factor levels were 0.8 pg / cm2 ( 0.7 ) , 0.6 mg / dl ( 1.0 ) , and 0.4 mg / cm3 ( 0% ) in the second and third trimesters of pregnancy . in addition , changes in cytokines were observed in the fourth , fifth , and seventh months of pregnancy in both the first and

sarcoidosis - related granulomatous reaction of the immune system could be attributed to environmental factors . however , data supporting this hypothesis remain controversial . clinical features of this disease include the following nonspecific symptoms : cough , dyspnea , erythema nodosum , febrile arthritis , uveitis , and parotitis . the diagnosis is established on the basis of compatible clinical and radiological findings and supported by histological evidence in one or more organs of noncaseating epithelioid cell granulomas in the absence of organisms or particles . in this article , we report a case of non - luminal her-2/neu - positive breast cancer in a patient who initially suspected to have metastatic disease .

purposeto investigate the origin and progression of glaucoma - associated atrophy ( ga ) after complete loss of the retinal pigment epithelium ( rpe ) and basal lamina ( brm ) in patients with generalized atrophy of the eye ( study eye ) and to determine the volume of the drusenoid ped and ensuing plateau.materials and methodsthis retrospective observational study was conducted in 20 eyes of 20 patients with ga . all eyes had consecutive eye - tracked spectral - domain ( sd)-oct scans taken at least every 6 months for 3 years . the most recent sd - oct with a signature of the region of interest was identified . then , serial b - scans of the area of interest were tracked back to baseline . in cases in which the scanning protocol varied during the course of follow - up , the b - scan closest to the area with the signature was matched manually and extracted for analysis . these extracted images were then stacked , aligned , and saved as a video file using the fiji distribution ( http://fiji.sc ; in the public domain ) for the qualitative analysis of the progression of the lesion . at each time point , the appearance of a plateau on the sd-oct was correlated to the appearance

pulmonary arterial endarteritis is a rare event , even in patients with congenital heart disease ( chd ) . a 4-year - old male child was admitted with the history of fever and progressive right - sided weakness for 1 week and cyanosis noted since 6 months of age . on admission , the cardiac apex was at left 5 intercostal space , 3 cm outside the mid - clavicular line . an ejection systolic murmur ( grade iii / vi ) was audible in the pulmonary area . an ecg revealed right atrial enlargement , extreme right axis , and poor progression of r wave . a chest x - ray revealed cardiomegaly with reduced pulmonary vascular markings . a full - segmental echocardiography revealed a single ventricle of the double - inlet left ventricular type .                  the patient was started on parenteral ceftriaxone 80 mg/kg / day ( plus , gentamicin  3 mg / kg / day for 2 weeks ) and was continued for 6 weeks for optimal treatment of the cerebral abscess . the patient became afebrile in 2 weeks and was able to walk after 4 weeks of therapy .

pre - implantation genetic diagnosis ( pgd ) is a diagnostic procedure that has emerged against the backdrop of in vitro fertilisation ( ivf ) technology in nigeria . the most common form of pgd involves the extraction of one or two cells from the pre- implantation embryo , often around the 8-cell stage . with pgd , the genetic status of an embryo can be determined before transfer into the uterus after ivf , thus almost completely eliminating the risks of bearing a child with the disease . we report the case of a 29-year - old woman with sickle cell anaemia and heterozygous genotype ( as ) , who underwent pgd for the diagnosis of sickle - cell trait . the patient was triggered with human chorionic gonadotropin 5000 iu and metaphase ii oocytes were injected with morphologically normal spermatozoa . embryos at 6 - 8 cell stage with less than 50% fragmentation were sent for genetic screening and analysed using pgd / polymerase chain reaction ( pcr ) technique . the results showed that 5 ( 33% ) of the biopsied cells were unaffected by an anemia . the remaining 3% of the cells were affected by sickle-cell disease . pgd was

pulse rate , also called heart rate , is the number of heartbeats per unit of time , commonly used as beats per minute ( bpm ) . we previously reported pulse rate - associated loci from the korea association resource ( kare ) genomewide association study ( gwas ) comprising 8,842 samples in the korean population . in this study , we extended the previous study and reexamined the relevance of single - nucleotide polymorphisms ( snps ) associated with pulse rate from an additional 3,703 korean samples . for the replication stage , subjects were recruited from the health examinee cohorts , called hexa - shared cohorts . from 4,302 genotyped samples , we excluded samples with a high missing genotype rate ( > 5% ) , minor allele frequency ( < 0.01 ) , and significant deviation from hardy - weinberg equilibrium ( p < 1  10 ) . the snp association for pulse rate was tested by linear regression analysis with an additive model , adjusting for age , sex , and recruitment area . the kare gwas and replication gwas were combined by an inverse - variance meta - analysis method , assuming fixed effects , with cochran 's q test used to

chylous ascites is a rare complication of aortic surgery and is an unusual complication following live donor nephrectomy . we describe the case of a 39-year - old woman who underwent an elective , living - related , transperitoneal left - sided totally robot - assisted donor kidney transplantation using the da vinci surgical system . on postoperative day 19 , the patient reported bleeding from the pfannenstiel incision without any further symptoms . on physical examination , she had a distended abdomen , with tenderness on palpation on the right and the left side , and below the umbilicus , with a shifting dullness on percussion during movement . an abdominal ultrasonography showed intra - abdominal fluid in all four quadrants of the abdomen and a diagnostic paracentesis demonstrated yellow fluid with a lipid aspect , suspected of being chylous Ascites and confirmed by biochemical analysis showing positive triglycerides of 57.98 mmol / l . the patient was treated with drainage and a medium - chain triglyceride - based diet . this is the first reported complication of postoperative chylosclerotic ascites after a total kidney transplant .
