objective . to investigate the association between epicardial fat thickness ( eft ) and diurnal blood pressure ( bp ) changes in patients with recently diagnosed essential hypertension ( eh ) , with a gender subgroup analysis . methods . this cross - sectional observational single - center cohort study included patients who underwent 24 h ambulatory bp monitoring ( abpm ) and echocardiography between january 2008 and october 2013 . a total of 441 consecutive patients , who underwent office bp measurements , 24-hour abpm , and laboratory measurements for cardiovascular risk factors , were analyzed . results . a higher prevalence of hypertension among overweight adults was found in women than in men , which may indicate different susceptibilities based on gender . eft was found to be significantly higher in women with obesity ( p < 0.001 ) than in those with hypertension ( p = 0.002 ) . in addition , a higher proportion of patients with obesity had higher eft than those without obesity . conclusion . obesity was associated with a higher incidence of hypertension and a lower prevalence of eft .

objectivekeyless abdominal rope - lifting surgery ( kars ) is a novel , gasless ( isobaric ) , single - incision laparoscopic surgical technique used in various gynecologic operations and cholecystectomies . in this study , we aimed to compare the postoperative pain levels for kars and conventional multiport laparoscopy performed for adnexal cysts.materials and methodswe performed a cross - sectional prospective clinical study of women with benign ovarian cysts operated on between november 2011 and june 2013 . the patients were divided into two groups according to the women 's choice as kars or conventional co2 laparotomy . the initial visual analog scale ( vas ) scores of 5.7 and 6.3  1.42 in patients undergoing kars ( n = 30 ) and conventional laparotomies ( n=30 ) indicated that we needed at least 30 operations in each group to perform a study with a statistical power of 80% at an  level of .05 . the demographic and physical characteristics of the participating patients included their age , gravidity , parity , height , weight , and body mass index , as well as the number of abortions , ectopic pregnancies ,

purposeto examine the potential patient- and procedure - related risk factors for post - endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography ( ercp ) pancreatitis ( pep ) in the iranian population.materials and methodsduring a four - year period ( 20082012 ) , 780 patients who underwent ercp in a tertiary care hospital were analyzed in this prospective study . the patients were referred to this center from different parts of iran , which enhances the ability to generalize the findings for the population.resultsthe prevalence of post - ercp pep ranged from 1% to 15.1% . the prevalence of pep was significantly higher in patients with a history of active pancreatitis than in those without it . erythrocyte sedimentation rate ( esr ) at 2448 hours after ercp was higher in the patients with pep compared to those without pep ( p<0.001 ) . esr was also higher among patients with active pep than in patients without it ( p=0.002 ) . the presence of upper abdominal pain 24 hours after an ercp procedure and a serum amylase level more than the upper limit of normal were significantly associated with higher risk of post-er

aim : the aim of this study was to evaluate and compare the efficacy of 4% articaine hydrochloride and 2% lignocaine for the orthodontic extraction.materials and methods : the study was carried out on 50 patients at outpatient department of oral and maxillofacial surgery who needed bilateral maxillary premolar extractions . all the patients were explained about visual analog scale ( vas ) before injecting local anesthesia . experimental sites ( group 1 ) were injected with 0.5 - 1.5 ml of articaine hcl containing 1:100000 adrenaline , incrementally in the buccal vestibule . no palatal anesthesia was injected , but the desired anesthetic effect was achieved with the above . on the other hand , control sites were injected 0.8 - 1 ml of 2.8 ml of lignoceltic acid in the same vein . pain rating and duration of anesthesia were recorded for the entire patients.results:the mean administered volume of 4.4% and 2.7% of the total anesthesia was administered in group 1 and 2 , respectively . the mean onset time was 1.337 0.2369 and 1.012 0.2058 min , whereas in group 2 it was

the rassf1a gene encodes the ras association domain family of proteins , which are thought to be critical regulators of cell survival , cell cycle progression , cell proliferation , and organ growth . rass f1a is the most characterized member of the family and has been implicated in the negative regulation of cell proliferation and apoptosis . although it has been shown to localize to microtubules of proliferating cells , it has not yet been reported to interact with activated ras or other microtubule - associated proteins . to our knowledge , there have been no reports demonstrating the interaction of endogenous ras- and ras - associated protein ( ras ) proteins with endogenous rassF1a . in this review , we summarize the current knowledge regarding the interaction between endogenous and exogenous ras proteins and discuss the potential role of ras as a scaffold for the regulation of apoptosis and growth .

methotrexate ( mtx ) is one of the most commonly used medicines in the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis or psoriatic arthritis . adverse drug effects of mtx are , usually , mild and self - limiting , but may include serious adverse drug reactions such as hematopoietic suppression , pulmonary , and hepatotoxicity . hepatic fibrosis is a usual adverse reaction reported with long - term use of this drug . we report the case of an adult woman who developed acute severe hepatitis following treatment with mtx at a dose of 15 mg / week .

mirizzi syndrome is a rare complication of cholecystolithiasis characterized by jaundice due to compression of the common hepatic duct . the diagnosis may not be immediately apparent , and management is controversial with open surgery still recommended by some authors . endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography ( ercp ) and laparoscopic surgery are useful to confirm the diagnosis and to place a stent through the papilla . a case is detailed herein of a 67-year - old man who presented with abdominal pain , fever , and jaundiced . a dilated bile duct was found on ultrasound , but the gallbladder could not be seen . he was diagnosed with a gallstone in the cystic duct . laparoscopy was not possible at ercp because of the obstruction . the stent was placed through the papilla without papillotomy . the patient recovered uneventfully .

dirofilaria immitis ( the canine heartworm ) is a filarial nematode naturally hosted by dogs , cats , foxes , muskrat , raccoons , and bears . adult worms live in the right heart of these animals and produce a debilitating disease . in the republic of korea ( = korea ) , the first case of human pulmonary dirofilariasis was reported in 2000 . a 48-year - old male was referred to seoul national university hospital after a routine check - up at a local hospital due to abnormal findings seen in chest computed tomography ( ct ) . ct showed a 1.21.0 cm sized subpleural solitary pulmonary nodule at the right lower lobe . although it looked as a benign inflammatory nodule such as cryptococcal infection , malignancy could not be excluded . the patient underwent surgical resection . the resected mass was a gray , firm , and well - demarcated tumor , measuring 1.00.7 cm , with slight pleural indentation and central necrosis , located in the left lower lobe of the lung . microscopically , chronic granulomatous inflammation which was composed of coagulation necrosis with rim of fibrous

backgroundthe maxillary posterior area contains cancellous bones with low bone density and thin cortical bones , the quantity and quality of which are lower than those of the mandibular bone . the purpose of this study was to compare the stress distribution of various types of 2sis that were installed and restored in severely atrophied maxillary molar sites.materials and methodsa finite element analysis ( fea ) was performed on the maxillary first molar from ct images and the occlusal load was transformed into finite element meshes and the crown and implant was smoothened . three - dimensional image data of the human maxillary maxilla were transformed into a finite element finite element model.resultsa total of 5 maxillary sites were restored with 2 short implants ( 2si ) and 5 regular platforms ( np  3.5 mm ) , and 5 wide platforms ( wp  5.0 mm ) were modeled . the mean height of the residual ridge in the 2si sites was significantly higher than that in the wp platform ( p < 0.001 ) . in addition , the mean stress distribution at the implant - bone interface was significantly lower in the short platform than in the regular platform ( rp  4.0 vs. 5.

purpose . the aim of this study was to investigate the skeletal manifestations of human cystic echinococcosis ( hydatidosis ) in serbia , it 's demographic distribution , site involvement , complications , and bone tissue comorbidities . materials and methods . the study comprised all the cases with histologically verified bone hydatids during the period between 1971 and 2010 in the territory of serbia . in order to standardize the data , the radiological assessment was made on standard radiography , having in mind the length of the study period and diagnostic procedures available for that time . results . the mean age of all patients was 40.918.8 years . a total of 24 male patients presented 58.5% of all cases . the thoracic site was the most commonly involved site , while the spine was the predominance of the most involved site . fracture was by far the predominant radiological diagnosis , followed by tumefaction and cyst ( cyst and fracture , respectively ) . pain was the main symptom in 41% of patients , while some patients demonstrated complications such as paraplegia ( 22.0% pathologic fracture ) , scoliosis ( 9.8% ) , and scapula ( 5.

background : workers ' health may be protected by his / her own preventive behavior , despite numerous health hazards in the workplace . however , worker behavior is constrained by various workplace pressures such as working conditions , including what is considered to be accepted working behavior and overall morale of the working group . the government has implemented regulations to protect worker health , such as the occupational safety and health ( osh ) act . to prevent occupational diseases , it is important to manage worker health via health monitoring for early detection of abnormal conditions.methods:in this article , we reviewed the various kinds of surveillance systems for occupational diseases in korea and resultant data , and suggest reformation of the workers health management system.results:since 2000 , the number of occupational diseases has increased continuously from 4,051 in 2000 to 11,472 in 2007 . occupational diseases accounted for 8,721 cases in 2009 , representing a decrease of 1,013 persons ( 10.4% ) compared to the previous 's 9,734 in 2008 ( 9.734 persons in 2008 ) . the proportion of reported cases is very small ( under 1% of total occupational diseases ) . workers ' compensation insurance has been continuously developing since 1960 ; in particular , its coverage has expanded from large -

introductionrational use of medicines is a major problem worldwide and represents the primary source of medical waste and harm . the rational use of medicine is an essential factor in the provision of quality health services , patient safety , and the rational management of health resources . the objective of this study was to assess the quality of prescribing among general practitioners ( gps ) at primary healthcare centers ( phccs ) in sudan.materials and methodsa cross - sectional retrospective study was carried out according to world health organization guidelines over 6 months and included 197 general practitioners and 19,700 prescriptions . the overall index of rational drug prescribing ( irdp ) indicator for gps was below the optimal prescribing practice level.resultswe found disparities in the IRDp between gps at different health facilities . ikyu gps scored lower than those at state ministry of health facilities or other health facilities such as those owned by private interests , universities , and non - governmental organizations . ijku found that the airdp was below optimal for all health facilities.conclusiongeneral practitioners at national insurance fund facilities and those who provide 80 % of the services scored lower in terms of the ird p than those who do not provide 80% of the health services .

blunt traumatic infrarenal aortic injuries are rare , with only a few case reports in the literature . an intimal flap progressing to dissection or pseudoaneurysm is an occurrence identified in 2% or less of the patients , and the majority of these occur in the thoracic aorta . this article presents the case of a 47-year - old woman who presented to the emergency department with back pain after a car accident . computed tomographic ( ct ) scans were obtained of her abdomen and pelvis , and on a single image from the abdominal scan , what appeared to be either a flap or contrast extravasation outside of the aortal lumen could be seen . her care providers had differing opinions as to what was being seen on the image , and since the abnormality was only seen on one image , the exact etiology could not be delineated . operative repair was recommended , but the patient s aort was too small for even the smallest endovascular stent graft available at our institution . therefore , an open replacement of her aortas was performed using a dacron 22  mm graft . in the operating room , a 4  mm tear in the intima had formed a mature

objectiveto investigate the role of proapoptotic enzymes in the regulation of bp1 expression in yeast cells.research design and methods yeast strains carrying proa - tev - rpt1 were grown in 6 l of sd medium to od600 0.8 to 1.2 . yeast extracts were then ground using an mm301 grinding mill ( restch ) under liquid nitrogen following manufacturer s instructions , or using a mortar and a pestle as previously described.resultsfrozen yeast samples were drop - frozen in liquid nitrogen , and the supernatants were mixed with rabbit igg resin ( cappel , mp biomedicals ) for 90 min at 4c . the resulting supernatant was then released from the resin by incubating with actev protease ( invitrogen ) at 2.5 unit / l culture in proteasome buffer containing 2 mm atp and protease inhibitors for 1 hr at 30c.resultsglycerol - containing eluates were concentrated using ultrafree-0.5 centrifugal filter device with 30 kda nmwl ( millipore ) . glycerol content was significantly higher in eluates than in yeast extracts ( p < 0.05).conclusionproa-tev-1

objective : the burden of head injury is greatest in low- and middle - income countries ( lamic ) , where 85% of the world 's population live . however , data is lacking on the actual load of vegetative patients and their outcomes , especially from developing countries.materials and methods : a prospectively maintained neurotrauma registry was queried from may 2010 to february 2013 for patients who had severe traumatic brain injury glasgow coma score ( gcs ) 8 at admission and motor response of m5 or lower on the gcs at discharge . demographic and clinical data was analyzed , and outcome assessed at 6 months after injury using a telephonic questionnaire.results:a total of 166 patients were included in the study . of these 166 ( 10.9% ) were unconscious at discharge from the hospital . the average hospital stay was 24.31 days ( range : 864 days ) and was 16.71 days for patients with m5 , 20.63 days for m4 and 26.33 days for those with m3 and m2 . the motor score was m5 in 32 ( 19.3% ) , 44 ( 44.5% ) and m3 in 59 ( 35.5%).conversion to

highly marbled beef from wagyu and hanwoo cattle is highly prized for traditional meat cooking methods such as sukiyaki for japanese and gogigui for korean consumers . the use of heifers and steers instead of bulls , intensive feeding system , and genetic ability of these breeds have resulted in greater fat deposition in these breeds compared to european breeds . as intramuscular fat ( imf ) improves beef quality at least in juiciness and flavor , the current paper reviews the characteristics and health benefit of highly marbled meat from these two breeds .

n-(2-mercaptopropionyl)glycine ( mpg ) , also known as tiopronin , is a synthetic aminothiol antioxidant . nac has been in clinical use for more than 40 years , primarily as a mucolytic agent in a variety of respiratory illness . intravenous and oral administration of nac have been extensively used in the management of paracetamol ( acetaminophen ) poisoning . in the present work ,                  we report a simple and cost - effective spectrophotometric method for the reliable analysis of mpg and nac in pharmaceutical formulations . the method is also based on the coupled redox - complexation reaction between nac and mpg , but uses 2,4,6-tripyridyl - s - triazine ( tptz ) as the chromogenic reagent .

introduction : management of arteriovenous malformations ( avms ) remains challenging because of their unpredictable behavior and high recurrence rate if treated by curettage alone without inadequate ligation of the feeder vessels . these lesions are treated by variety of techniques over the years , including surgical excision , irradiation , electrocoagulation , cryotherapy , intravascular magnesium or copper needles , systemic corticosteroids , interferon- , embolization and cryotherapy . sclerotherapy is an effective , conservative and low cost technique for the treatment of benign vascular lesions , included vascular malformation.case report : a twenty one-year - old saudi male patient presented to the department of oral and maxillofacial surgery clinic at king abulaziz university complaining of swelling on the right side of the face . extra - oral clinical examination revealed swelling of the right maxillary quadrant , which is soft and compressible . intraoral examination showed a bluish swelling at the junction of soft and hard palate that bleed easily on touch . computerized tomography ( ct ) and magnetic resonance image ( mri ) studies revealed that the lesion is a deeply seated vascular lesion . the patient was advised to have ethanol directly

objectivethe aim of this study was to characterize the risk factors for development of rickets in extremely low birth weight ( elbw ) infants fed with high mineral containing preterm formula.methodsthis was a retrospective case - control study of 55 extremely low - birth weight infants admitted into the severance children 's hospital neonatal intensive care unit ( nicu ) from 2004 to 2008 . all infants had their serum alkaline phosphatase ( alp ) , calcium ( ca ) and phosphate ( p ) levels reported weekly and bone screen radiography at 1 month of age . parenteral nutrition associated cholestasis ( pnac , defined as direct bilirubin [ db ] greater than 2.0 mg / dl ) , bronchopulmonary dysplasia ( bpd ; using nih classification ) , retinopathy of prematurity ( rop , included above stage 2 ) , and intraventricular hemorrhage ( ivh , above grade 3 ) were analyzed as co - morbidities.resultsthere were no significant differences in age , gestational age , bpd , or ivh between the 55 cases and the 55 controls ( p < 0.001 ) . there was no significant difference

kyphosis is a progressive curvature of the thoracic spine , which results in rounding or bowing of the back . kyphosis presents unique challenges for an ophthalmic surgeon , who is used to patients neck and head resting flat on the operating table and the eye in the horizontal position . intraoperative difficulties include poor view , inability to maneuver surgical instruments properly and constant threat of elevated vitreous pressure . several innovative solutions have been published , including the maximal reverse trendelenburg position and pillows under the head and neck , use of a donut and wedge - shaped head positioner , allowing patients to remain in the upright sitting position while their head is positioned horizontally in a modified waiting room chair , performing surgery while standing , using a 4-section orthopedic operating table , or laying the patients on their sides . however , a 49-year - old obese male with controlled secondary glaucoma needed cataract surgery in the right eye . the patient could only recline to about 40 from the vertical axis . a single 7/0 vicryl ( polyglactin ) corneal retraction suture was placed at the limbus at the 6 o'clock position to achieve the desired view . the assistant surgeon was

the cleavage of the c = n bonds is a very important transformation in organic synthesis as the functionality is widely used to protect both the carbonyl and amine . the surfactant , due to its hydrophobic and hydrophilic nature , forms micelles with water insoluble organic compounds and promotes the desired reactions to occur inside the hydrophobia ambience of the micelle core . here we report the dual catalytic activity of the system i2-sds - h2o which behaves as a lewis acid for the cleavage and deprotection of 2,3-diaza-1 ,3-butadiene , 1-aza-1,3,4-butadienes , and oximes to produce carbonyls and amines and the resulting reaction mixture reacts with indoles to produce bis- and tris ( indolyl)alkanes in situ at room temperature under neutral conditions .

parental drug misuse and its effects on children and families are policy priorities in the uk and elsewhere . however , existing research on drug use and parenting has tended to focus on opioids , opioid substitution therapy ( ost ) , or crack cocaine , and in the case of pregnancy , also alcohol , nicotine , marijuana and amphetamines . in this paper , we examine accounts provided by polydrug - dependent parents about their use of , and dependence on , benzodiazepines as compared to ost . we discuss the impact of polydrug use on parenting capacity and children s health and wellbeing .

objectiveto explore the vascular and cardiac changes of osteoprotegerin ( opg ) and tnf - related apoptosis - inducing ligand ( trail ) in experimental models of dyslipidemia and diabetes.research design and methodstwenty-four male apolipoprotein e null ( apoe ) mice and 12 male c57bl/6j ( cnt ) mice were studied for 14 weeks . at 6 weeks of age , apoe mice were further randomized to either apoe + dm or streptozotocin ( stz ) . systolic blood pressure ( sbp ) , total cholesterol , triglycerides , and hdl cholesterol were measured in the sera by autoanalyzer technique . at the end of the study , opg and trail levels were measured by elisa and duet systems , respectively.resultsof the 24 apoe and 12 cnt mice , only the apoe group showed a significant increase in the levels of opg ( p < 0.001 ) and trail ( p = 0.002 ) compared with the control group ( p > 0.05).conclusionstz and stz significantly increased the opg / trail ratio in the heart and blood vessels in apoe

palmyrolide a is a macrolide that is known to act as a neurotoxin at the voltage - gated sodium channel ( vgsc ) subunit of the neurotoxin veratridine . we have previously demonstrated that it acts as a partial agonist at this site . in this study , we investigated the ability of palmyrolides 14 , 7 , 8 , and 11 to antagonize the effects of veratidine - induced elevation of neuronal [ na]i in murine primary cerebrocortical neurons in the presence and absence of ( )-palmyroride a and its analogues and to block the effect of the drug on ca oscillation in neuro-2a cells ( ic50 = 5.2 m ) . the compounds were synthesized via the hydrogenation of diastereomers 1 and 2 , and their analogues were prepared exploiting the known acid - catalyzed ring opening of 1 , followed by treating the resultant aldehyde with either nabh4 or naclo2 . in total , 47 murine neurons were loaded with the na - binding benzofuran isophthalate ( sbfi ) and were monitored with sodium influx ( 340/380 ) responses to verat

molecular forensics is often associated with the detection of highly pathogenic microbes to which humans are deliberately exposed in cases of biological terrorism . however , human fingertip microflora left behind on touched objects at crime scenes may potentially contain forensically relevant information that may be useful for human host inferences accessible via microbial dna fingerprinting of physical fingerprints . for example , if endogenous microbial skin species / strains with a geographically restricted distribution could be retrieved from touched objects via dna analysis , the geographic origin of the human host individual could be determined indirectly . this information could be useful in suspect - less forensic cases where the evidence dna sample does not match either a suspect s dna profile or any in a criminal dna database . in such cases , geographic information derived from crime scene samples is expected to reduce the potential pool of suspects by allowing police investigations to concentrate on specific groups of people .

key clinical messageassisted / supported mechanical ventilation is designed to interact with patient muscle activity and ' share ' the work of breathing . if properly done , the ventilatory 's flow and pressure delivery must synchronize with patient effort during all three phases of breath delivery : breath initiation , flow delivery , and breath termination ( cycling ) . dys - synchronous interactions with non - invasive mechanical ventilation ( niv ) can lead to overload of the muscle ( ' imposed ' loads ) , compromise alveolar ventilation , overdistendendal units , disrupt sleep patterns , and cause patient discomfort , prompting additional sedation . patient - ventilator interactions are complex and are important to recognize and manage . understanding how common and common these dys-synchronous interactions are during niv has demonstrated how common they are and that understanding them requires understanding the complex interactions of respiratory gas exchange , muscle function , and neural drive .

a twelve year old boy presented to the children s assessment unit with a 24 hour history of unilateral face and neck swelling on a background of seven days progressive sore throat and dysphagia . a provisional diagnosis of tonsillitis had been made five days earlier by the local gp and a course of erythromycin and penicillinv commenced . examination revealed tender swellings over the left temporal area , angle of the mandible and upper third of the sternocleidomastoid extending into the submandibular region . the oral cavity was complicated by trismus , however no obvious tonsillar enlargement , erythema or exudate was recorded . initial blood results showed an elevated c - reactive protein of 155mg / l and white cell count of 16.6(10 * 9l ) . initial imaging of the neck comprised an ultrasound scan ; this demonstrated normal right sided vasculature but abnormal left external jugular vein ( ejv ) and retromandibular veins . these veins appeared dilated with increased wall vascularity and showed no recordable signal on doppler imaging , an appearance in keeping with an inflammatory thrombophlebitis . despite a coincidental finding of a smaller calib

backgroundhelicobacterium pylori is a gram - negative , spiral and microaerophilic microorganism that was first isolated from the stomach of patients afflicted with peptic ulcer . it is suspected that gastroesophageal reflux is involved in the pathogenesis of chronic otitis media . however , the correlation between chronic suppurative otitis and various aspects , such as tympanosclerosis , has not been sufficiently studied.objectivesthe aim of this study was to determine the role of helicobacter pylora in the development of the middle ear mucosa.material/methodsthis case - control and cross - sectional study was conducted on patients who were diagnosed with chronic infection of middle ear and were candidates to undergo surgery in the imam khomeini or apadana hospitals of ahvaz in 2013 . during the operations , samples were taken of the mucosa of the patients . in patients with sclerotic plaques , the samples were kept under sterilized conditions at 20 degrees centigrade , and were eventually sent to the laboratory . in the laboratory , dna was extracted using the roche high - pure template pcr kit ( roche , germany ) according to the manufacture

trigeminal neuralgia ( tn ) is a disorder of cranial nerve ( cn ) v that results in severe episodes of shock - like or lancinating pain in one or more of its three divisions ( v1v3 ) . the majority of cases of tn are the consequence of focal compression of the entry zone of the root of the trigeminal nerve , while only 2% of cases are observed in patients diagnosed with multiple sclerosis ( ms ) . although the use of radiosurgery ( srs ) has proven to be an effective management approach for medically   and surgically   refractory tn as a primary and repeat treatment modality , questions remain regarding optimal treatment modalities in specific patient subsets . for this reason , the goal of this paper is to provide a modern review of the literature thoroughly analyzing the efficacy of gkrs in the treatment of patients with tn , as well as evaluating the treatment planning and methods associated with this evolving modality .

a 55-year - old caucasian female underwent elective coronary artery bypass surgery for a recent myocardial infarction . the patient was intubated without any difficulty after routine intravenous induction with etomidate , sufentanil , and rocuronium . as the cardiac surgeon harvested the left internal mammary artery , the first assistant harvested the saphenous vein from the left leg via an endoscopic approach using co2 insufflation at 4 l / min and a pressure of 14 mmhg . mid - esophageal right ventricular inflow - outflow view revealed a massive snowflake appearance suggestive of gas bubbles from the right atrium to the right ventricle . we concluded that the gas bubbles were not caused from turbulence due to intravenous fluids or from a disconnected intravenous line , so a co2 embolism was suspected . however , the patient 's hemodynamic status improved shortly after endoscopic vein harvesting was completed , and the use of endoscopic co2 infufflation was discontinued .

drywood termites cause significant damage to wood in structures in the united states , with two species , incisitermes minor ( hagen ) and cryptotermes brevis ( walker ) , being responsible for the majority of damage . the size , number , and dispersion of drywood termite colonies in a structure can vary greatly , depending on the age of the infestation and the success of preventative or remedial treatments . a commonly used sign for determining the presence of termites in wood is the occurrence of hard , even - shaped fecal pellets , ejected through a kick - out hole in the external surface of wood from the internal galleries . these pellets often are found as conical piles or are scattered on horizontal surfaces below infested wood . we postulated that these pellets could be chemically characterized so as to determine the status of a colony as active ( alive ) or inactive ( dead ) . here , we report quantification of the hydrocarbons in pellets of i. minor aged for up to 1 year after they were produced . we document the changes in proportions of selected hydrocarbonbons as an indication of the length of time since the pellets were excreted .

summarythere is a need for computer methods that can calculate the aqueous solvation free energies of solute molecules accurately and efficiently . the semi - explicit assembly ( sea ) water model is computationally expensive , smears out the particulate nature of water molecules , and has limited transferability to situations for which they have not been optimized . here , we describe field - sea , a new approach that uses a solvation                 iously free - energy field surface instead of precomputed solvation waters . the new approach is a considerable improvement over sea , which gives accurate                 cynical free energies for ionic and neutral solutes , at no additional computational cost and with no degradation of the predictions .

glutathione s - transferase ( gst ) is a cytosolic enzyme involved in the cellular detoxification of a broad range of chemical substrates . gst enzyme activity of hydatid cyst protoscolices ( parasite ) , healthy and cystic liver tissues were compared and mentioned its importance for diagnostic biomarker in the diagnosis of the disease . in the present study , gst activity of the parasite protosol and liver extract were assayed spectrophotometrically at 25c with reduced glutathione ( gsh ) and 1-chloro-2 , 4-dinitrobenzene ( cdnb ) as substrates and compared with that of healthy liver tissues . the results showed that the gsts enzyme activity was significantly higher in the liver of parasite than that of the healthy liver tissue . in addition , gsts activity was higher in liver of parasites than that in healthy liver . gsts of helminth parasite may protect against exogenous xenobiotics as a result of immune effectors mechanisms from the host . in this study , the gst enzymes activity of parasite and liver were compared in the cytosol of protosolic and liver protosoli of sheep with respect to that of liver

objectivethe risk of stroke and myocardial infarction is considerably increased in subjects with diabetes . already at the time of diagnosis of type 2 diabetes , many patients have manifest cardiovascular disease ( cvd ) . this could be due to a long presymtomatic period with increased glucose level as evident by the increased cvd risk already present with impaired glucose tolerance ( igt ) . recently , increased plasma plasminogen activator inhibitor-1 ( pai-1 ) activities have been found to be a very early risk marker of the insulin resistance syndrome and diabetes . furthermore , haemostatic variables related to endothelial function , such as von willebrant factor and factor viii , also predicted diabetes , especially in women . data are lacking regarding other aspects of the fibrinolytic system and the development of diabetes . firstly , do subjects with normal glucose tolerance , who later convert to diabetes , have disturbances in the endothelial system that could explain their increased risk of cvd even before diabetes ensues ? secondly , if so , do these changes predict future diabetes , independent of the influence of the pancreas , thereby pointing to other pathways to the diabetic state such as endothelial dysfunction ? in this study , we studied

objectiveto provide clinicians with efficacy results that would allow a comparison of duloxetine 60 mg / day with four approved selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors ( ssris ) for the treatment of major depressive disorder ( mdd ) in japan.research design and methodsthe eli lilly clinical trial database contains all randomized controlled trials that have been conducted outside of japan for the acute treatment of patients with mdd . four ssris ( eg , sertraline , escitalopram , fluvoxamine , and fluvotrol ) were included in the analysis . the primary outcome measure was the mean change in hamilton rating scale for depression ( hamd17 ) total score from baseline to the end of the study ( week 8 ) . the secondary outcomes were the safety and efficacy of the drugs compared with placebo.resultsa total of four studies ( three studies on 40 or 80 mg or 20 mg , and one study on 60 mg/day ) were selected . the mean changes in ham d17 total score were significantly different between the treatments ( p < 0.001 ) . no significant differences were found between the treatment groups for the safety , efficacy , and tolerability . in addition , there were no statistically significant differences

objectives : to get students to reflect systematically and critically upon conventional and thus familiar ways of thinking and acting , expectations and beliefs in medicine ; to get them to reflect upon central concepts such as  bedarf / bedrfnis ( needs in  objective  vs. subjective  terms ) that are normative in character and have a structuring and guiding effect on medical care services ; to advance and refine students soft skills ; to employ the method of participant observation in order to gain awareness of patients concrete social and environmental living conditions , in this case people on the fringe of society ; and to raise students awareness of what can be deemed suitable support services for people whose behaviour is not consistent with the conventional expectations of health professionals.methods : this paper sets out to discuss the possibility of supplementing the above - described methods with methodological approaches and concepts from another discipline based in cultural studies , namely medical anthropology ( or medical ethnology ) , and to apply these in the context of a specifically designed form of experience to which students are exposed outside of germany . the objectives of the present paper are as follows :    to introduce a new teaching concept in the institute for the history of medicine at giessen university , namely , medical anthropology : observation and change in

foodborne liver and intestinal flukes are important groups of parasites from the public health point of view . the present study was performed to determine the actual status of infection with foodborne trematodes by recovery of adult flukes from riparian people of saravane and champasak province , lao pdr . a total of 1,460 fecal samples ( 1 sample per person ) were collected from the villagers ( 717 men and 743 women , 12 - 82 years - of - age ) in 12 villages ( in 6 districts ) in both regions . the prevalence of opisthorchis viverrini , haplorchis taichui , prosthodendrium molenkampi , phaneropsolus bonnei , centrocestus formosanus , echinostoma revolutum , artyfechinostomum malayanum , echinochasmus japonicus , and euparyphium sp . were detected in all villages . the frequency of infection varied by locality , and the intensity of infection ranged from 0.5% to 100% . the incidence of cholangiocarcinoma was significantly higher in villages with higher prevalence of o. v

health information is increasingly available at the click of a button with blue button . thanks in part to the power of health information technology , individuals have new opportunities to be part of the solution when it comes to assessing and improving data quality . building on a framework for data quality , consuming as the source of patient - centered data , consumers have important roles to play in reviewing and confirming their own health information and should be engaged partners in efforts to understand and improve the quality of electronic health data . for patients and families , the issue of data quality is personal . when treatment decisions or research conclusions are based on incomplete or inaccurate information , a misdiagnosis , an incomplete medication list , or the wrong demographic data , the wrong information is often the difference between life and death . recognizing patients as sources of valuable information and powerful forces for change in identifying and preventing errors will also help to foster a culture of safety and build a sense of partnership between providers and patients in the mutual pursuit of better health and care .

adverse drug reactions ( adrs ) , including interactions , in older people are a common cause of admission to hospital and are also a cause of disease in hospitalized patients . frail elderly patients appear to be particularly at risk of adrs . in some cases , this is because insufficient account is taken of the effect of age and frailty on the pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic of the drug , especially in relation to hepatic2 and renal elimination . moreover , patients with severe cognitive impairment may not be able to refer symptoms related to an adr , making the recognition of this clinical condition a medical problem . we report the case of a 90-year - old caucasian male , admitted to the university hospital of pisa for dyspnea , who experienced rhabdomyolysis related to clarithromycin treatment . the patient was affected by alzheimer s disease , in advanced phase , and did not take any home - medication as referred by his general physician . at admission in the emergency room , mental confusion ( the relatives referred progressive cognitive impairment for several months ) , sinus tachycardia ( 115 beats per minute ) , basilar wheezes , and rhonchi . at the second day of hospitalization , for continuing sinus

supravalvar mitral ring is a circumferential ridge or membrane arising from the left atrial wall overlying the mitral valve and frequently attached to the valve . it is a complex combination of four congenital heart defects : supravalvar valve ring , parachute mitral , parachute aortic valve , subvalvarortic stenosis , and coarctation . we herein present a case of a 17-year - old male , who presented with syncope . about 15 years ago , he had undergone a cardiac operation for ventricular septal defect ( vsd ) closure and valve repair . in chest x - ray both lungs were clear , and the electrocardiogram result showed normal sinus rhythm , had undergone right bundle branch block , and right ventricular hypertrophy . transthoracic echocardiography revealed ejection fraction of 5055% , pulmonary hypertension with pulmonary artery pressure ( pap ) of 80/40 mm hg , and pulmonary capillary wedge pressure of 23 mm . cardiac catheterization revealed a severely restricted diastolic flow ( mean peak gradient = 9.3 mmhg ) . there was a free floating high redundant obstructive membrane in the atrium that was attached

in the recent millennium , the constant trend of change in the demands of the community as well as transforming the trend of knowledge production has highlighted the necessity for researchers to adopt a more comprehensive approach . increasingly , many academic disciplines are utilizing qualitative research ( qr ) as the qualitative method investigating the why and how of the process of a developed concept . the authors of the present article endeavor to shine a light on the ethical issues affecting researchers and propose strategies to face the ethical challenges of qualitative studies , so as to provide applicable and trustworthy outcomes . this could be the basis for the formulation of specific guidelines in this regard .

abstractthis study was carried out to evaluate the antibiotic resistance of bacteria in fish and other cultured organisms from various aquaculture farms in sarawak , malaysian borneo , including kuching , bintulu , limbang , miri , and sampadi ( lundu ) . ninety - four bacterial isolates from 17 different genera were assessed for their susceptibility to different antibiotics utilizing the disk diffusion method according to the method described by clinical and laboratory standards institutes ( clsi ) on mueller - hinton agar ( mha ) . the results obtained were analyzed as the standard interpretative table by clsi and bonnet multiple antibiotic resistance index ( mar ) . in this study , commonly used antibiotics commonly used in veterinary and human medicine were selected for the antibiotic susceptibility testing . the mar index was determined for each isolate by dividing the number of antibiotics to which an isolate is resistant with the total number of antibiotic tested . the risk contamination was calculated to identify the risk of potentially hazardous human exposure to the isolates . the prevalence of resistance to different classes of antibiotics ranged from 0.2% to 100% . the most commonly tested antibiotics were sulfonamides , chloramphenicol , nitrofurans , oxyt

introductionwith an incidence rate of 0.92% , maternal puerperal lower extremity nerve injuries are rare . bilateral medial calcaneal nerve neuropathy as a result of external compression is a rare complication of epidural obstetric analgesia.case presentationa 32-year - old woman presented to our neurology outpatient clinic with tingling and numbness in both heels . she continuously complained about these sensations ever since she had given vaginal birth to her first child 3 months earlier in a hospital . spinal analgesia ( lumbar level , bupivacaine / sufentanyl bolus , followed by a continuous administration of 8 ml / h ) was used during labour . because of fetal compromise during the expulsion phase , a vacuum delivery system ( palmpump kiwi ) , together with episiotomy , was used for fetal extraction . epidural analgesia was continued during delivery . three months after presentation ( 6 months after delivery ) , her numbness had diminished , but had not completely disappeared . she had hypoaesthesia in the area of both heels.conclusiona diagnosis of bilateral neuropathy of the medial calceal nerve , most likely due to longstanding pressure on both heels due to external compression during vaginal delivery with epidural

backgroundalthough several studies worldwide have reported on the epidemiology of feline hemoplasmosis in sick or healthy client - owned pet cats , there have been few studies investigating stray cats in northern italy . the aim of this study was to evaluate , using a conventional pcr assay , the prevalence of mycoplasma haemofelis ( mhf ) and candidatus mycominutum ( cmhm ) infections in stray cats from colonies in milan and to identify possible risk factors for these infections.material/methodsa total of 260 stray cats were collected during a 2-year period ( january 2008 to january 2010 ) under a trap - neuter - release ( tnr ) program approved by the local authority of the city council . samples were collected aseptically from the jugular vein while cats were anaesthetized and placed in serum separator tubes . within 24 h of collection , a complete blood count ( cbc ) was performed on whole blood samples using an advia 120 system ( milan siemens healthcare diagnostics , milan , italy ) . the results were analyzed using spss software version 20.resultsthe prevalence of infection with feline haemoplasmas ranged from

objectivethe aim of this study was to evaluate the persian version of the conners adult adhd rating scale ( caars ) self - report ( screening version ) for the diagnosis of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder ( adhd ) in patients with motor vehicle accidents.materials and methodsin a case - control study , 140 patients including two equal groups of traumatic due to traffic injuries and non - traumatic patients were evaluated . the diagnosis was made using the persians version of conner 's adult adhrd rating scale , and the psychiatric comorbidities were assessed according to dsm - iv - tr using structured clinical interview for dsm- iv ( scid).resultsthere were no significant differences between the case group and the control group in age , sex , educational level , job , accident history , daily driving amount , adhd index , a subscale ( attention deficit symptoms ) , b ( hyperactivity - impulsivity symptoms ) and c ( total adhd symptoms ) subscale , and adhd total index ( subscale d ) . in addition , the p values less than 0.05 were considered statistically significant in both groups.conclusionin this study , the internal consistency of persian - version of caars was found to

crh is a 41-amino - acid peptide hormone produced in the hypothalamus by the hypothalamic - pituitary - adrenal ( hpa ) axis as part of the stress response . it is also produced in peripheral tissues including the placenta of humans and hominine primates . the mechanism regulating the exponential increase in placental crh expression remains unclear although positive feedback by glucocorticoids and increasing numbers of syncytial cell nuclei are suggested explanations . in the present study we investigated whether methylation of the human crh proximal promoter contributes to the control of crh gene expression in trophoblast cells . we used the bewo human choriocarcinoma - derived cell line exhibiting dynamic dna methylation as well as ability to increase crh protein expression when stimulated with 8-bromoadenosine-3,5-cyclic monophosphate ( 8- camp ) . we found that methylation at the cpg island of the crh promoter is associated with increased expression of the cyclic - amp response element ( cre ) sequence , suggesting that the methylation is involved in the regulation of the transcription factor binding affinity of the cre . furthermore , we observed that the expression

lincosamides are a group of clinically important antibiotics that block bacterial protein synthesis , which takes place on the ribosomes . the three compounds lincomycin ( a natural antibiotic produced by streptomyces lincolnensis and discovered in 1961 ) , clindamycin , and pirlimycin are two semi - synthetic derivatives of the natural antibiotic . the main spectrum of action of these compounds includes bacteria associated with skin infections , and they are first - choice antibiotics used in veterinary dermatology . however , extensive use of these antibiotics has led to increased resistance in many strains of bacteria , which have been developing various mechanisms to counter these drugs . one known mechanism is structural modification of the drug s target ( ribosome ) through the methylation of 23s rna ( e.g. , base no. , no. 2058 ) and/or mutations . other mechanisms of resistance are active efflux , or enzymatic deactivation . bacterial resistance together with side effects are most important reasons for designing modified compounds . the conformational properties of lincosamide and its derivatives have been studied using x - ray techniques , as well as h and c nmr spectroscopy and molecular dynamics . the conformation of the free conformers

introducing quality assurance frameworks to improve the quality of care in primary care - based memory clinics ( pcmcs ) is a challenge for the health - care system . this paper describes the results of a consensus approach to identify quality indicators ( qis ) and quality improvement mechanisms for dementia care in an ontario - wide network of interprofessional pcmc networks , and identify potential barriers and facilitators to the implementation of a quality assurance framework . a delphi technique was deployed to obtain agreement from p cmc clinicians and dementia specialists on preferred qis and improvement mechanisms . we identified 38 candidate qis , including those based on existing clinical guidelines and quality indicator and improvement compendiums , which were reviewed by the authors guided by respondent comments . the authors independently analyzed all written comments using descriptive content analysis and incorporated the feedback into the presentation of qis in the second round of the delphi round . a total of 38 qis were identified , of which 38 were selected as candidate mechanisms for the development of a framework . the majority of respondents ( 86% ) agreed that the proposed framework would improve quality of dementia care through targeted , educational quality improvement measures .

a 56-year - old caucasian male patient with a past medical history that included hypertension and coronary artery disease and a recent serum creatinine of 88.4 mol / l , was diagnosed 10 days earlier with urinary tract infection ( uti ) . two days before presentation to us , he developed worsening anorexia , nausea with vomiting and oliguria . renal ultrasound showed unilateral moderate right - sided hydronephrosis with suspected mass effect on the inferior urinary bladder . a non - contrast computerized tomography examination , on day 2 , again showed unilateral right-sided hydronesphrosis , bilateral nephric stranding and urinary bladder wall thickening suspicious for transitional cell cancer . a percutaneous left nephrostomy was performed . the pathology report revealed high - grade urothelial carcinoma , grade 3 of 3 , with invasion of the muscularis propria .

phage display technology is an in vitro methodology and system for presenting , selecting , and evolving proteins and peptides displayed on the surface of phage virion . phages reacting with contaminants in the target sample or other components of the screening system such as the solid phase ( e.g. , plastic plates ) and the capturing molecule rather than binding to the actual target are recovered with those target - specific binders ( displaying mimotopes ) during the rounds of panning . target - unrelated peptides ( tup ) , a term coined recently by menendez and scott in a review , are displayed on these phages , and the mimotope mimicking the binding site on the template and binding to target is defined as mimotopic , which was first introduced by geysen et al . in this study , we describe a web server named sarotup , which is an acronym for scanner and reporter of target- and target-related peptides , which can be freely accessed and used to scan phage - displayed random peptides libraries with a given target . the web server is capable of finding , reporting , and precluding tup , and it is very helpful for the development of new diagnostics , therapeutics , and vaccines . sar

reversible focal lesions in the splenium of the corpus callosum ( scc ) have been found in patients with various conditions including seizures , antiepileptic drug toxicity or withdrawal , viral encephalitis , hypoglycemia , wernicke 's encephalopathy , marchiafava - bignami disease , sympathomimetic - induced kaleidoscopic visual illusion syndrome , hemolytic uremic syndrome , altitude brain injury , and acute axonal injury . the most frequent cause of reversible focal lesions of the scc is influenza virus - associated encephalomyopathy ( iaee ) . here , we report a case of hantaan virus infection and hemorrhagic fever with renal failure syndrome ( hfrs ) .

a 91-year - old gentleman with history of hypertension , dyslipidemia and as presented to our office on 7/2015 with complaints of new onset sub sternal burning pain of 6 weeks duration . prior to 6 weeks patient could walk 2 blocks on level ground without shortness of breath or chest pain . echocardiography ( echo ) done in may of 2014 had revealed critical as with aortic valve area of 0.6 - 0.7 centimeter square and ejection fraction of 65 percent . the patient was directly referred for a cardiac catheterization to evaluate his coronary anatomy prior to avr . left main coronary artery revealed a common ostium - giving rise to the left anterior descending ( lad ) and the circumflex . right coronary artery ( rca ) revealed a 99 % narrowing in its proximal segment and a 90% ostial diagonal narrowing . after a detailed discussion involving the patient , his son , interventional cardiologist and the cardio thoracic surgeon , patient requested that only pci of the rca be considered , as the chest pain was of recent duration with no change in the valve finding . thus , a successful pci was done in the proximal rca with a bare metal stent . patient did extremely well after

there are a number of severe diseases caused by the disruption of adult globin genes , including thalassaemia and certain types of anaemia , which is caused by a reduction or abolition of the expression of one or more globin gene , resulting in an imbalance of - and -globin chains in red blood cells and consequent anaemia . the aim of this paper is to provide an update on the current status of research into the reprogramming of human embryonic stem cells ( erythropoiesis ) into various types of red blood cell ( rbcs ) and to discuss the use of cellular reprogrammers to treat rbc diseases such as sickle cell anaemia and thalassemia . this paper will discuss progress in utilising novel cellular techniques to reprogram adult and embryonic rbcs for the treatment of rbc disorders .

despite the use of a proximal coronary snare for hemostasis , bleeding from the arteriotomy site can occur in the presence of severe atheroma or calcification , causing bleeding through the anastomotic site . we report a case of massive embolism in a patient who underwent aortic root grafting without conversion to cardiopulmonary bypass ( cpb ) . the patient was displaced from the trendelenburg position , and the left anterior descending artery ( lad ) was connected to the ascending aorta via the left internal thoracic artery . during the procedure , the patient 's hemodynamic status deteriorated suddenly , and ventricular fibrillation developed . despite aggressive treatment , including defibrillation , epinephrine , and direct manual cardiac massage , adequate circulation could not be restored , and cardiac massage was continued . transesophageal echocardiography revealed a massive gas floating at the orifice of the mid right coronary artery ( rca ) . vasopressin 0.6 iu was quickly administered intravenously , and massive gas was removed by colloid solution . the remaining grafts were performed successfully , and no sign of bleeding was observed at the puncture site .

multiple sclerosis ( ms ) is a chronic progressive inflammatory and neurodegenerative disease of human central nervous system ( cns ) which usually affects young adults , with a reported median age of onset of approximately 30 years . currently , the cause and cure for ms remain elusive and in many cases ms ends with significant neurological disabilities . the pathophysiologic basis of such conduction block in ms rests on segmental demyelination with loss of whole internodes of myelin . in normal individuals , blocking the voltage - gated potassium channels does not have any significant impact on conduction of the action potential since these channels are enclosed by layers of myelination . however , in ms , loss of the myelin during pathogenesis of ms , exposes the potassium channels and interferes with production and conduction . in fact , the impulse conduction fails specifically at the site of demyelsination , while the normal segments of the axons on both sides continue their conduction normally . it appears that myelin loss involving the paranodal area is stronger factor in blocking conduction than a comparable loss of distributed along the internode . in this paper , we have attempted to unravel the role of ion channels in the pathogenesis and treatment of ms .

purpose . this study aimed to compare the diagnostic value of unprocessed ( psp ) and inverted digital images for detection of peri - implant defects using scanora software . materials and methods . periapical radiographs were obtained using digora optime imaging system ( soredex corporation , helsinki , finland ) , psp size 2 sensor ( 40.0mm30.0 mm2 ) and minray dental x ray system with exposure settings of 60 kv and 7ma for 0.16s . digital image tool and adobe photoshop software programs were used to evaluate all 60 images for the presence or absence of defects using a definite and probable defect e - five - point scale ( uably ) . results . psp and inverted images were found to be more accurate than film - based radiographs ( p < 0.001 ) and film-based images ( p = 0.002 ) for detecting peri- and simulated periodontal lesions ( p > 0.05 ) . no significant difference was found between the two imaging modalities for the detection of implant defects . conclusion . inverted digital image was more sensitive than psp ( p<0.05).conclusions . inverted images are more sensitive for the

aorto - esophageal fistula ( aef ) is an abnormal anatomical communication between the aorta and esophagus is a rare cause of upper gastrointestinal bleeding ( ugib ) . a 70-year - old male patient with hypertension with regular ethanol use , presented to a local hospital with complaints of hematemesis and melena from the past 1-day . esophagogastroduodenoscopy ( egd ) and colonoscopy failed to detect any source of bleeding . computed tomography ( ct ) angiogram was performed and revealed type b aortic dissection with rupture and active extravasation of blood into the esophagia , confirming the diagnosis of aef . he underwent emergency percutaneous thoracic endovascular aortal repair with stent placement . his shock resolved and did not have any more episodes of bleeding thereafter .

organic electronics have become an active research area in recent years because they are an alternative to the traditional semiconductor technology and challenges in design on a smaller scale from microscale to nanoscale . moreover , organic materials have vast potential for integration in low - cost microelectronic devices . organic - based semiconductors , for example polymeric gels , may have superior properties due to their flexibility against to regulate some physical properties of the materials even after the production , e.g. , by changing the volume of the gel . some water - tolerant organic devices may be biocompatible , that is , they may be used in circuits that directly reside on or are implanted inside animal and human tissue and could perform various sensing , interfacing , and controlling functions for drug delivery , prosthetics , and neural - electronic integration . in this paper , we have shown that the principle of the electrical conductivity in polymeric polymers can be changed by doping static ( inactive ) ions having counter ions as charge carriers and these gels can be used to form pn junctions that work similar to inorganic semiconductor semiconductor junctions . the pn junction formed by combining p - type and n - type gels in much closer contact rectifies the current .

leiomyosarcoma metastases from uterine leiomyoma to lymph nodes is a rare occurrence . we report the case of a 61-year - old woman with a history of uterine metastasis who presented with a severe dry cough . a posteroanterior chest x - ray showed a very large mass on the right cardiac border of the right lower lobe . a ct - guided needle biopsy of the huge mass revealed it to be metastatic leiocytoma . the patient was treated with excision of the mass and fine nodular lesions from the parietal and diaphragmatic pleura . two months later , large hypermetabolic masses were found from the left para - aortic area to the left pelvic cavity , and we diagnosed them as metastatic lymphoma . additionally , increased fluorodeoxyglucose uptake was observed in the peribronchial lymph nodes , the mediastinum , and the supraclavicular lymph nodes . en - bloc resection of the retroperitoneal masses with left nephrectomy was performed and the pathologic diagnosis was metastatic cancer . a chest ct scan done 1 year after surgery demonstrated no changes in the pulmonary nodules and

backgroundcopd is a common , usually progressive disease characterized by chronic inflammation of the airways and persistent airflow limitation . this study compared the screening efficacy of the copd population screener ( copd - ps ) and the international primary care airway group ( ipag ) questionnaires in the general japanese population , as no previous research has done so.methodsthis cross - sectional study was based on data from the ongoing population - based epidemiologic study designed to investigate the morbidity , mortality , and risk factors of cardiovascular and smoking - related diseases in the town of hisayama , japan . in 2012 , 2,336 subjects 40 years of age and older were solicited to participate in a town - wide health checkup that included spirometry . of the 2,643 subjects who were enrolled between june 2012 and october 2012 , 307 were excluded for the following reasons : 105 had physician - diagnosed asthma , 22 had a previous lung resection , 159 had poor studied data , and 21 had records with missing data . the subjects who provided informed consent to participate participated in the health check-up independently completed the japanesese versions of thecopd- ps and ipag questionnaires , and then , in addition to their

human cytomegalovirus ( cmv ) infection is one of the major causes of congenital infections . the aim of this study was to assess the incidence and risk of acquiring cmv infection in pregnant women in an urban area in northern italy in the period 20052007 . during the three years 20052007 , the microbiology unit of hospital of legnano , received samples for the detection of cmv antibodies from 2817 pregnant women ( mean age 32 years , range 1546 ; 2522 89.5% ) . forty - eight - eight women ( 1.7% ) were 20 or less years of age , 9.9% were aged between 21 and 40 , and 91 ( 3.2% ) between 41 and 50 . the risk of infection during pregnancy is 0.71.38  100 pregnancies among seronegative women , and 0.20.8  100 among women as a whole . furthermore , the prevalence of infection varies with the seroprevalence of the infection in the population .

non - muscle invasive bladder cancer ( nmibc ) comprises a heterogeneous group of patients and includes pathological stage ta ( confinement to the epithelium or mucosa ) , t1 ( invasion of the subepithelial connective tissue or lamina propria ) and cis ( tis : flat , high - grade , non - papillary carcinomas confined to the urothelium ) . of all newly diagnosed cases of bladder cancer , about 70% present as stage ta , 20% as t1 and 10% as cis . approximately 50 - 70% of patients will recur and roughly 1020% will progress to muscle invasive disease . the most important risk factor for progression is grade , not stage . when considering a patient 's prognosis , it is necessary to consider not only clinical and pathological factors but also take into account the potential effect of the intravesical treatment received and molecular alterations present in the tumors .

personalized medicine is defined by the use of genomic signatures of patients to assign effective therapies in order to achieve the best medical outcomes for individual patients , thus improving public health . despite the variety of clinical , morphological , and molecular parameters  used to classify human malignancies , patients receiving the same diagnosis can have markedly different clinical courses and treatment responses . the current system of  one - size - fits - all- diagnoses is simply not good enough . molecular biomarkers of disease risk and status are critical to an accurate treatment by identifying patients most likely to benefit from particular drugs or experience adverse reactions . the development of a biomarker classifier involves two distinct components : ( 1 ) a procedure for building a classifier and ( 2 ) validation / evaluation procedure for estimating the error rate of biomarker . in this paper , the gene selection algorithm used to obtain a feasible within - group ratio ( bw ratio , ) for ranking genes in the training phases of 20 trials of 10-fold cross - validation ( loocv ) procedure is described . in other words , in each looc v trial , 90% of the selected genes are ranked as significant . the results of the study are presented .

introductionpreimplantation genetic diagnosis ( pgd ) has become an important alternative to prenatal diagnosis for couples at high risk of transmitting inherited disorders to their children . in this study , we describe the successful pregnancy and birth after pgd for ornithine transcarbamylase ( otc ) deficiency with simultaneous analyses of fluorescence in situ hybridization ( fish ) and duplex - nested polymerase chain reaction ( pcr ) in korea.materials and methodsthe otc gene is located on the short arm of the x chromosome within band xp21.1 . it spans 73 kb , has an open reading frame of 1,062 nucleotides , and contains 10 exons and 9 introns . otc deficiency ( mim-#311250 ) is an x - linked and co - dominant metabolic disorder with partial penetrance in females . to our knowledge , only two cases have been reported where specific pgd was carried out using multiplex or duplex pcr assay and healthy babies devoid of the otc mutation were born.resultsin order to avoid a pregnancy with an affected baby , the couple was counseled for pgd in cheil general hospital . in order to determine the efficiency of single cell pcr and allele

purposemusculoskeletal stiffness and limited range of motion ( rom ) can restrict functional joint movement , particularly during ankle supination following neurological diseases . ankle dorsiflexion rom restrictions impair dynamic balance and gait , which may contribute to secondary injuries . we investigated the effects of an unstable inclined board on the active and passive ankle rom in patients with ankle stiffness.materials and methodsthe study included 10 female patients ( mean age , 22.8  5.3  years ; height , 158.9  3.0  cm ; weight , 61.2  4.7  kg ) with chronic ankle stiffness caused by diseases or medical conditions . the patients were assigned randomly to two groups ( both n = 5 ) and their height and weight were measured .  nefarious group ikypes 1 performed the exercise using a wooden inclined board , and  worrisome group ijkpesk 2 performed the exercises using an air - cushioned inclined board that provided an unstable surface .  worrisome patients were not cognitively impaired and were capable of independent walking for 20 m without difficulty . ikype patients were excluded if they had a history of or current neurological conditions.resultsthe patients in both groups showed significant increases in the percentage of active ankle dors

introductionrenal tubular involvement in sjgrengrengren 's syndrome ( ss ) usually manifests with fanconi syndrome , distal ( type 1 ) renal tubular acidosis ( rta ) , nephrogenic diabetes insipidus , and hypokalemia . we describe a case a young female with ss who presented with features of gitelman syndrome and hypokyalemic paralysis.case presentationa 29-year - old female presented with weakness of both upper and lower limb weakness since 15 days . she had a history of dry eyes and dry mouth for the past 6 months . laboratory evaluation showed hemoglobin - 9.5 g / dl , platelet 2.95 lakh / mm2 , blood urea nitrogen was 7 mg/dl , serum creatinine was 0.65 mg / ml , serum sodium was 134 meq / l , serum potassium was 1.81 meq/l , and serum chloride was 84.3                 cyte urinary chloride ( spot ) was 36.7                 cute , urinary creatinines 23.5 mg / l and urinary calcium 1.23 mg / day . the presence of hypomagnesemia and absence of hypercalciuria ruled out bart

introductioninadequate organization has been identified as an important cause of inappropriate courses of treatment for patients in the health care system . a response to this challenge has been to focus on integrated health care and co - ordination between the actors involved in the process . the aim of follow - up home visits to elderly and frail persons is to enable them to remain in their own home , avoid readmission to hospital , admission to nursing homes or other forms of sheltered housing and to improve the functional ability and general wellbeing of the elderly . this paper explores the motivation and rationale of local care providers to invest time and effort in follow-up home visits.materials and methodsthe aim of this study is to explore one particular aspect of follow- up home visit programmes : the motivation of gps , district nurses and hospital staff to take part in a cross - sectoral activity like home visits . in order to understand the organizational dynamics involved a short description of the setting is needed.resultsthe study was conducted as a randomized controlled trial which implies strict procedures for the implementation process . a total of approximately 300 patients from bispebjerg hospital ( a major hospital in copenhagen ) were included in the intervention and control groups . of these , approximately 100 follow up home

alzheimer s disease ( ad ) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by an extensive deposition of amyloid  ( a ) peptides in extracellular amyloids and by intracellular neurofibrillary tangles ( nfts ) of hyper - phosphorylated tau protein . sporadic late - onset ad is the most common form of dementia in the elderly and is strongly associated with age . in rare cases                  ( < 5% ) , ad is inherited and results in an early disease onset . the one genetic risk factor for late- onset ad that has been reported throughout many studies is apolipoprotein e ( apoe ) which physiologically functions as a ligand in receptor - mediated endocytosis of lipoprotein particles . several single nucleotide polymorphisms in apoe lead to alterations in the coding sequence and result in three common isoforms called apoe2 , apoe3 and apoe4 with the e4 allele being an ad risk factor and e2 being protective .                  strong evidence suggests that apoe influences ad via its effect on a metabolism ; however , the details of this process have to be fully elucidated .

objective : teriparatide ( 134 recombinant human parathyroid hormone ) is known to accelerate bone graft union and reduce the incidence of subsequent screw loosening after lumbar spinal fusion surgeries in elderly patients . the aim of this study was to evaluate the effect of postoperative use of this drug on the rate of solid posterolateral fusion mass and subsequent screw loosenings after multilevel instrumented spinal fusions.materials and methods : an open - labeled , nonplacebo controlled , retrospective , single center study is formulated by shortlisting 62 elderly patients ( male 9 ; female  =  53 ) between the age group of 65 to 78 years ( mean age    standard deviation [ sd ] =  70.1 years [ 95% ci ] = 70.7 ] , who underwent multilumbar laminectomy (  3 levels or 4 vertebral segments ) with pedicle screw constructs and postero - lateral intertransverse fusion with a combination of local bone graft and allograft.results:in total , 30 patients were enrolled in the experimental group and 32 patients were included in the control group . all patients were instructed to take calcium and vitamin d supplements every day following surgery . the union rate was

objectivethis study aims to establish the prevalence of hypertension , diabetes , obesity , and metabolic syndrome in the participants of a community - based screening programme in eastern nepal , asia.materials and methodsthe participants of the screening programme were recruited from the general population of the area . the national cholesterol education programme expert panel on detection , evaluation , and treatment of high blood cholesterol in adults ( adult treatment panel , or atp , iii ) , the national heart , lung and blood institute , and the american heart association have released a report on the criteria for diagnosing and managing metabolic syndrome ( ms ) . the panel describes ms as the presence of any three of the following : abdominal obesity , dislipidemia ( high levels of triglycerides , low hdl ) , increased blood pressure , and elevated fasting glucose . the international diabetes federation ( idf ) takes central obesity as a prerequisite for the diagnosis of ms with the association of any two of the other factors . the atp iii and the idf 's definitions of ms are used and compared.resultsthe prevalence of diabetes , hypertension , and obesity was significantly higher in the community than in the non - participating population ( p < 0.001 ) . there was a significant association between

background : balance is very important in daily activities of the elderly and greatly affects their rehensible quality of life . due to age - related changes , balance ability deteriorates . this study aimed to evaluate the effects of horseback riding exercise equipment on balance and gait abilities of elderly patients with osteoarthritis.materials and methods : in this study , 15 elderly subjects ( n=15 ) who were aged 65  years or over were selected for the study . the subjects , while providing conventional physical therapy , 8 weeks three times a week the horse riding equipment exercise , was carried out for 30 minutes . the balance test ( functional reach test ) and sbbsfergergert balance scale ( version 18 ) was used for data analysis . in order to compare the differences between balance test scores before and after horse riding exercise , paired t - test was used.results:sfbbs scores and range of frt increased significantly after horse iflower simulator exercise ( p<0.001 ) . sfbbs score and frt scores were also significantly higher after the exercise than before the exercise.conclusion:horseback riding exercises significantly improved the balance ability of elderly subjects . horseback iflower riding exercise can be used as a therapeutic intervention for elderly

medulloblastomas are characterized by activation of the hedgehog ( hh ) signaling pathway and are often driven by mutations in hh pathway components . tp53 mutations have recently been identified as a key event in the pathogenesis of this childhood cancer . although the specific roles of p53 are still largely unknown , the presence of recurrent p53 mutations suggests that p53 signaling plays an important role in driving medullobloma tumorigenesis . however , patients with li - fraumeni syndrome , caused by germ - line mutations in t p53 , offer an opportunity to study how medulloblasts arise at the genetic level .

ventricular tachycardia ( vt ) during pregnancy is a rare cause of new onset arrhythmia and can manifest as a new onset of cardiac arrhythmias . we report two cases of pregnancy - related vt of right ventricular outflow tract origin , which were successfully treated with intravenous lidocaine and magnesium sulfate .

sphingomonas wittichii rw1 is a microorganism of great interest to the bioremediation community for its ability to biotransform a large number of toxic polychlorinated dioxins and to utilize dibenzofuran as a growth substrate and sole source of carbon and energy . to optimize the bacterial degradation of pollutants , it is important to understand how these organisms function during growth on recalcitrant substrates and which factors influence their degradative abilities . the aim of this study was to investigate system - wide changes in protein expression , as compared to nonselective growth media , in response to dibenzo - p - dioxin exposure . the present study builds on previous work and utilized difference gel eletrophoresis ( dige ) coupled with mass spectrometry ( ms ) to exploit recently gathered genome data . when used together , these tools yield information on the response of cells of s. witticii to dienzofuran exposure and the bacterium 's ability to degradatively degrade this compound . dige and ms analyses of protein expression profiles revealed that growth on the compound significantly reduced the expression of a number of proteins , including the dioxygenase . these results
