psoriasis is an autoimmune , chronic , noncontagious , recurrent , inflammatory skin disorder . in western populations , its prevalence is estimated to be as high as 2.8% . the typical skin lesions of psoriatic patients are sharply demarcated erythematous plaques covered with silvery or opalescent scales . the inflammatory response is supported by the infiltration of polymorphic neutrophils in the skin , which trigger the chemokines and lymphokines released by keratinocytes and t - lymphocytes , respectively , and , in turn , trigger the activity of lymphocytes and keratinocyte , thus generating a vicious cycle limited to acute lesions but linked in any case to the extended inflammation . boswellia serrata gum resin extracts ( bses ) are used in traditional ayurvedic medicine to treat inflammatory diseases . the prevalent component of lipophilic fraction of olibanum is represented by the pentacyclic triterpenes boswellic acids ( bas ) , several forms of which have been characterized by analytical techniques , including - and -configured bas . the main anti - inflammatory properties of bas are attributed to suppression of leukotriene formation via inhibition of 5-lipoxygen

objectiveto examine the expression and subcellular localization of xlf and ku70 in bovine kidney cells and human cervical carcinoma cells.research design and methodsa cattle xlf gene                  ( nm_001075393.1 ) with an artificial ecori site at the 5 end and a bamhi site at 3 end was synthesized and ligated to the in - frame fusion gene of the peyfp - c1 vector . cattle cells were cultured in dulbecco s modified eagle medium ( dmem ) with 10% fetal calf serum ( fcs ) and were transfected with the gene . the cells were then cultured for 2 days and then monitored under an fv300 confocal confocal microscope ( olympus , tokyo , japan ) as previously described . immunoblotting , immunofluorescence staining and western blot analysis were conducted based on the previous methods . the binding to each protein was visualized using a select western blotting detection system                 .resultsthe expression and localization of the xlf protein in cattle cells was similar to that in human cells . the expression of the protein in the cell line mdbk was significantly higher than that in the human

folic acid modification of magnetic nanoparticles could be used to facilitate uptake to specific cancer cells for cancer therapy and diagnosis . our results showed that the uptake of folic - acid modified nanoparticles by 5rp7 cancer cells was also much higher than that of 3t3 cells . this modification can be used for successful targeting of cancer cells expressing the folate receptor .

introductionthis is a retrospective cross - sectional survey involving students at the higher institute of sport and physical education in the city of sfax.methodswe collected data on age , weight , height , and smoking habits are collected routinely when students join the institute , and then the sport in which the student is injured in the low back is registered in the institute health service files . consuming body mass index ( bmi ; kg / m2 ) , smoking habits , and history of any pain or injuries were taken for all the students in the beginning of the first grade . on the basis of the collected data , participants were dichotomized into those with or without low back pain ( lbp ) , while indicating the sports discipline in which they were injured or that they had been injured after cumulative fatigue due to the large number of hours spent in practicing sports and physical activities . the chi - square test was used to test associations between lbp and other categorical variables , and to compare the prevalence of lbp between males and female.resultsthe prevalence of low - back pain was significantly higher in males than in females ( p < 0.001 ) . the prevalence was higher in men than in women ( p = 0.002 ) . lbp was

objective : this study explored the meaning of health as perceived by iranian kurdish diabetic patients in order to develop an appropriate multidisciplinary outlook toward this ethnicity of patients.materials and methods : the research was conducted over a ten - month period during 2014 , in the diabetes care centre of tohid hospital at the kurdistan university of medical sciences in sanandaj , in western - iran . in - depth , face - to - face , semi - structured interviews were conducted with 20 diabetic patients , who were selected through a purposive sampling method . content analysis was conducted using a conventional content analysis approach . the data were analyzed using the statistical package for social sciences ( spss ) version 20.0.results:the mean age of the participants was 54.5 years , and they were predominantly male . the mean duration of the study was 10.6 years . the participants were of the age group of 25 to 60 years old , and the mean length of time since the diagnosis of diabetes was 4.6 ( range : 3 to 6 ) years . of the 20 participants , the majority of them were of kurds ethnicity . the most common questions that were asked during the interviews were about their perception of health and its meaning

introduction : proper amount of daily water intake in a healthy individual is controversial . there is a prevalent thought that we all have to drink eight glasses of water daily                 , we try to clarify this topic from the perspective of avicenna and traditional persian medicine ( tpm).materials and methods : al - qanun fil - tibb ( canon of medicine ) was reviewed . furthermore , medline , embase , scopus , iranmedex , and science iranian database ( sid ) focusing on the keywords  traditional medicine  ,  water intake ,  essential fluid , and  complementary and alternative medicine were reviewed to find relevant information.results:according to the findings of this study , the demand for daily water is not the same in different individuals . moreover , important recommendations of tpm sages on an appropriate amount of water intake should be considered to prevent associated disorders .

a 34year - old male presented for general examination on day 49 of a 50-day self - imposed fast , seeking approval to continue his fasting behavior . during this period , the patient reported only consuming water , tea , coffee ( with no milk or sugar ) , and a daily multivitamin tablet ( centrum performance ; pfizer ) . as expected , body mass loss during fasting was severe and noticeable ( 20.7% of body mass lost ) . however , physical function was maintained at day 49 , as assessed by general observation and six - minute walk test . one month following initial presentation , this patient returned for follow - up examination during which body composition , resting metabolic rate , urine , and venous blood were recorded . the results of this case study suggest that moderate physical function appears maintained at 49 days of fasting .

purposeto evaluate the anatomical and visual outcomes of pars plana vitrectomy ( ppv ) and internal limiting membrane ( ilm ) peeling with brilliant blue ( bb ) in the treatment of idiopathic macular holes.materials and methodsfifty eyes of 48 patients who had 23 g ppv and ilm peeling as a treatment for macular hole between july 2007 and december 2009 were retrospectively evaluated in the authors clinic . all patients underwent measurement of best - corrected visual acuity and intraocular pressure , anterior segment and fundus examinations before surgery , and macular examination before surgery and 1 , 3 , and 6 months after surgery using spectral optical coherence tomography ( oct).resultsthe mean age of the patients was 54.5 years . the mean follow - up time points were : 1-day , 1-week , 3-month , 3, and 6-month post - surgical . the visual outcome was excellent at 3-week and 1-month intervals , and the functional outcomes were excellent at 1- and 3-months . in four eyes , both ilm and epiretinal membrane peeling were performed . the functional outcome of ppv with bb was better than that of

objectivethe aim of this study was to investigate the association between type 2 diabetes and coronary artery disease ( cad ) in patients who had survived myocardial infarction ( mi ) in italy.research design and methodsa total of 330 patients with type 2 diabetics who survived mi were recruited from the case - control gargano heart study ( ghs ) in gargano , italy , from 2001 to 2008 and monitored until the end of 2009 . data on cardiovascular events were obtained by telephone interview , from their primary care physicians , or from death certificates.resultsthe incidence of major cardiovascular events ranged from nonfatal stroke to cardiovascular death . diabetes was present in 43 ( 16.2% ) of the patients . the prevalence of diabetes was similar to that reported by a nationwide epidemiologic study of end - stage renal disease ( esrd ) . the incidence of hypertension was significantly higher in patients with diabetes than in those without diabetes ( p < 0.001).conclusiontype 2 diabetes is associated with a higher risk of cardiovascular events .

in the past two decades , advances in genetic engineering and synthetic biology have made it possible to engineer biological systems that perform human - defined biological reactions . in this report , the authors describe the development of a new synthetic rna regulatory system for the control of mammalian t - cell proliferation , which is based on a platform of assembled rna devices formed by a modular sensor ( aptamer ) and a gene - regulatory component ( a hammerhead ribozyme ) . the system was tested in vitro and in vivo in mice and humans and showed that it modulated the response rate of t - lymphocytes in vivo and effectively modulated their biological response ( proliferation / viability ) in vivo . in addition , the system could strictly strictly control the expression of cd19 ( a marker for fluorescence - activated cell sorting and immunomagnetic selection ) and control ( by monitoring the expression levels of the input molecule ) the biological cell response ( by modulating the input and output of the target gene expression levels ) in response to cd19-positive t cells . the authors also demonstrated that the system can be applied to the use of adoptive cell transfer ( act ) for the treatment of cancer , which uses t cells that have a natural or genetically engineered reactivity to a patient 's

the wilms ' tumor suppressor gene ( wt1 ) encodes a zinc - finger - transcription factor presumed to regulate the expression of numerous target genes through dna binding . it has been shown to play an important role in the maintenance of normal podocyte function in embryogenesis , gonadogenesis and nephrogenesis . denys - drash syndrome ( dds ) is characterized by the histological finding of diffuse mesangial sclerosis ( dms ) , a finding also described in some cases of congenital nephrotic syndrome ( isolated diffuse mesangeal sclerosis , idms ) . in this report , we present two cases of idms with different mutations in the wt 1 gene that had previously been reported in association with dds . the first case is a 13-month - old girl who was born at 37 weeks ' gestational age with a birth weight of 2,500 g and normal female external genitalia . she developed generalized tonic tonic clonic seizure at a local clinic , hypocalcemia , hyperkaleemia , hyperpulmonary hypertrophy and hypoventilation . the second case is an 11-year - old boy who developed dds at the age of 30 days and underwent renal

titanium dioxide ( tio2 ) is a pigment used in many sunscreens , soaps , shampoos , toothpastes , cosmetics , paper products , plastics , ink , paint , and building materials in both its bulk form and its nanoform . it is also used in human food as a colorant and inactive ingredient , where it can also be present in both forms 1 , 2 . the majority of studies investigating nanotio2 toxicity did not take photoactivation into account and performed tests either in dark conditions or under indoor commercial artificial lighting that did not simulate natural solar irradiation . the aim of present study was to derive a phototoxicity ratio ( pr ) between the results of the nanotionotoxicity experiments conducted in the absence of sunlight and conducted under the same experimental setup and otherwise identical conditions . to achieve this aim , a literature review was conducted to collect available toxicity endpoints . only papers that reported results for 2 different exposure groups ( with and without simulated solar radiation ( ssr ) in the form of half maximal effective concentration ( ec50 ) , half maximal inhibitory concentration ( ic50 ) and median lethal concentration ( lc50 ) or lowestobservedeffect concentration ( loec ) were selected . the photot

we report 2 patients infected with panton - valentine leukocidin ( pvl)positive mrsa t034 . each patient had a medical history typical of that reported for community - acquired mrsas of other lineages , which in most cases are pvl positive . the patients lived in geographically distinct areas in the western part of sweden and had no connection to each other . no animal contact ( e.g. , pets , farming ) was reported by the 2 patients , their family members , or other close contacts . both patient strains carried pvl , confirmed by identification of the luks - lukf genes , and were resistant to digestion with restriction endonuclease smai when typing by pfge was attempted . they produced -hemolysin according to phenotypic detection methods that used rabbit blood agar with hot  cold analysis , which further indicated their animal origin .

the selective melatonin receptor agonism effect of ramelteon is useful for insomnia . here we wanted to present a refractory chronic migraine case , who had significant improvements in migraine after using the selective selective m1 receptor agonist , 8 mg / day . the precipitating factors were the stress , increase in physical activities and sudden cold weather . the migraine also exacerbated her insomnia problems , with fragmented sleep and inadequate sleep duration as 12 hours ( insomnia severity index [ isi ] score , 23 ) . she did nt have any comorbid mental disorder except insomnia . due to the fear of abuse potential related to hypnotics , the patient was treated with the use of the melatonin agonist 8 mg/day for 2 weeks . after 2 weeks , her sleep duration prolonged to 45 hours with less fragmentation . in addition , her migraine severity started to decline ( migraine disability assessment test [ midas ] scores , 11 ) . after 6 months of treatment , the migraine severity continued to relieve ( midas scores , 6 ) with stable sleeping quality ( isi score , 14 ) . in this case , we could speculate that the patient had significant effects in migraine in such mild prolongation of sleep duration . since the patient did not have insomnia before the first

objective . to determine the level of insulin sensitivity ( is ) and three different types of antioxidant enzyme activities gsh - px , gr , and sod , in t2d patients with atherothrombotic infarction ( ati ) and lacunar infarctions ( li ) . methods . in this study we included a total of 93 patients with type 2 diabetes , ascribed to the following groups : t2D patients with li ( group a , n = 30 ) , and t2 d with ati ( group b , n < 30 ) and without ischemic stroke ( group c ) . additionally , 93 nondiabetics were also included in the study . results . the mean is levels were significantly lower in the groups with li than in the other groups ( p < 0.001 ) . in addition , the levels of antioxidant enzymes were significantly higher in the group with li compared to the group without stroke ( p = 0.002 ) . conclusion . the results of this study suggest that t2ds with li and ati have lower levels of is levels , and that these patients also have higher levels of glutathione reductase and superoxide dismutase activities .

bacterial spores are characterized by a series of unique chemical features which can facilitate their identification in natural environments . besides the high content of minerals ( particularly calcium ) , spores contain high amounts of dipicolinic acid , dpa . the aim of this study was to adopt the fluorescence - based method to determine the spore content in soils sampled from various locations . soil samples were collected using a stainless steel soil core ( 15  mm in diameter ) , which was sterilized before each sampling . different bacillus species ( b. megaterium , b. subtilis ) were cultivated in liquid medium containing ( in g / l ) glucose , ammonium chloride , magnesium sulfate , calcium chloride , edta , potassium dihydrogen phosphate , dipotassium hydrogen phosphate , and yeast extract . to stimulate and stimulate sporulation , the bacteria were subsequently transferred to a 30c medium ( 150c ) . after additional 30 days of incubation , the spores were harvested by centrifugation , immediately frozen in liquid nitrogen followed by lyophilization , and stored on ice . after the return to the laboratory , the samples were analyzed for the presence of spores . the results showed that soil samples from different locations were divided into three groups : grasslands ( pasture

alzheimer 's disease ( ad ) is a common neurodegenerative disease in the central nervous system . the pathological features of ad include senile plaques formed by extracellular amyloid  ( a ) aggregation , neurofibrillary tangles formed by abnormal accumulation of hyperphorylated tau protein , synaptic impairments , and neuronal loss in the cerebral cortex and hippocampus . this study is central to the pathological changes of ad and leads to a series of pathological events , which further promote a aggregation , resulting in cascade amplification .                  the endoplic reticulum ( er ) , a dynamic membranous organelle , is involved in protein synthesis , posttranslational modification , folding , calcium storage , lipid metabolism , and steroid hormone synthesis . in turn ,                  these sensors serve to promote the activation of the er - specific apoptosis pathway by promoting the expression of the transcriptional activator ccaat / enhancer - binding protein homologous protein ( chop ) and caspase-12 activation , which in turn stimulate the c - jun amino - terminal kinase ( jnk ) pathway to promote cell apoptosis , thus preventing the damaging impact of misfolded and secreted proteins on tissues and organisms

despite the advances in the treatment of coronary artery disease ( cad ) , morbidity and mortality are still high , especially in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus ( dm ) . in this review , we summarize the current understanding of the cellular mechanisms underlying the cardioprotective effects of ischemic preconditioning ( ip ) in diabetic cad patients . ip is the intrinsic myocardial mechanism that provides the greatest protection regarding the reduction in myocardia infarct size . although ip was initially thought to result from the opening of collateral vessels and higher coronary flow , some interesting studies have shown that the phenomenon occurs irrespective of coronary flow changes . ip can be observed by sequential exercise tests ( sets ) , in which the improvement in ischemia parameters in the second of 2 sets were confirmed by invasive measurements of myocardium oxygen consumption . moreover , the clinical observation that patients with chronic ischeemic heart disease frequently describe attenuation or even cessation of angina symptoms if they rest and restart the exercise is termed  warm - up or walk - through angina . this phenomenon has been related to ip and documented in studies using sets . following the demonstration of ip , the cellular mechanism underlying the phenomenon began to be investigated . currently it is assumed that ip

a 55-year - old man presented signs of progressive cardiac decompensation during the last 4 months due to tumorous obstruction of the mitral valve . in 2014 the patient underwent resection of a suspected myxoma ( 824124 mm ) in the left atrium . however , in the histological workup , the tumor mass , resected from the posterior mitral annulus , was diagnosed as chondrosarcoma . during the course of 12 months , the patient was in a clinically stable state . in january 2016 the patient first presented with a recurring tumor mass on the anterior mitral leaflet ( pml ) max . in april 2016 , he showed increasing peripheral edema and orthopnea , with no signs of angina pectoris , and nyha iii - iv . the patient presented absolute arrhythmia with atrial fibrillation , pulse 109/min , and blood pressure 82/52 mmhg . echocardiography showed pleural effusions on both sides and distended vena cava inferior and hepatic veins . upon admission to our center , he presented a 403532 mm mass , originating from the pml . a cardio mri could not be performed , as the

cannabis is a prominent political , health , and law enforcement issue in north america that is currently receiving a great deal of attention . however , a critical piece of information often missing from these debates or not addressed adequately is the scientific evidence about the effects of cannabis use on a person 's overall health . this concern is even more pressing when it comes to the cognitive , physical , and mental health harms to adolescents who use cannabis . it is critical we ask these questions because adolescence is a time of rapid development that helps lay the foundation for success later in life . youth are disproportionately more likely than adults to experience greater harms from drug use .                  their brains are undergoing rapid and extensive development that can be negatively affected by cannabis use . furthermore , cannabis use can also acutely impair cognitive and motor functions like perception , coordination , and balance for hours after use .

hypertension ( htn ) is the most common comorbidity in the world with significant public health implications . the overall u.s . prevalence of hypertension among adults ages 18 years in 20052008 was 30.9% and was highest among persons ages 65 years ( 69.7% )   and non - hispanic blacks ( 44% ) . the american heart association estimates that the incurred costs of hypertension are more than $ 93.5  billion per year , and that cardiovascular disease and stroke for which htn is the predominant risk factor , account for 17% of the total annual health expenditures in the united states . hypertension and its sequelae , heart failure ( hf ) , are a progressive disease . evidence shows that less than half of patients with heart failure survive five years ( after diagnosis ) , and less than a quarter of them live ten years after their initial diagnosis . the framingham heart study showed that hypertensive patients were more likely to develop heart failure than those who were normotensive . the lifetime risk for development of hf among people with blood pressure ( bp ) > 160/90  mm  hg is double that of those with bp < 140/90 / mm hg . the relative incidence of h

backgroundradiological investigations and therapeutics have become an integral part of the management of critically ill patients in the intensive care unit ( icu ) . however , the available literature on the extent of radiation exposure to icu personnel is scarce and relates mainly to the level of scattered radiation within the icu . in our icu , resident doctors working on 12 h shifts are also responsible for the transport and management of icu patients when they undergo diagnostic and therapeutic procedures in the radiology department . therefore , we carried out this study to determine the total radiation exposure in icu resident doctors involved in the course of their duties.material/methodswe conducted a prospective , observational study in an icu and postanesthesia care unit of a tertiary care cancer referral center in mumbai , india from september 2012 to february 2013 . all resident doctors who gave voluntary written consent to participate in the study were included . we used thermoluminescent dosimeters ( tlds ) to measure radiation exposure by measuring the amount of visible light emitted from a crystal when the crystal is heated . data were analyzed using statistical package for social sciences ( spss ) version 20.resultsthe total radiation dose to icU resident doctors was calculated using the following

despite the huge burden of hepatitis b worldwide , very few patients in low- and middle - income countries are currently receiving antiviral treatment . this is largely to blame on lack of access to viral load quantification , because this is considered a mandatory component of the diagnostic work - up in all international liver society guidelines . even though rapid diagnostic tests ( rdts ) for hepatitis b and c are commercially available , the diagnostic accuracy varies from excellent to very poor . in resource - limited settings ,  furthermore , stringent requirements for storage and shipment of plasma to the referral laboratory are barriers in settings with limited infrastructure . the solution to this challenge would be to develop reliable , cheap , and easy - to - use assays that can be performed at the site of patient care (  point - of - care assays ) . currently , there is one product , truenat ( molbio diagnostics , goa , india ) , for hbv diagnostics for the real world , which has undergone field testing in sub - saharan africa and sunnyvale , caa , and has shown excellent performance .

ileocecal duplication cysts are extremely rare with only 4 cases reported in medical literature to date . we report a fatal case of a 41-week - gestational - age infant girl with a history of anorexia , vomiting and recurrent diarrhea . the patient was admitted to our hospital at 35 days of age with a one - day history of abdominal pain and vomiting . physical examination revealed a distended and tender abdomen . laboratory values were significant for a c- reactive protein ( crp ) of 1.7 mg / dl ( normal range was less than 0.3 mg/ dl ) . the diagnosis was suspected intestinal obstruction due to enteritis , antibiotics were started ( abpc / mcipc and amk ) . 4 hours after admission , she was pale with a temperature of 39.2 c and 220 per minutes pulse . blood pressure was low , and the fevers , vomiting , and diarrhea persisted and worsened despite antibiotics . she failed to respond to medical managements and died 27 hours later due to multiple organ failures . the cyst was unilocular , contained watery fluid , did not communicate with the adjacent intestinal lumen , and had an undefined mucosal lining though it resembled pyrolic glands or duodenal glands

presbyopia refers to the age - related loss of natural accommodation and resulting reduction of baseline near vision around the age of 40 years . this review will focus on corneal - based surgical strategies to treat presbyopia and in particular how these methods have been or may be used in pseudophakic patients . the two major techniques to alter the cornea generally utilize either an intracorneal inlay device or laser refractive surgery . several different types of inlays have been developed to improve uncorrected near vision in presbyopic eyes . the best studied inlay to date is the kamra inlay by acufocus , which is an opaque polymer ring that employs a pinhole concept to expand the depth of focus . the raindrop inlay is a clear hydrogel implant with a bifocal design similar in concept to the flexivue microlens implant . the icolens by neoptics are another type of inlay that creates a multifocal effect via a central plano zone surrounded by circular rings of plus power . laser - assisted in situ keratomileusis ( lasik ) is the most commonly used technique to alter cornea . lasik can produce monovision or multifocality

enteric duplications ( eds ) are uncommon anomalies that can occur at any point of the gastrointestinal tract . the small intestine is the most common location ; retroperitoneum is an extremely rare site . in general , they are diagnosed in the neonatal period or during infancy ; they are increasingly diagnosed prenatally ; early prenatal detection is possible . there have been seven reported cases of retro peritoneal ed cyst in the english literature . we report a case of a female newborn , vaginally born at 39 weeks of gestation from a 32-year - old mother , gravid 3 , para 3 . prenatal ultrasound at 22 week of gestation objectified an abdominal cystic mass located in the left upper abdominal quadrant associated with fetal pyelectasis .                 cyst was diagnosed by ultrasound and perioperative diagnosis by magnetic resonance imaging ( mri ) . the postoperative period was uneventful ; the patient was discharged on the 5th postoperative day .

conjoint bicondylar hoffa fracture involving both the femoral condyles is a rare injury and has been reported in the literature only once . we describe a rare case of a 17-year - old male labourer who presented to orthopaedic emergency with the complaint of acute pain and swelling in his right knee following trauma . he had a fall from a 10-m - high ladder with direct impact on his semiflexed right knee . local examination revealed a painful swollen right knee with a 1 cm  1 cm lacerated wound over the anterior aspect of the knee at the point of direct impact plain anteroposterior and lateral radiographs were inadequate to define the exact fracture pattern . noncontrast computed tomographic ( ct ) scan was performed which established the diagnosis of conjoined fracture . the patient was operated in supine position under tourniquet and regional anesthesia . lateral parapatellar arthrotomy was done and the vastus lateralis was retracted laterally after lifting it up from the lateral femoral side . the rest of the extensor mechanism along with the patella was retracted medially for unobstructed visualization . on exposing the knee , a tangential fracture was noted . both the cond

a patient of age 27 years reported to the department of prosthodontics for the replacement of missing left first molar . a three - dimensional model of the mandible was fabricated to assess the available bone for implant placement at the premolar root . the surgical guide was fabricated on top of the model . the extraoral impression was recorded with addition silicone putty and light body . a key system was incorporated to adjust the length and angulation according to each patient 's mandible . the accuracy of this method was verified with computed tomography ( ct ) scan . this article had won the best table clinic award in 35th table clinic conference conducted by army dental corps in delhi 2007 .

a 48-year - old woman with a long history of intractable migraine headaches was admitted to our institution 's headache unit for aggressive management . she had received one to two courses of dihydroergotamine mesylate ( dhe ) 45 . several hours after the final dose of her second round she became obtunded , minimally responsive and hypotensive with a blood pressure of 80/45 mm hg . on physical examination her abdomen was moderately distended with few bowel sounds . her chest radiograph was unremarkable , and her abdominal x - rays showed only mild prominence of the colon . over the next several hours she became progressively acidotic with a worsening bandemia . a bedside colonoscopy showed diffuse ischemic colitis with fecal impaction and no pseudomembranes . the patient was transferred to the intensive care unit , given intravenous fluid boluses , started on vasopressors , and given broad spectrum antibiotics . on admission she had no history of arterial occlusion , venous thrombosis , and non - occlusive ischemia caused by systemic hypotension or , as is strongly suspected in this case , ergotamine - induced vasospasm . she underwent emergency

mrsa is a major cause of nosocomial infections in hospitals in taiwan . mrsa isolates can be difficult to identify and difficult to treat , especially when they are associated with a panton - valentine leukocidin ( pvl ) gene and drug susceptibility to erythromycin , clindamycin , gentamicin , ciprofloxacin , tetracycline , trimethoprim - sulfamethoxazole , rifampin , and vancomycin . in this study , we demonstrate that the type iii sccmec element in the predominant mrsas from 1994 to 2003 had the same type as that in wis.isisplinghoff and co . , who reported that the predominant type iii element in type i clones at their institute changed from type iii in 1984 to type i in 1989 to type ii in 1998 . we show that type ii scc mec elements are more resistant than type i elements to certain antimicrobial drugs , especially fluoroquinolones .

abstractcarbon dioxide ( co2 ) has been used as an intravascular contrast agent for patients with iodine allergy or renal function impairment . we report the case of a 79-year - old woman with borderline renal function who underwent aortic embolectomy for acute ischemic limb ischemia . co2 was used as a substitute for iodine contrast in a patient with known chronic kidney disease and an acute limb embolic occlusion .

emphysematous gastritis is a rare disease characterized by the presence of air within the wall of the stomach and diffuse gastric wall inflammation by gas - forming bacteria , which may be also life - threatening due to systemic toxicity . gastrointestinal mucormycosis is very rare , and the manifestations range from colonization of peptic ulcers to infiltrative disease with vascular invasion and dissemination . here we report a 43-yr old man was admitted to the emergency room because of diffuse abdominal pain , indigestion , and poor oral intake for 4 days . he had had a 1-yr history of hypertension , and there was also a history of excessive ingestion of alcohol and heavy smoking for 20 yr . computed tomography ( ct ) and histopathologic findings showed angioinvasion , necrosis , ultimately disseminated to the colon and liver , and was fatal despite medical and surgical treatment .

amoebiasis is the infection by the protozoan parasite entamoeba histolytica with or without clinical manifestations . the only known natural host of this parasite is the human body with the large intestine as the major target organ . this parasite has a very simple life cycle in which the infective form is the cyst that is considered a resistant form of the parasite . clinical and etiological diagnosis of intestinal and extra - intestinal amoebia is neither easy or simple in part because of the discovery of two species made from the previously known species , both indistinguishable under microscopy . knowledge of both species with different pathogenic phenotypes comes from a large scientific debate during the second half of the twentieth century , which gave rise to the rapid development of diagnosis technology based on molecular and immunological strategies . the major purpose of the present work is to highlight the novelties in regard to human infection and the disease that can help the general physician from both endemic and non - endemic countries in their medical practice .

objective . to examine the relationship between physical activity variety and minutes spent in objectively measured moderate - to - vigorous physical activity ( mvpa ) in two groups of individuals who have successfully maintained their body weight long - term : ( 1 ) weight loss maintainers with previous history of overweight / obesity and ( 2 ) normal - weight individuals without a history of being overweight . methods . a convenience sample of men and women was recruited through advertisements placed in national and local publications intended for a general audience . participants were enrolled in the cross - sectional  lite study that compared weight control behaviors of weight - loss - maintainers and normal weight controls . participants engaged in a variety of different activities ( i.e. , walking + other leisure - time activities ) in one week , which was defined as the number of different types of self - reported moderate to vigorous activities performed in a single week , and minutes of objectively measured physical activity per day . results . participants in both groups reported engaging in a greater variety of moderate - intensity physical activity than those in the control group ( p < 0.001 ) . however , participants in the weight loss group reported higher levels of physical activity in minutes per day ( p = 0.004 ) . conclusion . the association between

the infrared spectra of c2f4 in natural isotopic abundance and isotopically enriched ( c to c ) 50% were studied for the anharmonic constants x18 , x17 , x18c , and x17f , which are used to calculate the fundamental anharmonicity of the carbon - to - carbon ( ccf4 ) and carbon - fermion ( cf4f ) reactions of this molecule .                  the                  anharmony constants of ccf2 and c4f ( egf4 ) were determined from spectroscopic analysis of the ir active band of the spectra , and                  a complete set ( 78 ) of                  constants is obtained . the x17c and x18f constants are related to some peculiarities                  of the reactions of the molecule , such as the preference of sp- to sp hybridization , the low                  magnitude of the pyramidalization ( wagging ) vibrations , and the high                  strength of the coupling cc stretch with trans pyramidialization . the                                                                 the                                   values of x18 are also related to the valence - bond analysis of weak                  double bonds

objective . to examine the relationships between intracranial radiographic abnormalities and preimplantation developmental assessment ( including head circumference ) with postimplant audiometric and language outcomes in children with symptomatic congenital cytomegalovirus ( cmv ) who have undergone cochlear implantation . methods . this study was a retrospective review of children with a diagnosis of symptomatic cmv who underwent cochlearing implantation between 2004 and 2010 . preoperative developmental assessments included the diagnoses of developmental delay and/or other disabilities , nonverbal intelligence quotients or developmental quotients ( iq / dq ) , and head circumference ( hc ) at the preoperative visit . results . twenty - eight children were included in the study . the mean age of the patients was 4.8 years ( range , 2 to 6 years ) . the median head circumference was 6.8 cm3 ( range 1 to 8 cm3 ) and the mean iq or dq was 3.5 ( range 2 to 4 ) . postimplacement audiometric behavioral testing included frequency - specific thresholds and speech awareness or speech reception thresholds ( sat / srt ) and pure tone averages ( pta ) were calculated by an average of 4 frequencies ( 0.

the millennium development goals ( mdgs ) were evaluated in the year 2015 , and there will be remaining new challenges to sustain the achievement at post-2015 era . in this review , leprosy , filariasis , schistosomiasis , soil - transmitted helminthiasis , and yaws are included in the list of neglected tropical diseases ( ntd ) in indonesia , which is one of the diseases that are not being considered as important diseases in the mdgs . the major ntd are tuberculosis , hiv / aids , malaria , and major diseases , which attract the attention of the world 's sponsors , researchers , and policy makers . however , the big three are not the same as ntd , which has not gained any priority from them and even worsened . poverty as one of neglected in ntd determinants frequently occurs in rural area , slums , and marginalized populations living nearby the equator . ntd in this area are closely associated with poverty and limited resources , such as clean drinking water access , poor sanitation , and healthy housing . the technology and resource to control , prevent , and eliminate these diseases are available , but not for the developing countries . who has included 17 diseases caused by bacteria , virus , and protoz

[ purpose ] this study examined the effect of the use of a trekking pole on muscle and cartilage damage and fatigue during downhill walking . [ subjects and methods ] eight obese women ( average age of 21.1  1.8 years , average height of 164.8  4.9  cm , average weight of 61.7  7.7 kg , and average body fat percentage of 33.4% ) with body fat percentages ranging from 30.0 to                 36.1 volunteered as subjects for this study . [ results ] the subjects were subjected to 30 minutes of walking at an intensity of 50% perrere . [ conclusion ] the use                  of a pole during downhill terrain walking can reduce the load and stress on the lower limbs ( such as the ankle , knee , and hip joints ) , and may thereby reduce the burden on lower extremity joint injuries .

periampullary diverticula ( pad ) are a rare cause of obstructive jaundice secondary to extrinsic compression of mid common bile duct ( cbd ) by distended pad filled with pus - like material as a result of impacted intradiverticular enterolith at the pad orifice . we report a case of lemmel 's syndrome in an 81-yr - old woman who presented to the emergency department with diffuse abdominal pain and obstructive liver enzyme elevation in a cholestatic pattern . endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography ( ercp ) showed distal cbd stone with upstream cbd dilatation . however , on coronal reconstructed images , the stone was not located within the cbd but inside the pad and the distended diverticulum was compressing the mid cbd . on endoscopic nasobiliary drainage ( enbd ) , stenosis at mid cmd was also shown to be resolved , likely because the pad had been decompressed . after confirming that no other pathology was present , enterolith was crushed and removed by dormia basket . the patient was discharged without complication .

objectiveto explore associations between body mass index ( bmi ) and dietary habits and between underweight or overweight / obesity and health status in a population - based multiethnic sample of centenarians ( 98 years and above ) from northern georgia , usa.research design and methodsa secondary analysis of data from the georgian centenarian study was conducted to examine associations between bmi , dietary habits , and chronic health conditions in a sample of 98 year - olds ( 98 and over ) from the usa . bmi was measured using a simple index of weight for height that is frequently used in the assessment of nutritional status.resultsthe prevalence of obesity was significantly higher in the underweight / obese than in the overweight / obese group ( p < 0.001 ) , while the prevalence of diabetes was significantly lower in the obese / overweight / underweight group ( or = 0.93 , 95% ci : 0.76 to 0.96 ; p = .002 ) . there were no significant differences in bmi or dietary habits between the two groups , with the latter being associated with a significantly higher prevalence of chronic health condition ( p > 0.05).conclusionthe findings of this study suggest that there is

introductionenvironmental factors play a major role in the etiology of cancers , cardiovascular disease , and other chronic diseases . exposures to these factors vary widely between populations , and between individuals within the same population . therefore , their measurement is essential to : ( i ) study associations in epidemiological studies with disease outcomes and assess their contribution to disease risk , ( ii ) monitor exposures to diseases risk factors in population studies and ( iii ) assess subject compliance in clinical trials or large intervention studies . biomarkers of exposure can be compounds present in the environment and absorbed in the gut after ingestion , inhaled in lungs , or absorbed through the skin . they can also be metabolic end - products derived from environmental compounds that were metabolized by the liver and other tissues , and the microbiota . they may also be macromolecular indicators of environmental effects ( e.g. enzymes , proteins or rna transcripts related to the status of a nutrient or toxic agent ) . to date , relatively little effort has been directed to collecting and organizing data on biomarker of exposure . the exposome - explorer database contains information on exposure to environmental chemicals such as pahs , pcbs , nonylphenols , or pesticides , but very limited data in biospecimens . expocast

a. fumigatus is a multicellular eukaryotic fungus responsible for life - threatening invasive aspergillosis ( ia ) in immunosuppressed patients . the cell wall of this fungus is composed of a unique -1,3/1,4-glucan skeleton with chitin and galactomannan covalently linked to the nonreducing ends of -1 ,3,4,5-glocan , which is mainly coated with glycosylphosphatidylinositol - anchoring ( gpi ) proteins , which contain n- and o - glycans . the functional importance of protein -1- and -4-glycosylation in cell wall organization has , until recently , remained poorly understood . however , during the past few years , it has become increasingly evident that glycoslyation is vital for cell wall synthesis and thus vital for growth and morphology of this species . in this paper , the mechanisms of cell wall biosynthesis in this species will be discussed , with respect to the enzymatic pathways involved and their functional importance . furthermore , the utility of the cell - wall - targeted drugs echinocandins , caspofungin ,

cardiac angiotensin receptor blockers ( arbs ) are considered second choice therapy for the treatment of heart failure ( hf ) with reduced ejection fraction ( ef ) in patients who are not tolerated to ace inhibitors or mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists in the current guidelines . however , it remains unclear which arb is better tolerated than ace inhibitors in patients with hf and whether there are differences between the drugs , because headtohead comparisons between arbs and ace inhibitors are still missing . the aim of this study was to evaluate the safety and efficacy of arbs compared with ace inhibitors for hf with reduced ef in patients intolerant of ace inhibitors . the study design was a prospective , randomized , multicenter , double - blind , placebo - controlled trial design . patients were randomly assigned to receive arbs or ace inhibitors at a starting dose and a target dose . the primary outcome measure was the incidence of death or hospitalization for any cause in patients receiving arbs at high or low doses . the secondary outcome measure included the rate of hf-related adverse events , which included hyperkalaemia , hypotension , cough , and impaired renal function . the main adverse events of arb patients were hyperviscosity , hypertension ,

background : indian population representing one - sixth of the world population has been the global melting pot of human diversity . it has all the world s major linguistic groups and the populations have been shaped by different waves of migrations and admixture . further , stringent mating patterns have led to the existence of several endogamous populations , which makes it an important resource for mapping genes . the indian genome variation consortium ( igvc ) project , an initiative of the council for scientific and industrial research ( csir )was set up to develop a database of genomic variations in indian populations for predictive marker discovery in complex diseases such as diabetes , asthma , neuropsychiatric , infectious and cardiovascular disorders , response to drugs , etc . the phase i of the project was conducted to determine the extent of genetic differentiation in india . toward this genotype data of 405 snps from 75 genes and 4.2  mb contiguous chromosome 22 regions were studied in 55 contrasting populations . five genetically distinct clusters were identified and a set of 24 populations that represent these clusters were selected for the phase ii.objectives:to address the need for an online comprehensive resource that enables users to visualize igvc data with integrated information about markers from different resources we have developed igvbrowser.materials

coronary artery disease ( cad ) remains the principal cause of mortality and morbidity around the world . previous studies have demonstrated that an imbalance between t - helper 1- ( th1- ) mediated and th2-mediated immune functions contributes to the development of atherosclerosis . moreover , regulatory t cells ( tregs ) , a special subset of cd4 t cells , inhibit atherosclerotic development by attenuating activated t cell responses . thus , previous studies from our laboratory have found that th17/tregs functional imbalance exists during atherogenesis . herein , we investigate serum galectin- ( gal- ) 9 levels in chinese patients with cad and the severity of coronary artery stenosis . gal-9 was measured by the gini-n scale and the t - cell immunoglobulin mucin- 3 ( tim-3- ) 3 receptor was evaluated by immunohistochemistry . furthermore , ifn- , tgf-1 , and il-4 were measured by elisa and western blotting , respectively . in addition , the severity and progression of coronary arteries were evaluated by gini - n - gi and gi-n-gi scores . the results showed that gal-3 was significantly higher in

backgroundsedation is often required in order to obtain good diagnostic cross - sectional imaging like computed tomography scan ( ct scan ) and magnetic resonance imaging ( mri ) . dexmedetomidine is a highly selective adrenoceptor agonist with a distribution half - life of six minutes and an elimination half-life of two hours . the purpose of this retrospective study is to present our institutional experience with dexmedmetomidine in relation to efficacy and related side effects.material/methodswe conducted a retrospective analysis of all patients who received sedation for mri from july 2007 to december 2012 at our institution . data collection included patient demographics , adverse events , physiologic variables , drug dosages , time required to sedate the patient , time needed to obtain the imaging study , and recovery time.resultsthe mean age of the patients was 4.5 years ( range , 2 - 8 years ) . the mean dose of dexmedomethylstilbene ( 2.5 mg / kg ) was administered intravenously . the peak onset of the sedation was 2.7 1.5 g / kg / h . the median time to achieve a minimum ramsay sedation score ( rss ) of 4 was

melanin is a photoprotective pigment of the skin that acts as a photoreceptor through body coloration and ros scavenging reactive oxygen species . in this paper , we have studied the tyrosine - tyrosinase reaction in terms of melanin formation and melanin scavenging ability . in a battery of studies , we found radical formation through the enzyme reaction , and discussed the formation mechanism in this study . the reaction mixture was prepared by using 5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline - n - oxide ( dmpo ) and deuterium water ( d2o ) as reagents . in addition , 1  mm of 1-methoxy-1,2-diphenyl-2,3-dihydro-2-carboxylic acid ( esr ) was used as an esr spectrophotometer to measure the reaction reaction . the results of the study showed that the reaction resulted in the formation of radical species , including free radical and dopa - melanin , and the ros - scavenging activity of the melanin was found to be enhanced in the presence or absence of esr . in conclusion , the reaction results showed that tyros

phytobezoars are concretions of poorly digested fruit and vegetable fibres that are found in the alimentary track and are mostly composed of indigestible cellulose , tannin , and lignin from ingested vegetables and fruits . they are responsible for 0.44% of cases of mechanical intestinal obstruction , which occurs mainly in the stomach and small intestine . the complications caused by bezoars include mechanical irritation , gastrointestinal obstruction , and , in severe cases , peritonitis caused by bowel perforation . we describe our experience regarding a large - bowel obstruction in a patient with a transplanted kidney . a 39-year - old man was admitted to our hospital for constipation , distension , and severe acute abdominal pain in the lower left quadrant . non - contrast computed tomography ( ct ) scan revealed a focal circumferential thickening of the sigmoid colon with an ovoid and encapsulated intraluminal mass measuring 4.7  3.6 cm . based on the ct findings , particularly mottled gas pattern and encapsulating wall of the mass , we made the diagnosis of phytobeozoar . the patient underwent urgent exploratory laparotomy . the obstructive intral

banna flavivirus ( bav ) is a mosquito pathogen that causes malaria in tropical and subtropical areas . bav strains were isolated in vietnam from culex annulus and cx . the genomic sequences of all 12 genome segments for the 5 strains were determined , and the phylogenetic clustering patterns of these sequences were analyzed . phylogenetic analysis showed that javanese and chinese bavs formed a single cluster for segments 1 , 2 , 6 , 8 , 10 , and 12 . for segments 7 , 9 , and 11 , however , they diverged into 2 clusters , i and ii . the sequences of the 2 vietnamese strains were included in cluster iii , whereas segments 6 and 9 of isolate 02vn178b belonged to cluster iv . moreover , segments 7 and 9 formed 5 independent clusters , and segment 12 formed 3 clusters , while the remaining segments formed 4 clusters . this phylogenetic pattern was consistent with a mixed electrogram pattern of clusters v , ii , and iii .

background : clozapine remains an ultimate option for patients with treatment resistant schizophrenia . however , treatment with this drug is associated with severe metabolic side - effects , such as weight gain . the aim of this study was to determine if patients with schizophrenia on a monotherapy for schizophrenia have higher fasting levels of agouti - related peptide ( agrp ) compared with healthy control.methods:a total of 24 adult hospitalized patients with paranoid adult schizophrenia ( 295.30.30 according to dsm - iv , f20.0 according to icd-10.0 ) and 24 healthy control subjects ( 24 healthy subjects ) were included into the study . the subjects were on clozapsine monotherapy with a minimum dose of 100 mg / day for at least two months prior to the assessments . biochemical and anthropometric measurements were combined with body composition determined using bioelectric impedance analysis ( bia ) which provides accurate measurements of body fat , lean mass and body water.results:fifty-four patients ( 46.9% ) were clozapped and 24 were healthy control . the mean age of the clozappers was 57.5 years and the mean body mass index ( bmi ) was 28.5 kg / m2 .

mutation is the main causal factor for diverse tumorigenesis . the recent development of next - generation sequencing ( ngs ) technologies has revolutionized the speed and throughput of dna sequencing , which facilitates the discovery of new driver mutations . in this study , in order to support the definition of recurrent mutations or phenotype - specific mutations from ngs data of a group of cancers with diverse phenotypes , we aimed to develop a user - friendly tool , named mutation arranger for defining phenotype - related single - nucleotide variants ( snvs ) ( map ) . map software was designed as a standalone program , compatible in microsoft windows environments with a user- friendly graphic interface , and compiled codes of map can be easily installed . when a user loads the input file , called sample descriptor file ( gsf ) , map software displays the sample set information and filter options . once data are loaded , the user can filter the data using the ' analysis options ' tab in map software based on annotation information . summary metrics include the mutation status for each sample , total mutation frequency , p - values for differences between phenotypic groups based on clinical information , genomic position , reference / observed sequence , and additional information , such as read depth by default . in the analysis result ' tab ,

objectiveto examine the association of arterial stiffness with markers of lv systolic and diastolic dysfunction and glucose status in individuals with type 2 diabetes.research design and methodsechocardiographic measurements were performed in 19992001 ( baseline ) and 20072009 ( follow - up ) examinations of the hoorn study , a population - based cohort study on glucose metabolism and cardiovascular diseases.resultsthe mean age of the study population at baseline was 70.3 years ( range , 70.2  3.6 years ) . the mean arterial distensibility coefficients of the carotid , brachial , and femoral arteries were comparable at baseline ( 4.2 , 13.1 , 4.1 and 6.1% for lv ejection fraction , la volume , lv mass , and early ( e ) mitral valve flow ) , early mitral lengthening velocity ( e / e ) , and late ( e2 ) valve length ( e3 ) , respectively . at follow-up , the mean lv stiffness was significantly higher in the impaired glucose metabolism group ( p < 0.05 ) than in the normal glucose metabolism ( p = 0.01 ) or impaired glucose tolerance ( p > 0.001

primitive neuroectodermal tumor ( pnet ) , a highly aggressive tumor of the kidney is a rare malignancy with only few cases being reported in literature . boys and men are more likely to suffer renal pnet , and the sex ratio ( male : female ) is about 3:1 . it usually presents at an advanced stage , with distant metastasis and is associated with a poor prognosis . we report in this paper , a case of a young male who presented with complaints of hematuria and heaviness in the right flank since 15 days . physical examination revealed a soft lump in the hypochondrium . ultrasound examination of the abdomen showed an irregular cystic structure in the kidney at lower pole with few internal echoes . ct scan demonstrated a large significantly enhancing right renal lower polar mass with necrosis with thrombosis in right renal vein . on the basis of these clinico - radiological findings , diagnosis of right side renal cell cancer ( rpnet ) was made . microscopically , the tumor was composed of monotonous sheets of round cells with scant cytoplasm , hyperchromatic vesicular nuclei , coarse chromatin and few conspicuous nucleoli , transverse by thin fibrous bands infiltrating and entra

female sex workers ( fsws ) are at high risk of hiv infection and transmission . however , despite the existence of established targeted sex worker interventions , lots of challenges still exist that have hindered many from enrolling into care . we conducted a cross - sectional qualitative study to identify the facilitators and barriers to linkage to hiv care among hiv positive sex workers in central uganda in order to design appropriate hiv interventions for this key population group . this study was part of a larger study that explored the barriers and facilitators to linkage in kampala and wakiso districts , uk , between may 2012 and december 2013 . a total of 28 fsw clients were selected from 301 clients registered in the hiv counselling and testing ( hct ) registers at the different outreach points in the two districts . in addition , five rom staffs who were involved in the implementation of the outreach program were interviewed as part of the study . hiv testing was conducted using the national rapid testing algorithm and hiv infected women were advised on the available health facilities for follow - up care . at the time of the completion of the hct testing , 14 of the 14 hiv - infected women who tested positive did not enrol for treatment . these included 14

a 57 year old woman presented to our stroke center with mild right sided numbness and weakness ( graded + 4/5 medical research council grading scale ) with the family reporting transient confused conversation and speech difficulty . multisystem magnetic resonance imaging investigations revealed a left lateral posterior thalamic infarct , well circumscribed on t2 weighted and diffusion weighted imaging without other brain parenchymal lesion . in view of the isolated reading impairment , an additional psychometric examination was performed on day 3 after the stroke onset , with the boston diagnostic aphasia examination ( third edition - short form ) . in addition , the test for semantic paralexia prone words were tested with the standard version of the vernier alexia examination . in comparison to normative data , notable deficiencies were noted only in speech fluency , articulation , oral word and sentence reading , picture word matching and writing . within 4 days , the reading difficulty had normalized but she remained with mild speech dysfluency , right hand ideomotor apraxia and developed a dejerine roussey or post stroke thalamusic pain syndrome . the diagnosis of a subcortical alexa has rarely been described . this report depicts a patient with thal

in this article , we review the literature on the history of the use of various types of morphological and morphological descriptors in the development of optical and optical systems and the influence of these descriptors on the design of optical systems . we also discuss the impact of these terms on the development and evolution of the optical and electronic systems . in addition , we discuss the influence these terms have on the understanding of the evolution of optical design and the evolution and development of electronic devices . finally , we describe the impact these terms had on our understanding of optical engineering and their influence on optical design .

peri - prosthetic joint infection ( pji ) is a common complication of total joint arthroplasty . the risk factors potentially correlated with acute pji infection can be divided into pre - operative ( usually related to patient comorbidities ) , peri - operative and post - operative , which are mainly linked to the behaviours of the surgeon and the hospital staff . the aim of this paper is to review the recent literature , summarising the most relevant risk factors that the surgeon can modify in order to reduce the incidence of peri- and post-operative pji .

the use of sodium hypochlorite as toilet detergent is related to a red - brown urine discolouration in patients taking mesalamine . in the period from august 2009 to april 2015 , the netherlands pharmacovigilance centre lareb received two case reports of a red-brown deposit in the toilet bowl in association with the use of a pentasa slow - release 500 mg tablet . both cases were reported by patients and the urine was discoloured after contact with the toilet water . in both cases , the urine initially had a normal colour and developed the red colour after contact . one patient added household bleach to the urine and within a few minutes the discoloursation developed . the other patient added a drop of the same mixture to the toilet and the same discolouring occurred immediately after shaking the mixture . this case concerned a 36-year old woman with colitis , for which she is treated with 2  g of prolonged - release daily - release mesalal ( pentasa sacal ) ( 2 het 2  granules ) twice daily since june 2009 , and her urine appeared red in the bowl , but only when she had recently cleaned the toilet with the detergent . when the patient added

purpose . to determine whether lecithin - bound iodine ( lbi ) will alter the integrity of the tight junctions ( tjs ) of retinal pigment epithelial ( rpe ) cells in culture . materials and methods . human and rabbit arpe-19 cells were cultured in lbi - supplemented medium and pretreated with 10% fetal bovine serum ( fbv ) . the tjs of the rpe cells were examined by immunohistochemistry and immunofluorescence . results . lbi significantly reduced the tj integrity of arpe - 19 cells . the levels of interleukin- ( il- ) 6 , il-8 , and the monocyte chemoattractant protein-1 ( mcp-1 ) were significantly increased in the presence of lbi ( p < 0.001 ) . in contrast , lbi did not significantly alter the tJ integrity of rabbit cells . conclusion . the results of this study suggest that lbi does not alter the structure of the retinal blood retinal barrier ( brb ) and that it is not necessary to increase the concentration of the lbi .

hypoglycemia is a rare cause of acute hypoglycemic spells in children and young adults . we report the case of a 21-year - old woman who presented to an outside facility with altered mental status after being found lethargic by family . on arrival to the emergency department , plasma glucose was 20 mg / dl . the patient was hospitalized and started on dextrose infusion , including 10% dextrospray in water . she was transferred to our facility on suspicion of an insulinoma as she was unable to recall her prior diagnosis . she underwent extensive inpatient hypoglyencemia evaluation with the following results : blood glucose of 47 mg / l after 2 h of fasting , proinsulin levels varying from 10.4 to 84.1 pmol / l , c - peptide level 3.1 ng / ml , negative insulin antibodies , and sulfonylurea screen . magnetic resonance imaging of the abdomen showed no insulinoma , but genetic studies revealed val452 leu activating mutation of the gck gene . the diagnosis of non - insulinoma pancreatogenous hypoglyemic syndrome ( niphs ) was confirmed by a selective pancreatic arterial calcium stimulation test ( spaci ) done at this time to detect hyperfunction

background : obstructive sleep apnea ( osa ) is a common public health problem worldwide and it has been shown to be associated with an increased incidence of cardiovascular complications such as death , stroke , or myocardial infarction . the 2014 american heart association / american stroke association guideline recommends that patients with stroke / transient ischemic disorder ( tia ) showed be worked up for osa . alternatively , the stop - bang questionnaire developed by chung et al . has been widely used as a sensitive screening tool . however , the cut - off values of this questionnaire are based on the data from caucasians , therefore we hypothesized that to use it in asian population , some adjustments are needed.materials and methods : in this study , 42 osa - induced hypertension patients and 82 healthy control subjects were included . demographic data and clinical features of both osa- and healthy subjects were recorded and compared . the clinical features included age , gender , bmi , neck circumference , mallampati classification , torus palatinus , and torus mandibularis . univariate logistic regression analyses were applied to calculate the crude odds ratios of individual variables for having osa.results:the osa patients were significantly older ( 59.

triple - negative breast cancer ( tnbc ) is characterized by absence of expression of the estrogen ( er ) and progesterone receptors ( pr ) and no human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 ( her2)/neu gene amplification . unlike hormone receptor - positive and her2-overexpressing breast cancers , this subtype is unresponsive to endocrine therapy or her2 - targeted agents , thereby limiting available systemic treatment options to conventional cytotoxic chemotherapy . thus , response to chemotherapy has not translated to improvements in progression - free ( pfs ) or overall survival ( os ) in the metastatic setting . nab - paclitaxel is indicated for the treatment of breast cancer after failure of combination chemotherapy for metastatic disease or relapse within 6 months of adjuvant chemotherapy . this work reports on a 52-year - old woman who experienced a long - term complete remission lasting just under 2 years and a pfs from diagnosis of nearly 3 years . she was treated on study with gemcitabine . the patient was initially seen at the university of miami sylvester comprehensive cancer center and enrolled in the phase ii study of nab -

type i interferon ( ifn- ) is a highly potent and widely used antiviral drug that blocks the spread of viruses by blocking the replication of viral rnas and by inhibiting the production of a variety of cell types . stat2 is a critical signaling intermediate that regulates much of the apoptotic and anti - proliferative effects of both type i and type iii interferons . while there are several pathways to activate stat2 , there is only one stat2-dependent pathway that directly recruits stat2 to the ifnar and ifnlr receptors . thus , stat2 acts as the gatekeeper to the antiviral response , which is underscored by the severe susceptibility of stat2 knockout mice to viruses ranging from influenza to dengue . in this review , we summarize the current understanding of the role stat2 plays in the regulation of interferonic and apoptotic responses to both type 1 and type 3 interferones . we will focus on the role that stat2 has in the development and regulation of apoptosis in response to both types of antivirals .

introductionfor patients with operable breast cancer , the major prognostic determinant is whether there has or has not been spread to the axilla and the number of involved axillary nodes . traditionally the surgical management of breast cancer comprised wide local resection of the primary tumour and axillary lymph node dissection ( alnd ) . the combination of the introduction of population - based mammographic screening , modern imaging methods , and increased public awareness resulted in patients being diagnosed more often with smaller - size tumours and less likelihood of lymph node metastases . it is evident now that 6070% of patients with early breast cancer are node negative at the time of diagnosis and alnd puts them at significant risk of short- and long - term morbidities without benefit . sentinel node biopsy ( snb ) is a safe and accurate procedure for detecting tumour cells in sln and predicting the status of the other axillary node ( non - sln ) . although accuracy and appropriateness of snb were disputed by the finding of 510% false - negative cases when snb was followed by axillary dissection at high - risk patients for axillary nodal nodal disease , false - positive results seem to have decreased with the increasing experience of surgeons

background : wellbeing refers to all efforts of employers , trade unions , voluntary organizations , and governmental agencies , which help employees feel better and perform ( work ) better . the main purpose of this study is to assess the wellbeing services of the staff working in the tehran university of medical sciences ( tums ) first to see if they are satisfactory enough or they need to be changed , and second , to help decision - makers develop services that are essential to improve the well - being of employees , as also the quality and effectiveness of the tums 's wellbeing delivery system.materials and methods : the research design was a cross - sectional survey ( 2008 - 2009 ) using a structured non - disguised questionnaire . the sample size was 98 with a 95% level of confidence and absolute error of 0/01.results:according to the first part of the questionnaire , the respondents were supposed to mark the services if they had ever used any of them , and those who had never used them , express their reasons for choosing not to use them by choosing from  almost satisfied , almost dissatisfied , and full dissatisfied , were the main reasons given by the respondents.conclusion:this study shows that there is a gap between the employees real needs and what is delivered to meet their

bilateral inferior shoulder dislocations are extremely rare and only 10 cases have been described so far in the literature . we describe a case of a bilateral luxatio erecta that was successfully treated without long - term complications . a 70-year - old female was accepted in our emergency department because of bilateral shoulder pain that onsets after a sudden fall happened in her house . at physical examination both shoulders were abducted and elevated approximately 90100 from the horizontal plane , with flexed elbows and with hands below the head . the patient was unable to adduct and lower the arms , and there was immediate pain when attempting passive movements . x - ray showed a bilateral inferior dislocation of humeral heads in relation with the glenoid fossa ; no fracture was associated . both arms were immobilized with bilateral slings in total adduction , intrarotation with the supinated arms . close reduction under sedation was achieved without difficulty by pushing the humeral head superiorly with an axial traction - countertraction maneuver on both shoulders . the progressive mobilization of the shoulders started after 2  weeks with passive movements and pendulum codman s exercises . after 3  weeks the patient gave up complaining paresthesias on the left arm and active movements were allowed .

a biloma is a rare abnormal accumulation of intrahepatic bile that is connected to a ruptured biliary duct . we report here a case of a 78-year - old woman who presented to the emergency room with acute fever and chills that developed the day before admission . the patient was diagnosed with diabetes a few years previously , but this was not being treated . on admission , the patient appeared to be in acute distress ; she was lethargic , the sclerae were slightly yellow and the conjunctivae were pale . the abdominal computed tomography ( ct ) revealed a 9.58.5 cm subcapsular cyst with a clear boundary in the left hepatic lobe and enlargement of the bile duct around the cyst . on the second day of admission , percutaneous drainage with an 8.5 fr pigtail catheter , under the guidance of an abdominal ultrasound was performed for diagnosis and treatment . the amount of bile drainage decreased over time , and on the twentieth day of the admission , 64 ml of pancreatico - bile was drained , and magnetic resonance imaging ( mri ) showed a biloma . the biloma that was connected to the intraheptic duct was successfully treated .

objectivethe aim of this study was to evaluate the cost consequences of unintended pregnancies ( ups ) in brazil , an emerging economy with vastly different fertility planning services than most advanced economies and with restrictive abortion laws.research design and methodsa decision tree model was constructed from the perspective of the brazilian public health system . the model was developed to evaluate costs and outcomes for 1 year post - delivery of ups . the possible birth outcomes of up included induced abortion , miscarriage , and ongoing pregnancy resulting in birth . outcomes and resulting costs for infants were also followed.resultsthe total annual number of pregnancies was estimated using the reported number of live births for the year 2010 and adjusted with estimated percentages of induced abortion and miscarriage from the percentages derived from the induced abortions and the estimated percentages from the miscarriage.conclusionprevious studies have reported that public health costs attributed to unintended births in a single year cost taxpayers $ 11.1 billion ( 2006 ) in the united states , suggesting that prevention of up using publicly funded programs in high - income countries has proven to generate significant cost savings for health services and public services . however , this is the first published study that describes the economics of up , and it is believed that this work can aid decision makers regarding access to contraceptives .

pmds is a rare form of male pseudo - hermaphroditism characterized by the presence of mullerian duct structures in an otherwise phenotypically as well as genotypically , normal man . despite the normal male genotype ( 46 xy ) and the subsequent normal development of fetal testes , mllerian structures do not regress either due to absence of mellerian inhibiting substance ( mis ) or lack of response to it . the persistence of a large uterus - like paramesonephric duct in a man is in itself clinically unusual , but when it forms a part of the contents of a hernial sac , it must be considered a rarity . we report the case of a 70 year old man with unilateral cryptorchidism on the right side and left obstructed inguinal hernia containing uterus and fallopian tubes .

spider nevus ( also known as spider angioma or vascular spider ) is a common benign vascular anomaly that may appear as solitary or multiple lesions . we hereby report a case of very large spider nevi in a patient with hepatitis c virus ( hcv ) related liver cirrhosis . a 48-year - old - female presented with a history of abdominal distension and jaundice of 3-month duration . physical examination revealed pallor , icterus , pedal edema , hepatospleenomegaly and tense ascites . she had multiple classical spider nevuses over the chest . also , she had a large arachnoid nevi situated on the forehead , measuring 8 cm in largest dimension with palpable dilated vessels and venous hum . deep compression on the central area of the lesion produced complete disappearance with blanching and when released refilling of vessels occurred . laboratory tests revealed a hemoglobin level of 9.8 g / dl , a white - cell count of 4240/mm , and a platelet count of 77,000/mm . anti - hcv antibody for hcv was positive with hcv rna of 1.6  10 iu / ml . thus , a diagnosis

objective : sympathetic nerve reconstruction surgery using intercostal nerve was performed in 19 patients with severe compensatory hyperhidrosis after endoscopic thoracic sympathetic surgery and evaluated the results of the procedure.materials and methods : from february 2004 to august 2007 , we performed endoscopic sympathetic surgery in 184 patients with primary hyperhidrotic disorders . among theses 19 patients , reconstruction surgery of sympathetic nerve using intercompleasible nerve was done in 18 patients except for one patient with severe pleural adhesion.results:the median follow - up period was 22 months ( range : 1 - 45 months ) . fourteen patients were male and 5 were female ( median age : 28 yr , range : 19 - 61 ) . the median duration of surgery was 47.5 months . the mean age of the patients was 28 yr ( range , 28 - 61 yr ) . median follow-up period was 4.5 ( range 4 - 111 ) months . three patients reported that the effects of reconstruction surgery were definite , two patients reported mild , and one patient reported absent.conclusion:reconstruction surgery of the sympathetic nerve with intercomprehensive nerve reconstruction is a safe and effective treatment for patients with moderate - to - severe compens

background : laparoscopic surgery for the treatment of endometrial cancer ( ec ) has been showed with many advantages compared to laparotomy , such as smaller incision , less pain , and faster recovery . however , data of long - term survival outcomes about laparoscopy for ec remain limited.objectives:the aim of this study was to compare the safety and efficacy of laparoscope and laparotomies in patients with high - risk ec.patients and methods : a total of about 400 patients with histologically confirmed ec who has been received surgical treatment in beijing chao - yang hospital affiliated capital medical university from 2000 to april 2014 were reviewed . we selected subjects according to the following criteria : high-risk ( grade 2 , histologic nonendometrioid , myometrial invasion 1/2 , positive pelvic or para - aortic nodes or stages ii                  ) patients who had undergone comprehensive surgical staging in the form of peritoneal cytology , total hysterectomy , bilateral salpingo - oophorectomy and pelvic lymphadenectomy or sampling , + / cytoreductive surgery by the same experienced surgical team via laparomy ( total abdominal surgery , tah )
